Dialogue
We've heard of you, <Character name>. Go on in, but tell no one what you see inside. Secrets must be kept, after all.
What is this place?
It's a place where we can come and go freely. Our first safehouse was compromised when the
Fallen Angels
took over the barracks. If we stayed, we would have risked exposure.
But you're impersonating the
Vanguard
. They're going to get wise and discover you sooner or later.
Our people in the Vanguard will keep this place a secret, one way or another. We also have a secondary entrance, for our non-human agents. Keeps things discreet.
Impressive.
That makes sense.
You can trust me.

Agent
is a rank within the
Order of Whispers
as well as the
Order of Shadows
. They are often found going undercover among other organizations.

sylvari

during the
Pact
's campaign against
Zhaitan
. Later, she accompanies the Pact fleet to the
Heart of Maguuma
to fight

only to see her allies being scattered in the
Elder Dragon
's surprise attack. She is among the uninjured at the
Order of Whispers Outpost
in
Tangled Depths
, where her comrades continually question whether or not she will be compromised by
Mordremoth
. Arwyna occasionally delivers intel to
Yanyna the Tamer
at the
Ogre Camp
.

and

In Straits of Devastation

Orr

How do you plan on doing that?

gods

Bye.

Commander

Sylvari
in Tangled Depths

Dialogue
If you just came through
Penitent Path
tunnel there, you might want to let your eyes adjust to the light.
What is this place?
That tunnel is the main north to south supply route to the front lines. This camp was established to secure the exit. If
Penitent Camp
up north is the mouth of the tunnel, then this is the...other end.
So this is how you keep the supply route open?
Only when we control both camps. Sometimes the Risen will overrun one or the other, then we can't get any supplies to the front lines. If we don't hold the camps up here, troops die down there.
Good luck.
Right. Good-bye.
Thanks for the advice.

Scouting report
“
There are two groups of sylvari east of here. Carmel leads the group over at the Old Sledge Site, where they're cleaning up pollution from the Inquest's more disgusting experiments. The other group has settled at Judgement Rock, but the Inquest members at Aquabase Terror-Seven aren't happy about it. Please lend the sylvari a hand. With the Inquests's attention focused there, they won't notice our plans to attack the Infinity Coil.
— Agent Blonks

I just wanted to say...thanks. I don't have much to offer in the way of rewards. Just some things I'd rather not see anymore.
What kinds of things?
You're welcome, but by the looks of things I didn't make much of a difference.
Well, you've given me hope. And hey, at least they haven't all killed each other yet.
At least there's that.
Don't mention it. To anyone.
Notes
He is in the downed state at all times, presumably very drunk or very hungover and is being tended to by
Sam Simmons
.

Agent Hamna
Race
Human
Level
80
Location

(
Domain of Vabbi
)
Organization

Image(s)
Interactive map
Agent Hamna
is a member of the
Order of Shadows
. She stands near the
Cragged Vale
Waypoint and greets travelers at the entrance of the
Domain of Vabbi
to brief them on the relationship between the
Awakened
and the Vabbian civilians.
Location
Crystal Desert
Domain of Vabbi
Cragged Vale
Dialogue
The Order of Shadows is following your exploits in the region with great interest. Given our common enemies, we consider you a valuable asset, if not an ally. But we have concerns.
Is that so?
You probably look around and think
Elonians
would be better off freed from
Joko
's tyranny as soon as possible. But you have to understand how delicate the situation is here in Vabbi.
All right, let's hear it.
It's different this far sound of the
wall
. People here have lived for generations under Joko's rule. He's their king. They believe he's practically a god.
So they're brainwashed?
It goes beyond just indoctrination. They've lived alongside the Awakened their entire lives. They view them as their friends and family, which is literally true for many of them.
They're friendly with the Awakened?
Abosolutely. In Vabbian culture, Awakening is viewed as a reward to Joko's faithful. They don't fear it—it's something they aspire to. Being Awakened to serve Joko is considered an honor.
That's difficult to fathom.
Think about it—since childhood, everyone one of these people has been taught to revere Palawa Joko. Being Awakened is part of the cycle of life and death, which Joko controls. He's all-powerful to them!
They sound completely out of touch with reality.
This is their reality. And I understand the impluse to treat them as your enemies. But I want to caution you against this. They're not dangerous or evil—they're innocent and deserving of protection.
Then let's free them from Joko's rule!
It's just not that simple. Saving these people may require a nuanced approach. You might have to allow them some of their delusions—otherwise the entire region could collapse.
Isn't that what you want?
Of course we want Joko's regime to fall, when the time is right. But there has to be something ready to replace it. If not, you risk plunging the entirety of Elona into complete chaos.
We can do better than that.
Look, we both know it's far from ideal. But we can't fight a war on multiple fronts. The
Forged
and
Branded
must be stopped. Joko's way is our best chance, and it's the only way the people here know.
Change has to start somewhere...
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.
Sorry, I don't have time for this.

Dialogue
We thought we had it bad with those steam invaders everywhere, but now the
dredge
are acting up.
What's happening?
The local dredge have decided that the
Durmand Priory
is a threat, and they've been harassing our outposts.
(If
Defense Plans
are in inventory)
I found these dredge documents. Need them?
Anything I can do?
We need to distract the dredge. If we could get them to attack the steam creatures, that'd slow down both their forces.
How would we do that?
If you attack the dredge while disguised as a steam creature, they'll forget about the Priory. Also, keep an eye out for
dredge documents
.
Where will I get this disguise?
I can transform you into a steam creature. So can my
agents
, who are hiding at the other camp entrances.
Okay, transform me now.
All right, thanks for the information.
Dredge documents?
Any plans, blueprints, or information that might help us fight the dredge. My agents and I are collecting them.
You mentioned something else.

You can change me back, now.
(Only if the player is currently transformed into a steam creature)
Sorry to hear it.
Good work! As we speak, the dredge are preparing to attack the steam creatures. Here, take something for your trouble.
Show me what you have.
That was some fun rabble-rousing. Take care.
Notes
Anomaly
:
Kaukiri has an Order of Whispers title, wears their armor and sells Whispers-themed items but in dialogue and her reward letter, she describes being part of the Durmand Priory.

Dialogue
If not in Order of Whispers
I'm impressed that you recognized me, <Character name>. Or do you not know who I am?
How do you know my name?
My allies and I make it our business to know what's going on in the
world
. And even if that weren't the case, everything you've done to help people in need has made your name well-known.
And who are you?

. We work from the shadows to make sure there is a tomorrow, despite the efforts of the

What's going on around here?

Oh yes, thanks to Zee, agents like me are always armed with the very latest espionage and survival gadgets.
I bet that makes your job really easy.
Haha, I guess. For every one of Zee's gadjets that saved my life, TWO have either gotten me caught or just blown up.
Why do you keep using them?
You know, I'm starting to wonder about that myself. Gadgets can't be the answer for everything, right?
Right! You should tell Zee that you don't need fancy gadgets.
Zee is a Creator. He makes gadgets and masterminds the missions. I just follow his orders.
What's the latest mission?
We're subverting the undersea Inquest lab.
How'd you get all of their equipement up here?
I stole it. All from that undersea Inquest laboratory. Zee, here, is going reverse engineer them and get me some new gadgets.
That's very clever.
Subvert? Why not just destroy it?
That's not how the Order of Whispers does things, usually.
Bah. I'll show you how its done.
Ah, excellent.
Sounds like I should be talking to him, then.
Of course not.
Thanks, I'll be sure to stay away from from
[
sic
] them.
That doesn't sound like field agent work. Why is he out here?
(Same as "We're subverting the undersea..." above)
That's good.
And what would you be doing on this mission?
(Same as "We're subverting the undersea..." above)
Sounds like you might be here for awhile.
Does the Order always leave so much junk lying around?
(Same as below)
Your secret is with me.
What's all this junk lying around here?
Hey, it wasn't junk when I brought it up here. It was Zee that tinkered into heaps of scrap.
You brought this stuff here? Where did it come from?
Zee? Is he an inventor?
(Same as "How'd you get all of their..." above)
Glad I wasn't around when that happened.
(Same as "Zee? Is he some sort of..." above)
Carry on.

Agent Zott
is an
asuran
optics specialist in the
Order of Whispers
. He participated in the retaking of
Claw Island
where the
Pact Commander
helped him redirect the energy of a lighthouse. Zott and his new partner
Elli
later helped assault
Orr
from a naval point. The two developed feelings towards each other, shown by Zott's attempts to comfort Elli when she recognized
Risen Captain Yejj
as an asura she had once known. Zott helped the
Pact
take the Orrian shore, only for the Risen
Marksman Rukbat Rami
to fatally shoot him in the back with an arrow. Zott's death motivated Elli to keep on fighting for the Pact to bring down the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
once and for all.

Conscript the Dead Ships
, Zott mentions that he had then lost someone or something making him less lighthearted.

PAGE: Aid Worker Sya

======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 1
.
Aid Worker Sya
Race
Human
Profession
Mesmer
Location
Fort Marriner
(
Lion's Arch
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Image(s)
Interactive map
“
Seeing so many die in those weeks...it made me realize how important it is to embrace life. To celebrate who we are.
—
Aid Worker Sya
Aid Worker Sya
is a
trans
Order of Whispers
agent who helped around the refugee camp on the
Stormbluff Isle
during
Scarlet Briar
's
attack
on
Lion's Arch
. Inspired to embrace her true self in the face of losing everything, she shed her former name, and used her
mesmer
magic to aid in her transition. After the Lion's Arch reconstruction efforts finished, she returned to the city.
Biography
Prior to transition, Sya joined the
Order of Whispers
and assisted them in gathering intel. In 1326
AE
she was teamed up with fellow Whispers agent,
Lightbringer
Brokka
, and the pair disguised themselves as aid workers from
Sparkfly Fen
at the refugee camp on
Stormbluff Isle
. Their objective was to listen in on any useful gossip, tend to refugees, and collect sputum samples of victims infected with the
miasma
which
Scarlet Briar
had released on
Lion's Arch
when her
alliance
had
assaulted
the port city. Sya's eagerness to be formal in front of the Lightbringer nearly blew their cover, and Brokka reprimanded her for it.
After the wind had changed and the toxic miasma had largely dissipated, the
Lionguard
, the three
orders
, the
Pact Commander
and various adventurers
fought their way back into Lion's Arch
, crippled and drove away Scarlet's Alliance,
killed Scarlet herself
and destroyed her flagship, the
Breachmaker
. Lion's Arch was left in ruins, however, leaving Sya and Brokka to continue aiding the victims in the
aftermath
and the lengthy rebuilding of the city.
During the relief efforts Sya and Brokka had many conversations, one of which detailed Brokka's plans to marry
Tokk
and adopt the orphaned
charr
Leyah
and
human
Jordyn
. When Sya expressed disbelief at the
asuran
couple tending to children who were not asura, Brokka explained that the orphans would benefit from the couple's love and support in order to cope with the tragedies they had witnessed. Brokka's insightful and inspiring words left a lasting impression on Sya.
Having witnessed the horrors of war first-hand and remembering Brokka's wisdom, Sya decided to embrace life in full, and publicly transitioned to her true self. She changed her name to Sya and changed her appearance to be more feminine using
mesmer
magic. Sya stayed in the rebuilt Lion's Arch and kept in touch with Brokka's family, even befriending other citizens like
Lionguard Hela
who encouraged Sya to continue on her new path.
Locations
Kryta
Lion's Arch
Fort Marriner
Historical location
Kryta
Bloodtide Coast
Stormbluff Isle
Stormbluff Isle Refugee Camp
(from
Escape from Lion's Arch
until
Lion's Arch was rebuilt
; as Aid Worker Symon)
Dialogue
For previous dialogue:
Aid Worker Sya/dialogue
Beautiful, isn't it? After
Scarlet
attacked
the city, this statue was rubble. Now look at it. More glorious than ever. Just like Lion's Arch.
Have we met before?
I was one of the aid workers in
Bloodtide Coast
after Scarlet's attack. I'm Sya. Back then I was known as Symon.
Thanks for your service. What prompted the change?
Scarlet destroyed so much in the blink of an eye. It reminded me how short life is and how we should spend every moment embracing who we are.
That's brave of you.
Thank you. Being a
mesmer
makes it easier. Take care.
Be well.
A remarkable tribute to this city.
Notes
During the
Battle for Lion's Arch
, she had the
appearance
of a generic
Order of Whispers
agent.
Trivia
Sya was added by
Jessica Teddy Croft
.

says that whoever breaks Jormag's tooth will lead us in defeating Jormag. So, we wait like lazy beasts until that day. Disgusting.

Aurielo the Survivor

(

Aurielo the Survivor
is an

agent in
Gendarran Fields
, sitting by the bridge East of Applenook Hamlet Waypoint. He informs those players who have joined the
Order of Whispers
of the
Provernic Crypt
puzzle in the north of the zone.
Location
Kryta
Gendarran Fields
Cornucopian Fields
Applenook Hamlet
Dialogue
This is so infuriating. These people work hard enough without a bunch of thieving, yellow-bellied, underhanded, foul-smelling, worthless, rotten pirates wrecking everything.
Who are you?
Aurielo the Survivor, if that tells you anything. I was a drifter after I lost my second warband. When I came here and saw pirates setting fire to the farms I decided to stay and help the farmers.
(If in
Order of Whispers
)
You're here to control what is uncontrollable?
And kill the unkillable. A friend in the Order, I see. If you're looking for adventure, there's an old crypt up north a ways, the tomb of an ancient war leader of Ascalon."
What can I find there?
A fight. It's got an ooze problem, and some of the spiders lurking in there are particularly nasty. Be quick on your feet; dodging the spike traps is the best part, but getting caught in one isn't much fun.
Thanks.
I see. Nice to meet you.
I agree.
Notes
Lore discrepancy
:
Aurielo mentions a
"tomb of an ancient war leader of Ascalon"
that is
"up north a ways"
from
Cornucopian Fields
.
However, the only tomb in that direction is of
Mazdak the Accursed
, who hails from
Orr
and (purportedly) founded
Kryta
. (ref.
Dead of Winter
)

======================================================================
PAGE: Awakened Koss Dejarin
CATEGORIES: Order of the Sunspears, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Awakened_Koss_Dejarin
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
and
The Icebrood Saga
.
Awakened Koss Dejarin
Race
Awakened
Level
80
Rank
Elite
Locations
Sun's Refuge
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
Hall of Monuments
(
Eye of the North
)
Organization
Order of the Sunspears
“
Koss was no patsy in life, and he will not be in death.
—
Awakened Koss Dejarin
Awakened Koss Dejarin
is an
Awakened
Sunspear
"guarding" a cave known as the
Grotto of the Defeated
. In life, he was a noble from
Kourna
, son of
Zudash Dejarin
and husband of
Melonni
. Boastful and narcissistic, Koss makes a show of his fights to prove his abilities.
Biography
Early years
In his youth as a noble in
Kourna
, Koss Dejarin frequently got into trouble with his friends and often dragged his sister,
Ailonseh Dejarin
, into his problems. During this time, he had garnered many
corsair
contacts to his father
Zudash Dejarin
's shame. Koss eventually left his
home
to join the
Order of the Sunspears
, though this act resulted in his father
disowning him
under the belief that Koss had joined simply to find more brawls.
During his time as a Sunspear, Koss openly garnered more criminal contracts which prevented his own promotions to becoming an officer. He took tutelage underneath
Spearmarshal
Kormir
alongside the
hero of Nightfall
. When
Nightfall
was beginning in 1075
AE
, Koss aided in rallying the Sunspears to
lay siege
to
Gandara, the Moon Fortress
, but was captured when
Warmarshal
Varesh Ossa
unleashed
demons
onto the Sunspears.
After
being freed
and
securing passage
for the injured back to
Istan
with the corsair
Margrid the Sly
, Koss continued on with the other Sunspears to stop Varesh, ultimately being part of the
lich
Palawa Joko
's
release
and
Abaddon
's defeat. During this time Koss mentored
Tahlkora
who developed feelings for him. Tahlkora ultimately
stood aside
, however, to let Koss and their adventuring partner
Melonni
pursue a relationship.
After Abaddon's
demise
and Kormir's
ascension
, Koss married Melonni and wrote an autobiography named
Koss on Koss
as well as an
extended edition
of it. He was one of the few Sunspears who knew the threat that Palawa Joko would eventually become, and rallied a group, which included
Lonai
, to end the threat the lich posed early. However, the assault on Joko failed, and Koss was reanimated as an
Awakened
by Joko, forced to serve his enemy. Though Koss believed he had died a glorious and honorable death, he had also died not knowing the fate of his wife and children. The Dejarin Estate, Koss's ancestral home, was eventually
sacked and destroyed
as Joko asserted his dominance over Kourna and the rest of
Elona
.
Eventually, Koss was sent to Istan and the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
. He tricked the Awakened there into persuading him to "guard" a sealed cave. In the decades that passed since, he had been challenged by many corsairs seeking to curry favor from the infamous Sly family of corsairs by taking back a coin that Koss had won off of Margrid the Sly. The villagers of
Champion's Dawn
learned of Koss's presence, though not his identity, and took to enjoying the bouts between him and the corsair challengers.
Living World Season 4
While searching for the lost coin to persuade the Sly family to release the captive
Spearmarshal Zaeim
, the
Pact Commander
ventured into Koss's lair in 1330
AE
and discovered the Awakened champion's identity. After learning more about Koss, the Commander asked him to hand over the coin so it could be used to liberate Zaeim from
the Sly Crew
, but the ever boastful champion
challenged the Commander to a duel
for the right to earn the coin. After a brief clash, the Commander emerged victorious much to Koss's disbelief; however, the champion kept his end of the bargain and gave up the coin.
Despite learning of the Sunspears' struggles against Joko and the lich king's absence, Koss was reluctant to join the fight on the side of the Sunspears out of worry that one of Joko's lieutenants might force him to fight against the organization he had once been a member of. Instead, Koss asked the Commander to hurt Joko enough so that no one else would have to suffer the fate of Awakening and being tied to Joko's will ever again.
If
Redeemer Kossan
had been encountered in
the Desolation
, the Commander had an option to let Koss know that one of the champion's descendants was still fighting for Elona. Koss was happy to hear that his line had survived and that his descendants could live their lives as they chose.
Following the
defeat
of Palawa Joko in the
Battle of Gandara
in 1331
AE
, Koss felt his compulsion to obey Joko suddenly dissolve. Realizing that the Commander had somehow succeeded in killing the seemingly immortal lich king, Koss wandered out of the grotto to see the outside world for the first time in decades.
Koss eventually learned of a
summit
being held at the
Fortress of Jahai
where different Elonian factions—including the
Free Awakened
, the
Joko Loyalists
, the
Mordant Crescent
, the
Order of Shadows
, and the Sunspears—would decide on a post-Joko ruling council for Elona. He arrived at the fortress just in time to witness an attempted coup by
Archon Iberu
and his Mordant Crescent, and killed the
archon
to end the threat in a single strike. Tired of the factions' arguing, Koss intimidated most of the delegates to agree on Spearmarshal Zaeim's treaty, only to face opposition from former
Grand Vizier Utumishi
who initially refused to cooperate with the people who had been responsible for Joko's death.
The summit was interrupted again, however, when the
Death-Branded Shatterer
assaulted the fortress with its
Branded
minions on behalf of the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. It forced the Commander and the delegates to flee into the courtyard before it gave chase to the Commander's
dragon
ally
Aurene
. Kossan finally got to talk with Koss in the aftermath, revealing that they were related and asking to speak with the champion in private.
After the Sunspears decided to move survivors of the attacks and local refugees to a safer location to withstand future Branded incursions, Koss
told
his allies of the old Sunspear base he had once been using in the campaign against Varesh and how it could be used to shelter people in need again. Upon finding the entrance, he
accompanied
Kossan, the Commander and
Braham Eirsson
to the abandoned Sunspear base in
Jahai Bluffs
. The group divided into two pairs and began a friendly competition to clear the cave of the spiders and the
Colossal Broodmother
, which had been dwelling there, and lighting protective braziers before Koss named the reclaimed base as
Sun's Refuge
.
Shortly after clearing the cave, the group met with the Commander's other allies who arrived there, only to witness another Branded invasion from
rifts
which suddenly appeared. Koss stayed with Aurene to protect her along with other allies while the Commander took a small group to fight the Branded head-on. After the invasion had been thwarted, Koss witnessed the Commander return to Aurene with the
ghosts
of
Eir Stegalkin
and
Snaff
who had come with news of great urgency: Kralkatorrik was devouring
magic
in
the Mists
, and Aurene needed to confront the Elder Dragon before it would become too powerful to face.
Aurene received visions of multiple futures, however, in which she died no matter which allies she brought with her for the confrontation, and fled while leaving the Commander and the allies worried about the content of her visions. To stop panic from spreading, the Commander's ally
Blish
pointed out that he and the Commander already had a plan to attach a tracker to Kralkatorrik via a magical lure so they could monitor the Elder Dragon's movements and evacuate settlements before the Branded could invade populated areas.
Kossan spoke with Koss whenever he could in Sun's Refuge, only to see Koss leaving temporarily upon the latter learning that Tahlkora, an old friend from the past, had been Awakened. When Koss returned to Sun's Refuge, Kossan found himself unable to ask the champion all the questions he had been thinking of. Koss sensed Kossan's uncertainty and answered the most important question his descendant had had in mind: he was proud of Kossan but also reminded the agent that his combat stances and shouts still needed some polishing. However, the champion refused to tell anyone what his reunion with Tahlkora had been like and what they had discussed on
Yahnur Plateau
.
Upon his return, Koss was grateful to the Commander who had helped grow the refuge into a beacon of hope in the time of crisis. As a token of his gratitude, he gifted the Commander with the pommel of Joko's
staff
, which he had found during his journey back to the refuge, and hoped that the piece of the staff would be of
some use
to the Commander. During his stay in Sun's Refuge, Koss gained many fans such as
Nadim
. He instructed Sunspears to look around the refuge to find a way back into Jahai north of
Argon Garrison
so that the allied forces could use the passage to discreetly slip reinforcements and supplies in and out of the sanctuary.
The Icebrood Saga
Koss would eventually learn about the
defeat
of Kralkatorrik, and he traveled to the
Eye of the North
in 1333
AE
. While there, he conversed with the Pact Commander and told them about a
book
written by
Snargle Goldclaw
that depicted Koss's romantic pursuits. Koss was amused by the tale; however, he noted that it was only fictional in parts. He offered the Commander a copy of the book as he was carrying around several books already.
Location
Crystal Desert
Jahai Bluffs
Sun's Refuge
(as Koss Dejarin, leaves after ambient dialogue plays, returns after
Full House
)
Koss is west of the central brazier. You might notice the book Koss on Koss: Volume Two near him
Far Shiverpeaks

Forging Steel
)

the
player
if he downs them. He uses the

.

voiced
by
Keston John
.
Koss' combat shout, "KOSS!" is a reference to one of his
idle quotes
in
Guild Wars Nightfall
:
"I think my name makes a fine battle cry. "Koss!" What do you think?"

Battery Commander Shortshot
Race
Charr
Location
Haunted Nolani
(
Black Citadel
)
Organization
Iron Legion
Order of Whispers
Image(s)
Interactive map
Battery Commander Shortshot
is an agent of the
Order of Whispers
who prevents players from accessing the
Ghostblast Battery
.
Location
Black Citadel
Haunted Nolani
Dialogue
Where do you think you're going? This descender is for authorized personnel only.
(If in the Order of Whispers)
Perhaps today you could allow the disallowed.
Then you would know the unknown. Greetings, Whisperer. What do you seek?
What is going on at the bottom of this descender?
They're test-firing the Ghost Battery below. It used to be our main defense against the ghosts from the old Nolani Academy, but they're not so important now that the ghosts are less of a threat.
Then why is the test-firing of an obsolete weapon so off-limits?
There's talk in the higher circles of a possible upgrade to the weapons that may make them much more effective. Maybe even enough to defeat the ghosts for good. But there are few details beyond that.
It's good to know the Order has you here to monitor things.
Where does this descender go?
Down to the Ghostblast Battery platform. But it's off-limits while they're test-firing the turrets.
What's the Ghostblast Battery?
An array of our long-barreled ghost toasters. Used to be our main defense against the ghosts from the old Nolani Academy.
"Used to be"? Have the ghosts stopped coming?
Bah. Ghosts aren't so much a threat these days as they are a wispy pain in the haunches. Of course, they're still fun to send back to the Mists, or whatever it is we blast them to.
Thanks for the explanation. Good-bye.
When will they be finished with the test-firing?
Hear those loud boom-boom sounds? When they stop, it's over. Until then, keep your distance from this descender.
I'll check back when things get quiet. Bye.
I may come back when the tests are over. Good-bye.
I'll check back some other time.
Who can I see about getting authorization?
At the moment, everyone who's got authorization to give authorization is probably monitoring the test-firing of the Ghost Battery.
Then can you give me directions to this Ghost Battery?
Sure, it's located right at the bottom end of this descender. Which, as I mentioned, is for authorized personnel only.
So what is this Ghost Battery?
(Same as "What's the Ghostblast Battery?" above)
Maybe I'll come back later. Good-bye.
I think I'll just be moving along, then.
Got it.
Notes
Despite what he implies in his dialogue, it is possible to use the teleporter to go down to the platform, without incurring any repercussions or extra dialogue. Additionally, there is no one else on the platform itself like he also mentions.

======================================================================
PAGE: Benn Tenstrikes
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Benn_Tenstrikes
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
and
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Benn Tenstrikes
Race
Human
Level
80
Locations
Arborstone
(
Cantha
)
Fort Trinity
(
Straits of Devastation
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Pact
Image(s)
Wounded Tenstrikes in
Early Parole
.
Benn Tenstrikes
, later
Benn Longshot
, is a
human
mercenary
and member of the
Order of Whispers
. His parents are Captains
Fel Vaerdenmer
and
Merri Sunbower
, and has a sister in
Divinity's Reach
who is a priestess of
Balthazar
. His first chosen surname, "Tenstrikes," was not his legal surname, but a self-given name out of his pride of being able to fell an enemy in ten strikes or less.
Biography
Personal story
Benn was born as the son of Captains
Fel Vaerdenmer
and
Merri Sunbower
, and also had a sister who became a priestess of
Balthazar
. He eventually joined the
Order of Whispers
and led a band of mercenaries. He adopted the name Benn Tenstrikes out of his pride of being able to fell an enemy in ten strikes or less.
When
Demmi Beetlestone
ran away from her father,
Minister Caudecus
, Benn was assigned the task of escorting her as her bodyguard to the
Chantry of Secrets
in 1325
AE
. However, he was ambushed by the
Ministry Guard
, which gave the
Jackdaw Pirates
the chance to kidnap Demmi. After
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
and his
partner
had successfully brought Demmi to the Chantry, the Ministry Guard attacked and captured Benn again, and Caudecus's mercenaries held him captive in
Beetletun
to interrogate him for Demmi's location. He was eventually freed by the Order of Whispers, possibly involving the
Pact Commander
and
Trahearne
if the Commander chose to get involved in the operation. Benn and his mercenary forces were then potentially recruited by the Commander to assist in the
battle
to retake
Claw Island
from
Blightghast the Plaguebringer
.
Benn was not seen again until after the
battle
with the
Elder Dragon
Zhaitan
. He attended the
Pact
's victory celebration at
Fort Trinity
where he could be found attempting to woo Crusader
Eilye Jeyne
.
Interim years
Benn was aboard the Pact fleet during the assault on the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
, but went MIA after Mordremoth and his forces
brought the fleet down
over
Verdant Brink
in 1328
AE
. After surviving the Pact fleet's crash he was captured by
Coztic
hylek
and was later rescued by the
Itzel
who he ended up staying with for a time to learn their ways.
End of Dragons
Benn appeared in
Arborstone
in
Cantha
after the allied forces' victory over
the Dragonvoid
in 1335
AE
, propping up the bar in
Club Canach
under the new name Benn Longshot. He reunited with the Pact Commander and explained what had happened to him since the campaign against Mordremoth, lamenting that he had not gotten to Cantha in time to help the allied forces in battle.
Locations
Ruins of Orr
Straits of Devastation
Fort Trinity
Caer Aval
(only after completing the
personal story
)
Cantha
Arborstone
Club Canach
(as Benn Longshot)
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 4: Order Neophyte
Down the Hatch
Chapter 6: The Battle for Claw Island

Retribution

Victory or Death

Blood Legion Soldier Marloth
Race
Charr
Level
36
Location
Warrior's Crown
(

)
Organization

:

I'm curious why you're out here.

world

What's so interesting about it?
Behaviors. Ways of acting. There are many, and I see them all. They go about their lives, oblivious to others around them. But I see hidden things too.
What hidden things?
Looks between young lovers. Tensions between the races. Truths no one wants anyone to see. Let this be a lesson: keep your eyes open and the world will be your best teacher.

Trivia
Search YouTube
for videos related to
Captain Penzan
.
Captain Penzan is
voiced
by
Yuri Lowenthal
.
Captain Penzan is a reference to Gilbert and Sullivan's
The Pirates of Penzance
. At certain times, he will sing a parody of the
Major-General's Song
:
I am the very model of a modern pirate general,
I've information magical, mathematical and mineral.
I know the Queens of Kryta and can cite matters historical,
From Bloodstones to the dragon spawn in order categorical.
In fact, I write my washing bills in old Canthan cuneiform.
I'll tell you every detail sewn on Caudecus's uniform.
The skills and builds of warfare are essential to my strategy,
You'll say a better pirate general has never sat-a-gee,
For when you know the habits of the Charr and the Elonians
When you can tell at sight a Kurzick from an Ascalonian,
You'll know my martial knowledge, quite extensive and still myriad,
Myriad...myriad...myriad...ah!
Has only been updated for the Interregnum period!
But still in matters magical, mathematical, and mineral,
I am the very model of a modern pirate general!

.
Destiny's Edge is going into the City of the Gods?
They're kinda together again. And, I should say, "City of the HUMAN Gods." Story goes that when the gods first gave magic to humans, they settled here. The gods left, and Zhaitan took over.
Have you heard how they plan to take on the Elder Dragon?
I'm not sure. They're pressing with every available airship, and I hear that the Pact has been working on a special weapon that packs a wallop.
What can I do to help?
Head into the ruined City of the Gods, but I don't recommend going in alone. If you're smart, you'll take four others with you to watch your back and flanks. Once you're in, you'll find the others there.
(*)
Okay.
Good to know.
Got it.
(If not a full party)
[Find more people using LFG.]
I'll be back later.
After finishing Victory or Death
Zhaitan has been defeated, but its minions continue to fight inside Arah.
Ogden
went there to discover the secrets of the elder races.
Who's Ogden and what elder races?
Ogden Stonehealer. He's one of the last dwarves, works with the Durmand Priory, and is on a mission to learn more about the Elder Dragons.
And what was that about elder races?
Yeah, Ogden has a list of races that were around during and survived the last incursion of the Dragons. They were
dwarves
,
Seers
,
Forgotten
,
jotun
, and
mursaat
.
What can I do?
Ogden believes the human gods gathered more information here. Head into Arah, but don't go alone. Take four others to guard your back and flanks. You'll find Ogden in there.
Okay.
Good to know.
Interesting. I have to go.
(If not a full party)
[Find more people using LFG.]
I have to go.
Notes
It should be noted, that this information is about the "story mode", which can't get accessed directly from here anymore. You can't enter the dungeon to get to the story mode. That content is only available via
Victory or Death
.
His appearance is randomly generated, but will always appear as a human male Order of Whispers member.

You'll be visiting old
Grenth
soon.

Yarr!
Don't get in my way.
Notes
Named after Clint Oliver who, along with many other community members, have NPCs in Cursed Shore named after them for their involvement in growing the Guild Wars 2 community while the game was still in development. He was involved with hosting several community events in
Guild Wars
including
MantleCon
,
The Royal Ascalonian Family Masquerade Ball
and
Pink Day in LA
. Clint was also an editor/writer for GuildMag, as well as one of the 3 hosts of the original GuildMag podcast.

Dialogue
Hello. I can only talk for a moment. I'm the Order of Shadows weaponsmith, and there's a lot of pressure on me right now to develop new weapons against the Forged.
Any new weapons you need help testing out?
(Only before completing the Mastery Insight.)
You're volunteering? Great. Take this Sulfurthrower out, and see how it works on a variety of different Forged chassis. Come back for another if you lose it.
What is this thing?
(Same as **(2) below)
What do I do with it, exactly?
(Same as **(3) below)
Can I get a new one if I lose it?
Sure. You can come back here. I've also stashed some extras in strategic locations around the
Mouth of Torment
.
Tell me about what this thing is again.
(Same as **(2) below)
Thanks, I'll get right on it.
Thanks!
What sort of weapons?
(Before and after completing the Mastery Insight.)
The Forged around here go to great lengths to avoid sulfur. It's highly corrosive to their armor. If I can get my
Sulfurthrower
prototype finalized, we'll have a huge advantage.
**(1)
And what's a Sulfurthrower, exactly?
What do you think a Sulfurthrower is? It expels vaporized sulfur at high pressures. The Forged are going to hate it.
**(2)
So how does it work?
It's not hard. Point it at a Forged target, and pull the trigger. Be sure to kill your target, so we can see if it makes enough impact to warrant mass production.
**(3)
Why sulfur?
(Same as **(1) above)
I'll give it a shot.
I bet they will.
We could use an advantage.
You seem busy. I'll come back later.

This base is a hub of information for the Order. Though our primary focus is local Priory infiltration, I receive news on agents stationed all over Tyria.
So this is gathering spot for spies in the Durmand Priory?
"Gathering spot" makes it sounds like a homestead. The homestead is a front. There's no socializing down here. Priory spies report their findings to their superiors here so proper action may be taken.
What are they finding?
Whatever the Priory has found. They're proficient at digging up practical information, but they're scholars and explorers. We can't trust them to accomplish anything with the data they collect.

Notes
Bug
:
While the generator event is active, the user is able to access Zee's vendor inventory while the heart is not completed.
Trivia
There are multiple references associated with this NPC:
Creator Zee is a reference to the James Bond character,
Q
The line "your mission, should you choose to accept it" is a reference to
Mission: Impossible
.
Dilithium
is a fictional chemical element featured in
Star Trek
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Demmi Beetlestone
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Demmi_Beetlestone
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2
,
Living World Season 3
, and
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Demmi Beetlestone
Race
Human
Location
Chantry of Secrets
(
Kryta
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Pact
Image(s)
Demmi's Order of Whispers Outfit
“
I'm tired of being someone else's pawn. I can't live like that. It's time to stop running.
—
Demmi Beetlestone
Demmi Beetlestone
is the daughter of
Legate Minister Lord Caudecus Beetlestone
, a highly influential member in
the Ministry
. Her mother was
Lady Wi
's cousin. Having discovered her father's troubling ties to unsavory elements within
Kryta
, Demmi decides to forge her own path no matter the cost.
Biography
Early years
Demmi Beetlestone was born to the influential Beetlestone family in
Kryta
and lived in
Caudecus's Manor
. Her parents were
Lady Wi
's cousin and
Minister Caudecus
, and the necklace from her mother became one of Demmi's most treasured possessions. When Demmi's mother left the manor and was ostensibly killed by
bandits
before
Ministry Commander Landon
could bring her back, Lady Wi stepped in as godmother and helped raise Demmi the best she could.
Although she lived a sheltered life, Demmi became well versed in the kingdom's politics by observing her father and his many guests over the years. She was close to the Wi family and befriended their daughter,
Lady Valette Wi
. Caudecus himself was often busy with matters in
the Ministry
and elsewhere, dedicating more time to politics than his family.
Personal story
In 1325
AE
, Demmi was getting upset that her father was hiding things from her and made him promise that there would be no more secrets between them. However, she discovered that Caudecus had broken his promise when she found armaments and unfamiliar brutes in the manor's cellar as well as accusatory letters from her late mother. She began doubting the story Caudecus had told her of her mother's death and wondered if Caudecus had actually had a hand in the mother's untimely demise. Realizing she could no longer stay home, she wrote a letter addressed to her father before fleeing the estate and seeking out those who could protect her from him.
Demmi contacted the
Order of Whispers
in
Divinity's Reach
and offered them detailed information about Caudecus's shady deals in exchange for the Order's protection. The Order assigned
Benn Tenstrikes
as her escort, and Benn briefly taught her how to handle a rifle, only for her to nearly shoot his ear off. However, Caudecus learned of the plan. Unbeknownst to Demmi, he could find her location via a tracking enchantment he had placed in her mother's necklace which she was wearing. Benn was ambushed by the Ministry Guard, and Landon locked up Demmi. However, Demmi eventually managed to gather enough courage to escape and eluded her pursuers, venturing deeper into the countryside. Before she could be tracked down, the Order of Whispers lost contact with her somewhere between Divinity's Reach and
Applenook Hamlet
.
Realizing how valuable the intel Demmi held could be, the
Master of Whispers
sent
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
and an
Initiate
on a mission to locate Lady Beetlestone and escort her to a safehouse in
Lion's Arch
.
While looking for clues, the two agents discovered that Demmi had been captured by the
Jackdaw Pirates
and held for ransom to the
Ministry Guard
who had been sent to bring her back by force if necessary. Tybalt and the Initiate disguised themselves as pirates and rescued Demmi who was not impressed by their improvised plan and appearance. Although Demmi showed surprise at a charr like Tybalt helping a human like her, a talk with the Initiate made her trust the pair. She refused to divulge what she knew about her father until she would be safe, however, as she believed the Order of Whispers would not abandon her as long as she held information they wanted.
After reaching
Lion's Arch
, they met with Lady Wi, who turned out to be an undercover Whispers agent, and discovered the tracking enchantment on Demmi's necklace. Realizing that Demmi would not be safe in Lion's Arch for long, Lady Wi arranged for Demmi to be escorted to the
Chantry of Secrets
beyond Caudecus's reach. Demmi managed to barely elude Landon while fleeing from Lion's Arch as Tybalt and the Initiate kept the pursuers occupied.
Upon reaching
Stormbluff Isle
, however, Demmi had had enough of Landon pursuing her. She decided to make a stand with Tybalt and the Initiate and face Landon head on to send a clear message to her father that he could not manipulate her anymore. While setting up the trap, Tybalt gifted Demmi with his rifle. As expected, Landon showed up soon after and tried to goad Demmi to return with him, only to witness Lady Beetlestone refusing him. Although Landon's men were close to overwhelming the trio, more Order of Whispers agents showed up and turned the tide, and Landon met his demise at Demmi's hands. Finally free, Demmi decided to join the Order of Whispers and become a real operative so she could aid not only Kryta but the whole world against threats such as Caudecus and the
Elder Dragons
.
After being initiated into the Order of Whispers, Demmi studied under
Preceptor
Valenze
and found her calling as an analyst, putting her political knowledge to good use although she also thought of becoming a spy in Lion's Arch like her fellow operative
Riel Darkwater
was. She later learned of Tybalt's demise in
the Battle of Claw Island
against
Zhaitan
's
Risen
minions and mourned her fellow operative and friend's loss. When the Order of Whispers began looking for allies to retake
Claw Island
, Demmi suggested that they liberate Benn Tenstrikes, whom the Ministry Guard had taken captive for refusing to divulge Demmi's current location, and recruit his mercenaries for the assault on Zhaitan's forces.
However, the Risen unexpectedly diverted forces from assaulting Lion's Arch and targeted the headquarters of the three
Orders
of
Tyria
instead. When the dragon minions
invaded
the Chantry of Secrets, Demmi assisted
Trahearne
and the Initiate, who had been promoted to Lightbringer, in fighting through the defenses and Risen, and applied the knowledge Tybalt had taught her to override the defenses to let them reach the trapped people inside. They rescued the Preceptors and the Master of Whispers whose identity was revealed as Riel Darkwater. The Risen were driven back after most of the invaders had been buried by carefully set explosions and
Admiral Feiste Bakkir
, the Risen commander leading the assault, had been defeated.
Shortly after saving the Chantry of Secrets, the Order of Whispers joined forces not only with the allies they had gathered earlier but with the other two Orders—
Durmand Priory
and the
Vigil
—as they assaulted and liberated Claw Island from Zhaitan's control. Demmi stayed behind in the Chantry to assist in the war effort from the home front while Trahearne formed the
Pact
and led a successful assault on
Orr
which culminated in Zhaitan's defeat.
Living World Season 3
After having spent years trapped in the
Royal Palace
as Queen Jennah's "guest" since the
Separatist
assault on Caudecus's Manor, Caudecus fled from the palace and publicly revealing himself as the current Confessor of the
White Mantle
. He incited his Krytan supporters and remaining
centaur
allies in an insurrection against the crown, which culminated in the
Siege of Divinity's Reach
in 1330
AE
. However, the campaign was short-lived when the combined forces of the
Shining Blade
,
Logan Thackeray
's
Seraph
and
Legionnaire
Nylia Steelpaw
's
warband
successfully defended the Krytan capital and eventually pushed Caudecus's forces back at
Lake Doric
with the help of the
Pact Commander
.
Caudecus retreated to his manor where he and his remaining White Mantle captured a Shining Blade infiltration squad led by
Canach
, torturing and killing most members while succumbing to the addictive and strengthening effects of
Bloodstone Dust
which they had been consuming. When Canach's squad had not returned in time, another infiltration force was sent to the manor, this time including Countess Anise, the Pact Commander, and Demmi who knew a hidden passage into her former home which would give the party an element of surprise.
Although Anise questioned whether Demmi would ultimately be loyal to the Order of Whispers or Caudecus should she be forced to choose sides, Demmi remained steadfast in helping the group rescue Canach from the hands of torturers and also asked to be given a chance to talk to Caudecus's ally,
Lady Valette Wi
, in hopes of making her abandon the White Mantle's lost cause. After saving Canach, the group found Caudecus who was already in the process of succumbing to the effects of Bloodstone dust. The now deranged Confessor, accompanied by Valette, was furious at Demmi's betrayal of the family. He announced that Demmi was dead to him and revealed his involvement in the murder of Demmi's mother, furthering Demmi's resolve to stop him.
During the clash Demmi tried to reason with Valette, but Caudecus rebuked her and claimed that Valette was his daughter now. The Confessor was eventually weakened from the fighting but before he could be apprehended, he shot Demmi in the chest with a Bloodstone bullet and fled through a secret passage while claiming that he would see her and his wife again in
the Mists
. The act was enough to make Valette rush to the gravely wounded Demmi who urged her to help the group stop Caudecus so he could not poison any more lives ever again.
While the group headed down to the hidden sanctum to face the Confessor one last time, Logan made the Seraph search the manor for remaining White Mantle and stayed with Demmi while arranging for a medic to look after her. By the time the group had defeated Caudecus and returned to the manor, Demmi had been asking for Valette. Before the pair could exchange words, however, Demmi suddenly claimed to see Tybalt who had found her before she finally succumbed to her injuries. Her body was taken back to her godparents who arranged her funeral.

Hello. I'm priming the portal spike for our next run at the Maws. This will shut down the
Forged's
realm portal and keep their reinforcements from coming through.
Tell me more about the realm portals.
Balthazar's
forces are gathered in the

Better hope those portal spikes work.
What exactly are these portal spikes?
Creator Jadi
invented them. Place one on a realm portal, and it will sap power until the realm portal is closed. The downside is, it needs constant turning once in place. Not very safe for me.

Portal spikes! Genius. I'm prepping one now so we can use it to shut down the Forged realm portal over at the East Maw. That'll stop their reinforcements coming through!
Tell me more about the realm portals.
Balthazar's got all his Forged goons gathered in the Realm of Torment. Lately, they've managed to get portals open, and Forged troops have been spilling out. You see the problem.

What exactly are these portal spikes?
Creator Jadi made it. It saps the realm portal's power until it closes up. But it needs constant tuning, which makes it hard to defend myself while working on it.

Be careful
What type of Forged emerge from the Maws?
Each Maw gifts us with a different breed of Forged—reinforcements to fight the Awakened. The Portal Keepers are the worst. They'll keep the portals open until they're defeated.
I'd like to ask you something else.
They do sound like trouble.
You're in the Order of Shadows?
For the moment. We're really in the Order of Whispers. We heard these Shadow guys might need some help. My brother and I hopped on the first ship over.
You were in contact with the Order of Whispers?
I wouldn't say we were "in contact."(sic) It's more like occasional urgent messages made their way to us. Everyone else hemmed and hawed about lifting a finger to help, but not us!
I'd like to ask you about something else.
Goes back to the initial dialogue
Good. They seem to need you!
I'm sure they appreciate it.
Good! That would definitely be a victory.

Dialogue
All over the region, rogue elements are being driven wild by chaotic ley energies. Innocent lives are at stake. If you're willing to help, pick a target from our bounty board.
How do your bounty boards work?
You can only contract for one bounty at a time. Just choose a target from the board, and hurry to catch up with them in the field. Your contract has a time limit.
What if I change my mind about the bounty I've chosen?
You can swap your contract, join someone else's contract, or abandon the target you've chosen. We keep offering new bounties, so be sure to check back here and at other bounty boards.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
How formidable are these bounty targets?
Each presents a challenge worthy of a group, especially the bounties we've termed "legendary". You may need to cross large distances to find your target. Better mount up and take along some friends.
Why have you labeled some bounties as "legendary"?
Legendary bounties are particularly dangerous. Only renowned adventurers can contract to take on one of those targets. However, anyone can join a bounty hunt in progress. Good fortune!
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
What do you mean, "driven wild by chaotic ley energies"?
Magical feedback from nearby ley lines seems to be driving some creatures and people into a savage frenzy while also granting them unpredictable, anomalous abilities.
Do you know why this is happening?
We have no idea why or how, but something recently caused a major imbalance in the flow of magic across the region. All we know is that it's put all of us in mortal danger.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
Why are you in disguise?
(Only agents in Vabbi)
That's confidential. Let's focus on the matter at hand, hm? There are dangers that need addressing.
I have another question.
Fair enough. I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
Notes
The agents in Vabbi use random Elonian noble appearances.
According to the ambient dialogue of the
Order of Shadows Agent
at
Kodonur Temple
, who refers to specific bounties of other desert regions, the names of the agents who collect bounties at the Chalon Docks in the Domain of Istan and at the Necropolis in the Domain of Vabbi are Doran and Erick, respectively.

PAGE: Doern Velazquez
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Doern_Velazquez

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
and
Living World Season 2
.
Doern Velazquez
Race
Human
Rank
Champion
Location
Chantry of Secrets
(
Kryta
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Pact
“
Kryta? No, no...I mean, yes! Of course. The human nation, right? Kryta. Yes.
—
Doern Velazquez
Doern Velazquez
is one of the three
Preceptors
of the
Order of Whispers
, as well as their infiltration and assassination specialist. When the
Pact
is formed, he becomes the liaison between the Pact and the Order of Whispers, and is the leading officer for Order of Whispers agents.
Upon questioning, Velazquez claims to be from
Kryta
, but displays a lack of knowledge about the kingdom. He does not look
Canthan
or
Elonian
either and is evasive when subjected to further questioning about where he is from, stating that the information is classified. He also lets slip that his career as a field agent ended when his wife was killed in the line of duty.
Biography
Early years
Doern Velazquez's origins and how he joined the
Order of Whispers
are classified, presumably only reserved to the highest-ranking members of the Order such as the
Master of Whispers
and the
Preceptors
. He claims to be from
Kryta
, but displays a lack of knowledge about the kingdom, and does not look
Canthan
and
Elonian
either while being evasive about further inquiries on the subject. While working for the Order, he became a skilled infiltration and assassination specialist. Doern used to work as a field agent until his wife was killed in the line of duty, after which he became a trainer in the
Chantry of Secrets
. He rose through the ranks and eventually became one of the Preceptors, the highest-ranking members answering directly to the Master of Whispers and being one of the few people alive to know the Master's true identity.
Personal story
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography.
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
and the latest
Iniate
to the Order of Whispers brought
Demmi Beetlestone
, who had fled from her father
Caudecus Beetlestone
's estate to deliver troubling news about her father's schemes against the crown of Kryta, to the safety of the Chantry of Whispers in 1325
AE
. Doern, alongside
Riel Darkwater
and fellow Preceptors
Halvora Snapdagger
and
Valenze
,
welcomed
the trio to the headquarters after the trio had defeated the pursuers sent by Caudecus.
Living World Season 2
Once the
Pact
had received reinforcements and funding from the five nations to take on the Elder Dragon
Mordremoth
in 1327
AE
, Doern traveled to the Pact's forward base in
Camp Resolve
to oversee the soldiers' combat training. While there, he expressed delight at being reunited with the Pact Commander and inquired if the Commander would travel with the Pact fleet to the
Heart of Maguuma
. The Commander declined the offer, however, and intended to fight the dragon from a different flank instead, which Doern acknowledged while wishing the Commander good luck.

Reason:
Missing quotes
Trivia
Doern Velazquez is
voiced
by
Peter Giles
.
Velazquez is a Spanish patronymic name, meaning "son of Velasco." "Velasco" is a masculine first name derived from the
Basque
bela- meaning "raven" or "crow" and the diminutive suffix -sco, meaning "little raven/crow" and suggesting that Velazquez may mean "son of the little raven/crow."

Dialogue
Before completing heart
(if account is linked to the
Hall of Monuments
)
Your spirit feels familiar. Is it answers that you seek?
(if account is not linked to the Hall of Monuments)
How can I set your spirit at ease?
Can I do anything for the others around here?
Lead the lost ones to their resting place, pacify the enraged ones, and rekindle the wardlights to repel the
ghost eaters
. If you seek wisdom, visit the
Forgotten
statues.
Tell me more about the lost
ghosts
.
Speak with them and help them reach the gravesites to the north and south, so they can rest. You'll need to best the enraged ones in combat to calm them.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
Forgotten statues?
They contain the ashes and final words of Forgotten
Vissh Rakissh
. Read all eight of them, and you may hear his final message.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
What are the wardlights?
The candles along the Lair's perimeter project a ward of protection against
Joko
's ghost eaters, but they must be kept lit.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
May I ask another question?
I gather you made a name for yourself in your day.
It was my great duty and honor to support
Spearmarshal
Kormir
and to help her avert
Nightfall
.
What can you tell me about Nightfall?
The outcast
god
Abaddon
corrupted leaders and set our forces against each other as he set about remaking
our world
in his image. Heroic effort and
sacrifice
saved
Elona
from destruction.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
You fought beside Kormir?
I was strategist to the
Sunspears
, and she was our Spearmarshal. Kormir was a scholar who became the leader we needed in our darkest hour. She transcended
torment
and
anguish
to become our goddess.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
What keeps you here at the Lair?
Some may be unable to let go of bitterness or regret, but I've chosen another path. As I make amends, I do what I can to help the others lingering here, especially
Meymo
and
Kyrisah
.
Amends? What amends?
I saw Palawa Joko freed to reconquer Elona. I share responsibility for the generations of cruelty he's inflicted on my homeland.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
What's special about Meymo and Kyrisah?
They've had a...difficult time.
My son's spirit
was trapped in the Realm of Torment, so I know death is no guarantee of peace. Those girls should be free to stay young at heart.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
If
asura
:
You're one of the most brilliant human strategists in history. Any insight?
You can extrapolate from every source of knowledge and still fail to predict the consequences of your actions. Take comfort in knowing it's a universal truth.
Existentially terrifying, but fair. Thank you.
If
charr
:
Strategy was the key to your success. Any tactical advice?
If you want to ensure that you never lose a battle, never start one. And if you must fight, consider first how you'll pay the cost of victory.
"In blood" probably isn't the right answer. I salute you.
If
human
:
You've fought one of our gods before. Do you have any advice?
Don't let it shake you to the core. Like us, they are both sublime and flawed—and if we are knowable, so are they.
Thank you for the perspective.
If
norn
:
You're a legend among your people. What advice can you give me?
A legend doesn't need to be true, but legacies bear consequences. When people recount your legend, will you want them to tell the truth?
I'll think on it. Thank you, hero.
If
sylvari
:
You must have more rings than a tree! Any advice for a sapling?
I wish I'd been more flexible when my life was ahead of me. Hold on to your curiosity, and help when you can. It's all any of us can do.
That's both frightening and comforting. Thank you.
May I ask another question?
Thank you for your time.
Thank you for your time.

(if account is not linked to the Hall of Monuments)

(if account is linked to the Hall of Monuments)

Eladaar
is a
Sunspear
found in the
Jahai Bluffs
. Her wife,
Ysri
, and son, Takar, were killed on orders of
Palawa Joko
. Her anger as a result of this event has not dissipated upon Joko's death and she now defaces the
memorials
erected by
Free Awakened
in the bluffs in an act of revenge. She has a home in the vicinity of the
Ancestor Tree
in the
Ruined Procession
.

Dialogue
Why are you disturbing us here? These poor spirits wander in misery, and you insist on inflicting more pain. I should send you to join them!
You're welcome to try.
I'm not here to fight. Can I ask you something?
Ask your questions.
Who are these people?
They are ghosts of Ascension seekers who died during their trials. They're trapped in this world.
Why are they so hostile?
We aren't easily destroyed. But each time our essences are dispersed, we return with a little less of our minds intact. Many of us become violent.
Back up a second.
(Returns to the beginning.)
That explains a lot.
Tell me about something else.
(Returns to "Ask your questions".)
I think I'll be going now.
Who are you, exactly?
Binah, of the Sunspears. This place was once our sanctuary. When the other ghosts came, I swore to protect them.
Tell me about something else.
(Returns to "Ask your questions".)
I think I'll be going now.
I need to leave.

Engineer Vexus Snarlgear
Race
Charr
Level
36
Location
Axedrag Cut
(
Fields of Ruin
)
Organization
Iron Legion
Order of Whispers
Image(s)
Interactive map
Engineer Vexus Snarlgear
is an engineer stationed at
Fangfury Watch
, and an agent for the
Order of Whispers
.
Locations
Ascalon
Fields of Ruin
Axedrag Cut
Dialogue
Keep an eye out. It's not safe to wander around here, even if you're armed to the teeth. You win nine out of ten fights, that last fight will still kill you.
What is this place?
It was a guard post for the road, so soldiers could go to and from
Deathblade's Watch
safely. Now it's a scouting post. We keep an eye on
Renegade
and
Separatist
activity and send word back to the delegations.
What do you do here?
Whatever's needed. Mostly fortifications, and scouting when I don't have something to busy my hands with. I'll probably shift my focus to siege weapons soon.
Sounds like fun.
I see.
(If in Order of Whispers)
Have you ever broken the unbreakable?
I have, and I've repaired the irreparable. I was certain you were in the Order, but one can never assume. What can I do for you?
Heard anything interesting?
I've heard rumors that some sort of
dark, malevolent presence
has found its way in the
Flameroot Caverns
, near an altar deep within. I'm sure if you removed whatever it is, people would be grateful.
I'll consider it.
I know what I'm doing.
Quotes
Greetings
For Iron Legion.
Let me guess. Trouble.
Rely on iron, not false gods.
You have a question?

======================================================================
PAGE: Enz
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Enz
======================================================================

Enz
Race
Sylvari
Level
80
Location
Cathedral of Silence
(
Cursed Shore
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Pact
Enz
is an agent of the
Order of Whispers
located in the
Cathedral of Silence
.
Location
Ruins of Orr
Cursed Shore
Cathedral of Silence
Dialogue
Take a good look. What you see here hasn't been seen in over two hundred years. Well, not by any living thing. And not exactly like this.
What is this place?
This is the legendary
Altar of Murdered Dreams
. It's been underwater for over 250 years, hence the barnacles, general dank smell, and overall disarray.
What used to go on here?
This was Orr's temple to their death
god
,
Grenth
, before it went to the bottom of the
Sea of Sorrows
along with the rest of the continent.
Who came here?
Ritualists
, water
elementalists
,
assassins
and
necromancers
would congregate here and perform their rituals to seek the favors of their patron god. Rough crowd, but devoted.
So now that it's been cleansed, what will happen to it?
Probably be restored to its original purpose. Grenth still has his fans. Besides, what else would it be good for? Not many people want to live in a formal temple to the god of death and ice.
Altar of Murdered Dreams is kind of a grim name, isn't it?
Grenth's temples tend toward the grim, but you can't name shrines to the god of death after flowers and bunnies. Grim architecture, too. Helps the necromancers relax and enjoy their rituals.
I'll just do a little poking around on my own, thanks.
Guess not.
Good to have the place back up and running. I guess.
I see.
Interesting.
Ehn, you've seen one ruined temple to Grenth, you've seen them all.

Evryn

Level
2
Location

Image(s)
Interactive map
Evryn
is a
sylvari
located in the
Village of Astorea
. While in the
Dream of Dreams
, he was surrounded by tall icy mountains, representing the
Shiverpeak Mountains
. He is a member of the
Order of Whispers
and has special dialogue for fellow members.
Location
Maguuma Jungle
Caledon Forest
Village of Astorea
Dialogue
In my Dream, I was surrounded by enormous mountains. Blue-black ice shards were falling from the sky, and the land bled where the shards impaled it. What do you think it meant?
(If Order of Whispers)
You dared to dream the undreamt.
And you travel the untraveled. Welcome, Whisperer, to Astorea. Are you coming from the Grove or going to it?
Going to it.
Then let me warn you. Speak with the wardens as you enter. They take their duty to protect the Grove very seriously. Also be sure to check in at our camp. You'll find allies there.
Good advice. I will do so.
Coming from it.
Then I will warn you. The dangers in this area are many. In recent times, we've had more and more Risen coming westward from the water. Its disconcerting, at best.
Good to know. Anything else?
The Nightmare Court have increased activity in Wychmire Swamp, to the northwest. They are evil and will stop at nothing to bring suffering to everyone they encounter.
Who are the Nightmare Court?
Whereas I seek to do only good, the nightmare courtiers believe the Dream must be tarnished by the nightmare.
Good to know.
Good to know.
Okay. I'll beware.
I'm in a hurry. I'll talk to you some other time.
(If human, charr, or asura)
You had a dream?
Not just any dream. I and all those like me are conceived in the
Dream
, a mystical place where all the world's knowledge is held. I awakened, and thus, I am now here, with you.
Did you bring all that knowledge with you?
No. Only a small dipperful from the vast pool that is all history.
That's too bad.
How strange.
(If norn)
It sounds like you dreamt of my homeland.
Yes, I think so too. I plan to leave in three days time and go there. Any advice you can give me?
Stay alert. Snow and ice often line.
I'll remember that.
Good.
If you go to Hoelbrak, tell them <Character name> sent you.
(Same as "Stay alert...")
Toughen your bark, or you won't survive a fortnight.
(Same as "Stay alert...")
Just be careful.
(If sylvari and chose White Stag in biography)
Your quest is to find that out.
Just as yours was to save the
white stag
?
You heard about that?
Oh, yes. Your Dream was very important. Many are talking about. You saw
a dragon
in your Dream, after all. That is no small omen.
It's no omen at all. Merely, a guide.
Exactly.
I have no idea.
I have no idea.
(if none of the above)

Interactive map
In life,
First Spear Dehvad
was a member of the
Order of the Sunspears
. He was the one responsible for training the Sunspears who would eventually free
Palawa Joko
and defeat
Abaddon
.
When he died, like all Sunspears since Palawa Joko's return, his body was cremated. He was then buried in the
Sandstrewn Shrine
. His spirit remains where his ashes were buried, to offer guidance to those who seek it.
To summon him, players must use either /salute or /bow
emote
on the Sunspear platform.
Location
Crystal Desert
The Desolation
The Darklands
Dialogue
