======================================================================
PAGE: First Spear Hakima
CATEGORIES: Order of the Sunspears, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/First_Spear_Hakima
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
.
First Spear Hakima
Race
Human
Level
80
Locations
Istani Sunspear Base
(
The Astralarium
)
Sun's Refuge
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
Organization
Order of the Sunspears
“
Zaeim has become the Spearmarshal of these Sunspears. We serve his vision of an Istan free from Joko's grasp.
— First Spear Hakima
First Spear Hakima
is the second in command of the
Istani
Sunspears
. She dutifully carries out
Spearmarshal Zaeim
's orders, most recently planning a daring
raid
on
Palawadan, Jewel of Istan
.
Biography
As a native
Istani
, Hakima witnessed the cruelty of the
Mordant Crescent
and Istani nobles loyal to King
Palawa Joko
first-hand. Prompted by the rogue
god
Balthazar
's invasion of
Elona
in 1330
AE
, the
Joko Loyalists
began forcibly recruiting Istani to reinforce the mainland's army while also withholding food supplies from the countryside and claiming those not living in
Palawadan, Jewel of Istan
to be disloyal to the regime. When an eager
Sunspear
named
Zaeim
returned to Istan during its plight, Hakima was the first to be recruited to his Istani branch of Sunspears. The slowly growing Sunspears established a
hidden base
below
the Astralarium
, safe from the prying eyes of Joko's enforcers.
Over time, Zaeim's fame as a commander grew around the
Isle of Istan
, which prompted the ire of the Mordant Crescent who placed a bounty on his head in response. Eventually Hakima and other Sunspears urged Zaeim to take on the title of
Spearmarshal
, accepting him as their leader. Zaeim made Hakima his First Spear, or second in command, who would help him plan strategies for the Sunspears' campaign to free Istan from Joko's yoke.
The Istani Sunspears eventually learned of the deeds of the
Pact Commander
as well as the fall of Balthazar in the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
. They also met with
Shadows Agent Kito
and established contact with the
Order of Shadows
who sent agents to help the Sunspears in their campaign. When news spread that the
Awakened
had mysteriously grown in strength and numbers after the Battle of Kodash and taken over
Champion's Dawn
, Zaeim hurried to liberate the oppressed town. Before leaving, he delegated Hakima to execute the long-planned
raid
on Palawadan.
Hakima met with the Commander who had traveled all the way to Istan to meet with the Sunspears to learn a way into
Fahranur
, the long abandoned city of the
Primeval Dynasty
. Although Hakima was glad to meet the famous Commander, she explained that only Zaeim knew a way into the ancient city and he had already left for Champion's Dawn. She instructed the Commander to find more information about the Spearmarshal's whereabouts from
Priest Sazeer
who was living in the area. After the Commander had left, Hakima rallied the Sunspear forces and sent them to raid Palawadan as planned. A number of Sunspears, assisted by
Dragon's Watch
members
Canach
,
Rytlock Brimstone
and the
dragon
Aurene
, eventually assaulted the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
to liberate the Commander and Zaeim who had been captured. Champion's Dawn would be liberated as well, strengthening the Sunspears' resolve to carry on the fight.
Location
Crystal Desert
Domain of Istan
The Astralarium
Istani Sunspear Base
Jahai Bluffs
Sun's Refuge
Story involvement
Living World Season 4
Episode 1:
Daybreak
Under the Stars
Dialogue
Is there something else you would like to know?
Tell me more about the Sunspears.
First Spear Hakima:
The Sunspears are an ancient order, honorable protectors from brighter years in Elona's history.
First Spear Hakima:
Most of my brothers and sisters were Awakened and forced to serve Joko. You know them now as the Mordant Crescent.
First Spear Hakima:
A few survived, in hiding. Recruiting to sustain the Order across the generations in hopes they might one day rise against Joko.
First Spear Hakima:
Zaeim has become the
Spearmarshal
of these Sunspears. We serve his vision of an Istan free from Joko's grasp.
Tell me more about Zaeim.
First Spear Hakima:
Zaeim was trained by a Sunspear named
Kitur
, until word of Istan's plight reached his ears.
First Spear Hakima:
When
Balthazar
attacked the mainland, things got worse in Istan. Much worse.
First Spear Hakima:
Istan was Zaeim's home, and despite his mentor's protests he could not sit by while it burned.
First Spear Hakima:
Zaeim vowed to bring the Sunspears out of the shadows. To once again make them a shield for the innocent and the scourge of the corrupt.
First Spear Hakima:
I was the first he recruited, but many would follow. Eventually we convinced him to take the title of Spearmarshal.
Tell me more about the rebellion.
First Spear Hakima:
Balthazar's campaign on the mainland caused ripples throughout the Kingdom.
First Spear Hakima:
Crops burned; trade lines disrupted. Shipments to Istan that never got past
Palawadan
.
First Spear Hakima:
Joko's "
loyalists
" are comprised mainly of the island's nobility, and only they are allowed to live life within Palawadan, its port city.
First Spear Hakima:
They control what comes in and what goes out. They decided food would not go out.
First Spear Hakima:
When people objected, the
Temple of Joko
said those outside Palawadan's walls were disloyal—and didn't DESERVE food.
First Spear Hakima:
It started out as a way for the nobility to feel virtuous watching half the island starve. But it escalated. Quickly.
How did the fighting start?
(only after listening to the first three monologues)
First Spear Hakima:
Issnur Isles
had enough, and declared their independence from the rest of Istan.
First Spear Hakima:
By then, the Temple congregation had armed themselves. The loyalists went down there, intending to make an example of the disloyal.
First Spear Hakima:
Zaeim was prepared, though. He taught us a great deal abouit guerilla tactics.
First Spear Hakima:
The Sunspears crushed the loyalists. Sent them running all the way to the northern shore.
First Spear Hakima:
The Awakened garrison was manned by a skeleton crew-no pun intended-thanks to your... liberation of the mainland forces.
First Spear Hakima:
Nonetheless, the Mordant Crescent sent fifty Awakened to find this famous Sunspear Champion-and end the rebellion.
First Spear Hakima:
We don't know how he did it, but two facts are undisputed: those Awakened lie dead, and Zaeim lives.
First Spear Hakima:
The people started calling him "The Hero of Istan." It galvanized the whole island behind him.
So what turned the tide against the Sunspears?
(only after listening to the fourth monologue)
First Spear Hakima:
The Mordant Crescent and their "loyalists" imposed martial law, even within Palawadan itself.
First Spear Hakima:
They started conscripting the living, and Awakening the dead. If you won't serve in life, you can always serve in death.
First Spear Hakima:
Anyone suspected of aiding the Sunspears gets rounded up for "re-education."
First Spear Hakima:
With their supply lines broken, their families imprisoned, recruits getting harder and harder to find...
First Spear Hakima:
Well, as you can imagine, things started falling apart.
First Spear Hakima:
The Mordant Crescent would rather watch Istan burn than tolerate any commoner who shows some spine.
First Spear Hakima:
And Zaeim and his Sunspear recruits-well, we have spines of iron.
I have no other questions for now.
Note:
First Spear Hakima will not speak to you if you have not yet completed the 'Speak to Archivist Rhadha' step of
Under the Stars
.
Trivia
Hakima is the feminine form of Hakim and means 'wise' in
Arabic
.

======================================================================
PAGE: First Spear Kitur
CATEGORIES: Order of the Sunspears
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/First_Spear_Kitur
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
First Spear Kitur
Race
Human
Level
80
Rank
Veteran
Locations
Yahnur Plateau
(
Domain of Vabbi
)
Vehtendi Academy
(
Domain of Vabbi
)
Sun's Refuge
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
Organization
Order of the Sunspears
Part of
Help First Spear Kitur recover his equipment and escape imprisonment
Image(s)
Kitur first appears while injured.
“
Think of me every time you plunge your weapon into the guts of a Mordant Crescent, eh?
— First Spear Kitur
First Spear Kitur
is a member of the
Order of the Sunspears
who has become slightly unhinged after being imprisoned and tortured by the
Mordant Crescent
. He used to be the mentor of
Spearmarshal Zaeim
.
Biography
Path of Fire
After joining the
Order of the Sunspears
, Kitur served them loyally over the years and rose to the rank of First Spear. He eventually took in an apprentice from
Istan
,
Zaeim
, and trained him to become a Sunspear. When
Balthazar
's
Forged
army invaded
Elona
and
Palawa Joko
's regime imposed stricter laws everywhere in 1330
AE
, Zaeim learned of Istan's plight and returned home to aid his people despite Kitur's protests.
Kitur continued to fight and organized a refugee escort through
the Desolation
with
Second Spear Nayrim
waiting on the other end. However, the escort was ambushed by the
Mordant Crescent
, and Kitur fought the ambushers as a distraction, allowing the refugees to flee to safety while being captured himself. He was stripped of his weapons and armor and tossed into
the Warehouse
in the
Vehtendi Academy
where he was questioned and tortured by the Mordant Crescent who wished to learn the whereabouts of other surviving Sunspears. The mental and physical torture did not break Kitur, however, although his treatment and lengthy imprisonment did seem to affect his sanity somewhat as he believed his blade Rashee was speaking to him.
After some time had passed, the invasion of
Kralkatorrik
's
Branded
caused enough chaos around the academy that allowed Kitur to think of a way to escape. He was helped by the
Pact Commander
and other adventurers who happened to be in the region and escorted him out of the academy while helping him gather his armor pieces and trusty blade. Following the successful escape, he parted ways with the adventurers while wishing them good fortune. Before leaving the area, however, he learned that the Commander had reeducated the Vehtendi cadets about the real history of the world instead of Joko's altered version of it, and believed that the Commander's actions may have planted the seeds of a new generation of Sunspears to oppose Joko's regime.
Following Balthazar's death at the Commander's hands in the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
, Kitur and other Sunspears were drawn to
Yahnur Plateau
by strange but inspiring visions. They found their way to the
Sunspear Sanctuary
and met with the conscious remains of
Tahlkora
, the last Spearmarshal, who revealed that she had summoned them all there to help reunite the scattered order and inspire the people of Elona. The Commander aided the Sunspears and the Spearmarshal's
griffons
and helped them
defend the sanctuary
from a Mordant Crescent scouting squad which tried to kill all the Sunspears present after discovering the hideout.
Once the Mordant Crescent had been defeated, the Sunspears finally had a base of operations in the
Domain of Vabbi
safe from prying eyes of Joko's agents. Their next task would be to learn to trust the griffons and how to fly with them, giving the Sunspears an edge against landbound
Awakened
forces. Kitur conversed with the Commander during his stay in the sanctuary, explaining what he had been up to and informing the Commander of the great work done at Vehtendi Academy.
Living World Season 4
In 1331
AE
, Kitur and the other Vabbian Sunspears received word about Joko's death in the
Battle of Gandara
and that every Sunspear's help would be needed to fortify the newest base of the Sunspears,
Sun's Refuge
, in
Jahai Bluffs
against the growing threat of Kralkatorrik and its Branded. Tahlkora sent the Vabbian Sunspears to Jahai to aid the Istani Sunspears, and Kitur was reunited with Zaeim who had received Tahlkora's blessing to become her successor as the new Spearmarshal of the Sunspears.
Locations
Crystal Desert
Domain of Vabbi
Vehtendi Academy
The Warehouse
Yahnur Plateau
Sunspear Sanctuary
(Only after earning
Yasfahr, the Rune of Sacrifice
)
Jahai Bluffs
Sun's Refuge

can be expanded
“
Keep to the shadows, and stay quiet.
— Gamina
Gamina
was the
First Mate
of the
Priority Divide
in 1237
AE
, but when
Captain Tarb
had retired she left the old
asura
's service and joined the
Lionguard
.
Bronn Svaard
, captain of the Lionguard, kept trying to give her command of one of the
Havens
, but Gamina always refused.
Cobiah Marriner
had discovered why when she approached him with an offer from the
Order of Whispers
. In return for keeping her informed about the
Captain's Council
meetings and allowing her to attend, the Order would keep him apprised of the goings on in the criminal underworld of his city. This tradition continued right up until the city's destruction in 1327
AE
, with
Riel Darkwater
as the most recent agent to hold this role.

Where is that, exactly?
Inside the circle of torches, just up the hill. Stand on the icon of the sun, and show a sign of respect.
Thank you.
Tell me about something else.
I'll do that, thanks.
(If working on
Open Skies: The Desolation
)
Actually, I'm here looking for a rune. Seen it?
Can't say that I have. You might ask First Spear Dehvad.
Where is he?
Go up the hill and stand in the circle of torches, on the sun. Then show a sign of respect.
Thank you.
I'll do that, thanks.
Sorry, no.

Organization
Order of Whispers
Services
Living World
Heralds
were NPCs that were added to each of the major
cities
with the
Flame and Frost: Prelude
release
. These NPCs provide information on the latest occurrences in the
Living World
of
Tyria
, and direct players where to go in order to experience any new content.
Upon logging in for the first time after the introduction of heralds, players receive a unique
mail
that varies depending on their race, informing them of the heralds' appearance and purpose.
Location
Historical locations
There are two heralds in each of the major capital cities (The
Black Citadel
,
Divinity's Reach
,
Hoelbrak
,
Rata Sum
,
The Grove
, and
Lion's Arch
), located next to the main entrance as well as the asura portal to Lion's Arch (or, in the case of Lion's Arch, on the bridge to the gate hub leading to the other cities).

Trivia
An anonymous herald referring to themselves simply as "Your herald" sends characters an introductory mail each time they reach the level of a
Dungeon
's story or unlock its explorable mode. During
Living World Season 4
, she finally reveals herself to be
Lady Camilla
.
Based on the dialogue, it seems like there was another version of the Beacons of Kryta activity during Queen's Jubilee planned.

Historian Hartwell
Race
Human
Level
25
Location
Traveler's Dale
(
Gendarran Fields
)
Organization
Durmand Priory
Order of Whispers
Image(s)
Interactive map
Historian Hartwell
is a historian staying at
First Haven
.
Location
Kryta
Gendarran Fields
Traveler's Dale
Dialogue
It seems I'm being rather a bit of an annoyance here to these Lionguard. You'd think soldiers stationed in the first haven ever would be respectful of its history, regardless of whatever centaur squabbles are going on.
(If character is a member of the Order of Whispers)
Are you in the Priory? So am I. My name is <Character name>.
Doesn't ring a bell, but that doesn't mean anything. I've been out of touch, and my particular historical research doesn't contribute much to this dragon business. I'm Hartwell. Good to meet you.
Tell me more about what you're studying.
The political history of armed forces in Tyria, specifically the role of each within society. I've spent time with both the Seraph and the Lionguard. Next, I hope to make the long, dangerous trek to Ebonhawke to study the Vanguard.
Ebonhawke?
Ebonhawke's the last remaining human resistance against the charr, though currently there's a truce. I prefer to travel by foot, not asura gate, so it'll require me to cross the breadth of the norn lands and continue through the charr territories.
Hope all goes well.
Well, they have jobs to do.
Notes
Anomaly
:
He refers to himself and the player's character as Durmand Priory, but the dialogue only appears if you are on a character who joined the Order of Whispers.

PAGE: Jitta
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Jitta
======================================================================

Jitta
Race
Sylvari
Level
76
Location
Theater of Delight
(
Malchor's Leap
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Pact
Jitta
is an
Order of Whispers
agent studying the risen on the south edge of
Theater of Delight
, just below the statue of
Melandru
.
Location
Ruins of Orr
Malchor's Leap
Theater of Delight
Dialogue
It's not often that I see other living things way out here.
(If in the
Order of Whispers
)
I like to explore the unexplorable.
Then you have reached the unreachable. I've been sent here to study the habits of these
Risen farmers
. Unfortunately, it's not for purely intellectual reasons.
What do you mean?
The Order of Whispers studies the techniques the Risen use in battle. So we use these Risen farmers almost like lab animals. We provoke them to attack, taking minimum risk while observing them.
Why the farmers?
While the Risen farmers can be just as vicious as the rest of the Risen, they are unarmed. They have only their farm tools to attack with. This makes them easier to defeat once engaged.
So there's no risk?
We have lost several Whispers agents to Risen farmers. They have only one objective - to kill the living. And a rake can kill you just as dead as any sword or gun.
Let's hope the days of observing the Risen are over soon.
What are you doing out here?
Just watching these Risen farmers. It's eerie how they go about their daily routines as if they hadn't spent the last 250 years at the bottom of the sea. For them, nothing has changed.
What do you mean?
They till the same earth even though it's now barren. The cows continue to graze, though there is no grass. And the Risen chickens still crow every sunrise to wake the others, although the farmers never sleep.
It's tragic.
It's a dangerous place to be alive.

By Death's Gaze Torch

Welcome to our camp,
Commander
. Don't look surprised... I know who you are. It's our business to know everything that's going on around here. You couldn't have come at a better time.
Do you need help with anything?
Absolutely. You can repair sentry turrets or collect data from our surveillance devices. Agent in the field may need medical attention. And of course, deal with any
Forged
and
Awakened
you find.
I have another question.
I'll get right on it.
What's the situation here?
Terrible. It's a rat's nest of Forged down here. We're in a constant tug-of-war to close portals letting them in from the
Realm of Torment
. Though many leave the area once they get through.
Where do they go?
Lately they've been heading east through the
Scorched March
. I assume they're targeting
the Bone Palace
. If they control that, there's not much to stop them from reaching Vabbi.
The Bone Palace? They're coming after Joko's Awakened?
Palawa Joko
didn't take too kindly to a whole army appearing on his doorstep. When the Forged started to show up, he mobilized pretty quickly. These days, it looks like his side is loosing.
I have another question.
Interesting. Thanks.
I have another question.
Sounds like a desperate situation.
I have another question.
I'll see what I can do to help.
Thanks. I'll have a look around.
You've lived up to your reputation,
Commander
. Sometimes it feels like we're against impossible odds, but with your help, we're making progress. We have supplies on hand if you're interested.
Let me see what you've got.

Dialogue
In Eye of the North
Well met,
Commander
. The Crystal Bloom is doing what we can to safeguard the Eye.
Where's
Bangar
?
(after completing
Champion's End
)
He's been taken to a field hospital. When
Jormag
died, he collapsed in his cell. Thankfully, we got to him in time. According to the medics, the release of
dragon corruption
was...grisly.
I see. Thanks for the update.
Is
Caithe
here?
Lady Caithe is attending to the
Crystal Bloom
's efforts elsewhere. No doubt she'll reconvene here in due time.
I see, thank you.
Your aid is appreciated.
In Arborstone and Seitung Province
Commander, it's good to see you again! Is there anything you need from me?
That's an
impressive weapon
you have there.
Thank you. The greatsword is part of a set of powerful weapons based on Aurene. I have some supplies that can help you get started toward crafting one. Would you like to see?
Yes, please show me your supplies.
Yes, please show me your supplies.
Yes, please show me your research materials.
Not right now, thanks!

======================================================================
PAGE: Librarian Addisu
CATEGORIES: Order of Shadows
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Librarian_Addisu
======================================================================

Librarian Addisu
Race
Human
Level
80
Location
The Darklands
(
The Desolation
)
Organization
Order of Shadows
Image(s)
Interactive map
Librarian Addisu
is a member of the
Order of Shadows
stationed in the
Chantry of Shadows
, on the library's first floor.
Location
Crystal Desert
The Desolation
The Darklands
Chantry of Shadows
Dialogue
Oh, a visitor! We haven't gotten that many lately. No one reads anymore. What are they teaching their kids? I'm Addisu, by the way, head librarian for the Order.
The Order has a librarian?
Someone had to keep track of all these books. What you see here is only a fraction of our collection. We have thousands of volumes. Many are hundreds of years old.
Why do you collect so many?
Well,
Palawa Joko
has a habit of destroying anything that contradicts his official propaganda. We're trying to preserve the knowledge and history of
Elona
and
Vabbi
.
For posterity?
Can I ask about something else?
In a way. When Joko is finally defeated, these books will help us rebuild our society. We will step into our future armed with the wisdom of our past.
Can I ask about something else?
Your optimism is refreshing.
Keep up the good work.
Can I ask about something else?
I'll have to come back and see it later.
Anything good I could borrow?
(The second dialogue option will trigger a random answer among the ones listed here)
Are you looking for a new pet? May I suggest "The Joys of
Junundu
: trapping and Taming There Misunderstood Creatures"? I found it quite helpful!
Can I ask about something else?
I'll stick with the raptor, thanks.
I've got all seventeen volumes of "The Complete Lore of
Tyria
", if you're interested.
Can I ask about something else?
I would, but I don't own a bookshelf.
How about, "The Value of Loyalty" by Imperial Bodyguard
Shiro Tagashi
? Hmm, maybe not.
Can I ask about something else?
I think I'm better off without it.
For a light, easy read, I recommend "A Treatise on the Life and Mating Habits of the Common
Sand Eel
, Vol. 3." The other two are checked out. We can't keep them on the shelves!
Can I ask about something else?
Sounds fascinating, but no thanks.
Here's "Lonely Tyria: Traveler's Guide to the Crystal Desert." It's the 1074
AE
edition, though.
Can I ask about something else?
I think a few things have changed since then.
We did just acquire the latest volume of "The True History of Elona : Revised Edition", by Palawa Joko. I keep it in the "Morbid Humor" section.
Can I ask about something else?
That's not my style of humor.
We have the complete "Unending Quest of Heroes" series, though if you haven't started them yet, I wouldn't. the author died before he could finish it. Terrible cliffhanger.
Can I ask about something else?
I hate when that happens.
How about this old favorite? "the
Gods
Are Always Watching you." For some reason, no one checks it out anymore.
Can I ask about something else?
I wonder why.
Well, we have a new one, just in from some traveling
Priory
scholars out of Central Tyria : "
Trahearne
: Selfless Hero or History's Biggest Thief?" I haven't read it yet.
Can I ask about something else?
Thanks but no thanks.
Do you cook? We've got "The Aromatic Kitchen Presents : How to Cook
Sulfurous Ooze
." Very popular.
Can I ask about something else?
I just lost my appetite. Forever.
I was just stopping by.

Order of Whispers
rank.  For the title, see

is a rank within the

. They are squad leaders and spy masters among the organization.

is an
Order of Whispers
Lightbringer stationed in
Earthshake Basin
to monitor
Jormag
's progress along with her pet
Stella
.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Frostgorge Sound
Earthshake Basin
Dialogue
You don't look like you're here to deliver supplies or reports. Unless I miss my guess, you're <Character name>, right? I'd heard you might show up around here. You need something?
Why's the Order interested in things so far north?
The Order's interested in everything. Stella and I've been up here monitoring
Jormag
's progress for a long time now. It's been rough, and it's only getting worse.
Looks like the
Vigil
and the
Priory
are here, too.
Sure, the Vigil had this place first, but it was manned by a skeleton crew. I alerted the crew and the Priory about Jormag's efforts here, and now it's a proper base.
Sounds like it was dull until then.
Monitoring minions is dangerous work—plenty of fighting. And Stella's been great company. I also took up knitting. Don't laugh; this far north, it's a survival skill.
What about the Vigil and the Priory?
(Same as "Looks like the Vigil and the Priory are here, too.")
Touché.
Glad to hear it. Thanks
Just you and your dog? Sounds boring.
(Same as "Sounds like it was dull until then.")
I see.
I'm fine, thanks.

No, I'm just fantastic at tracking things down.

[
sic
]
to provide extra protection for Pact forces.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Lightbringer_Tybalt_Leftpaw
======================================================================

This article is about the story character.  For the
Mist Champion
, see
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw (mist champion)
.
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
Race
Charr
Profession
Engineer
Location
Unspecified
Organization
Order of Whispers
Image(s)
Character introduction cinematic.
“
When you stand with the Order of Whispers, you never stand alone.
— Tybalt, in
An Apple a Day
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
is a former soldier of the
Iron Legion
. He left the legions in disgrace after an explosion wiped out his
warband
and mangled his right hand, fusing two of the fingers together and giving him his nickname. Tybalt hints that at least some of his warband survived the explosion and forced him out. After leaving the Iron Legion, he joined the
Order of Whispers
in 1319
AE
, performing clerical work for six years and designing the security system for the
Chantry of Secrets
before being allowed to serve as a field agent. He was paired with
a new initiate
and assigned the mission of finding, protecting, and escorting
Demmi Beetlestone
to the Chantry of Secrets; during this mission, he acted as a senior field agent, and despite this he and the initiate continued to work together in recruiting one of the lesser races of
Tyria
in the battle against the
Elder Dragons
.
After discovering a
Risen Creature
in
Lion's Arch
, Tybalt and his partner traveled to
Claw Island
to warn
Watch Commander Talon
of the impending attack. Unfortunately they are too late, and Tybalt sacrifices himself so that his partner,
Trahearne
, and the injured
Lionguard
can make it to safety.
Zhaitan
and
Mordremoth
(and their minions) both taunt Tybalt's former partner about his death.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 4: Order Neophyte
Stealing Secrets
Down the Hatch
They Went Thataway
Thrown Off Guard
An Apple a Day
Chapter 5: Helping Hands
Grawl path
Doubt
Salvation Through Heresy

Pets and Walls Make Stronger Kraals

Quaggan path
Bad Ice

Pastkeeper

Skritt path
Rat-Tastrophe

A
hallucination
of Tybalt in
the Tower of Nightmares

A
mental projection
of Tybalt in during

A vision of Tybalt appears upon collecting the
Wandering Memory
during
The Battle for the Jade Sea
.
Notes
Tybalt typically uses a rifle in combat, as well as explosives and turrets and in
An Apple a Day
, he uses a flamethrower, which shows he is likely an engineer. This is further supported by comments made by Tybalt, such as
"...a broken-down engineer."
or in the moments before his death, when he says
"Nobody needs a shaky engineer."
However, during the few times he is seen swimming during
Down the Hatch
, he is seen wielding a
Dark Asuran Harpoon
, which engineers cannot wield, as they are limited to harpoon guns.
Trivia
Tybalt is known across the
Order of Whispers
for his curious fondness for apples.
Although he wields a pistol and shield in the "Welcome to the Order of Whispers" cinematic, he wields a rifle in battle.
Tybalt is
voiced
by
Jim Ward
.
A character of the same name
Tybalt
appears in the Shakespearean play
Romeo and Juliet
where he is once referred to as the "King of Cats."

—membership—confirmed.

This—unit—reports—to—the—
Order—of—Whispers
. Local—contact—is—undercover—as—Inquest—
Krewe
—Member—3269.f1.7841.
Why are you here?
I—am—an—observer—and—communicator. I—document—and—relay—data—back—to—heaquarters. I—also—receive—messages—and—mission—updates—on—a—regular—basis. I—am—undercover. The—Inquest—do—not—know—I—am—a—spy.

They—arrived—on—the—first—submarine. At—that—time, they—worked—for—
Lazarus
, but—their—mission—has—changed. Once—they—began—exploring—the—island, they—began—to—excavate.

Zinn
's—legacy. They—have—discovered—that—the—
asuran
—genius—Zinn—came—here—after—fleeing—
Rata—Novus
. The—theory—is—that—he—brought—his—magitech—with—him. They—are—searching—for—it.
Why do the Inquest care about Zinn?
The—Inquest—have—an—aversion—to—lost—knowledge, ever—since—
Quora—Sum
—was—destroyed. Zinn—is—viewed—as—one—of—the—greatest—geniuses—of—all—time. Their—priority—is—now—to—find—his—lost—work.
Why is the Order of Whispers concerned?
The—Order—of—Whispers—believes—Zinn—was—working—on—dangerous—magitech—in—Rata—Novus, based—on—findings—in—his—data—banks—there. They—wish—to—ensure—it—doesn't—fall—into—the—wrong—hands.
What kind of magitech?
I—have—limited—information—on—that, but—I—was—directed—to—watch—for—anything—unnatural—and—report—back—immediately—if—one—of—Zinn's—devices—turns—up.
Good to know. Carry on.
Excellent information. Carry on.
Mum's the word.
Understood.
Mission accepted.
After completion

Don't mind if I do!

Master of Shadows
Race

80

(

)

The
Master of Shadows
is the head of the
Order of Shadows
stationed in the
Chantry of Shadows
.
Location
Crystal Desert
The Desolation
The Darklands
Chantry of Shadows
Dialogue
If in
Order of Whispers
Hello,
Commander
. The Order of Shadows is always pleased to welcome visitors from
our sister order
. I hope you're here to help us with our fight.
Yes, I'm here to help stop
Balthazar
. What can I do?
Keeper Pamir
is the one you want to talk to. He can tell you what's needed. We appreciate you assistance and are glad to have you stand with us.
Let me ask you something else.
I'll go find him now.
How did you know I'm in the Order of Whispers?
We have some limited contact with the Order of Whispers, exchanging news of our efforts, but very rarely. I'm not surprised they never mentioned us to you.
What's the difference between the two orders?
Our guiding principles and methods are very similar. However, our focus is necessarily different.
Palawa Joko
has been our primary concern for many years.
Let's talk about something else.
That makes sense. Thanks!
Back up a second.
They told you about me? Great.
Your headquarters are a little...modest.
Would you really expect us to be ostentatious? Besides, in true Shadows fashion, there's more to this place than what you can see.
Like through that door behind you?
For instance.
Can I see what's in there?
No.
All right, enough about the door.
I don't want to go in there anyway.
Got it. On more thing...
I'm not even going to ask.
Point taken. Let's talk about something else.
Of course there is.
We'll talk later.
If not in Order of Whispers
Greetings, Commander. Welcome to our outpost. I must say, I hope you're here to help us with our fight.
Yes, I'm here to stop Balthazar. What can I do?
(Same as above.)
Order of Shadows? Is that like the Order of Whispers?
Yes and no. We began as one and the same, but when Elona was cut off from the rest of
Tyria
, we charted our own course. We maintain limited contact with them.
What's the difference between the two orders?
(Same as above.)
Back up a second.
I'd better be going.
Your headquarters are a little...modest.
(Same as above.)
We'll talk later.
Notes
Originally the Master of Shadows's dialogue addressed player characters from any of the three
orders
as members of the Order of Whispers. This was fixed with an unannounced change in a later patch.

======================================================================
PAGE: Master of Whispers
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Master_of_Whispers
======================================================================

“
The Master of Whispers is the shadowy individual who leads our order. You're not cleared to know more.
—
Doern Velazquez
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Master of Whispers
.
The
Master of Whispers
is the leader of the secret organization known as the
Order of Whispers
. To protect the Order their identity is kept secret from all but the three
Preceptors
who pass their orders on to the agents of the Order.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to the story of
Guild Wars 2
.
Riel Darkwater
is revealed to be the
Master of Whispers
in the
personal story
Critical Blowback
for players who have chosen to join the
Order of Whispers
. Riel became the Master of Whispers after defeating the previous Master, who had been corrupted by the dragons. Riel was assisted by
Trahearne
in her struggle against her predecessor.

Dredge mining activities are disrupting our operations in this area. We could use help sabotaging their mining operation.

Notes
Anomaly
:
In addition to costing more karma than other mithril mining pick cost, the one that Mensorr sells has only 75 uses instead of the usual 100 and requires level 60 instead of the usual mithril level requirement of 45. It is also of "fine" quality instead of basic.

PAGE: Momac

======================================================================

Momac
Race
Sylvari
Location
Garden of Noon
(
The Grove
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Image(s)
Interactive map  (Depths)
Momac
is Noon bloom
sylvari
who tells travelers about the
cycles
and studies the
Nightmare Court
for the
Order of Whispers
.
Location
Maguuma Jungle
The Grove
Garden of Noon
Dialogue
May the sunshine warm your shoulders. You look as if you've seen a few adventures. You're welcome to stay and rest awhile with us Noon blooms.
(if non-sylvari)
Noon blooms? What does that mean?
Of course, you wouldn't know! I'm sorry. All of my kin are born during one of four cycles: Dawn, Noon, Dusk, or Night. Your cycle affects your personality, we've found.
How so?
For example, Dawn blooms have a talent for diplomacy. For Noon blooms, it's action. Dusk blooms often pursue more
scholarly
activities, and no one knows the shadows like Night blooms.
How does this happen?
Our
mentors
teach that one's cycle-the time of day one awakens from the
Dream
-imprints certain qualities upon us. Of course, there's always free will.
I see.
I'll see you around.
I'm not sure I've got time for the rest of this.
(If in the Order of Whispers)
We should cure the uncurable.
That would require us to change the unchangeable. I've been studying the Nightmare Court for the Order of Whispers.
Have you learned anything interesting?
I understand more about their philosophy. They see the
Ventari Tablet
as manipulative and false, and they seek to free others from its influence. They see themselves as the saviors of the race.
How does one become so blind?
It takes effort. To join, you must purge yourself of everything that is good about you by doing terrible things. Once your morality is destroyed, you have a place in the Court.
Depressing. Good-bye.
That is frightening. I don't want to hear any more.
Keep up the good work.
Not now, I'm afraid. I have much to do.

PAGE: Myrun Skialkin
CATEGORIES: Order of the Crystal Bloom, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Myrun_Skialkin
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
The Icebrood Saga
and
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
Myrun Skialkin
Race
Norn
Profession
Elementalist
Level
80
Location
Dragon Response Mission: Thunderhead Peaks
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Organization
Durmand Priory
Order of the Crystal Bloom
“
Strength of will and a bit of heart can go pretty far.
—
Myrun Skialkin
Myrun Skialkin
is a
norn
member of the
Order of the Crystal Bloom
who used to be a member of the
Durmand Priory
. They defend
Thunderhead Keep
from
destroyers
and offer
Braham Eirsson
advice on how to embrace his role as the Norn of
Prophecy
.
Biography
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand biography with events from End of Dragons.
Myrun Skialkin used to work for the
Durmand Priory
but joined the
Order of the Crystal Bloom
some time after
Aurene
's rise.
In 1333
AE
, they helped the
Pact
defend
Thunderhead Keep
against the
Elder Dragon
Primordus
's invading
destroyers
and welcomed
Braham Eirsson
and the
Pact Commander
who had arrived at the keep to
assist
the besieged troops.
After the party had helped boost the morale of the survivors, they advanced deeper into the keep and discovered that a group of
Deldrimor
stone dwarves
had emerged from the
Depths of Tyria
to battle the destroyers. Myrun was surprised to see so many dwarves on the surface but quickly joined the Deldrimor's side as the party headed deeper into the keep. Myrun exchanged words with Braham about
Jormag
being a twin of Primordus and whether that would validate the theory that only the twin Elder Dragons could kill one another. When Braham expressed doubt how Primordus being Jormag's weakness would fit into the Norn of Prophecy's destined role to slay Jormag, Myrun reminded Braham that the truths of
prophecies
would not become clear until they had come to pass.
The party eventually discovered a large
wyvern
, the
Destroyer of the Last Forge
, leading the invasion and battled it to drive back the rest of the destroyers. Myrun suggested that the Commander use Aurene's gift to alter the power of the
Dragonsblood Spears
to injure the destroyer
champion
and that Braham should use his connection to the destroyers to distract them so the Commander could inflict damage on the champion.
After the party had triumphed over the destroyers, Myrun thanked the Commander for being granted an opportunity to fight alongside them. Myrun also encouraged Braham by stating that they understood the weight Braham was carrying as the prophesied hero while reminding Braham that his actions would be for nothing unless he had his heart in the fights to come.
Myrun spent some time in Thunderhead Keep cleaning up the aftermath of the destroyer attack. Following the deaths of Jormag and Primordus in the
clash
at
Anvil Rock
, Myrun attended to Aurene in the
Eye of the North
. Later, the scholar traveled to
Hoelbrak
to enjoy
Dragon Bash
and met up with Braham who was not in a festive mood despite many norn celebrating two Elder Dragons' demise. Myrun revealed that they had arrived to defend their high score in
Hologram Stampede
while Braham admitted to have come for the starcake. Myrun approved of Braham's taste, stating that Asta made the best starcakes.
In a subsequent conversation with the Commander, Myrun explained what they had been up to since Thunderhead Keep. The scholar also pointed out the origins of Dragon Bash which they found problematic and suggested that none in the group should tell Aurene about the group attending the festivities to celebrate the slaying of Elder Dragons as Aurene might take the news the wrong way.

Dialogue
Dragon Bash (Hoelbrak)
Commander
, good to see you. I hope you're taking some time to enjoy the festival. This is a celebration of your legend as much as anyone else's.
Are you just visiting Hoelbrak?
Myrun Skialkin:
Ah, yes, I've been all over the North since
last we met
. Spent some time helping clean up
Thunderhead Keep
.
Myrun Skialkin:
Then it was back to the
Eye of the North
, to attend to
Aurene
.
Myrun Skialkin:
But I had to come to Hoelbrak for the festival. I look forward to it every year.
Myrun Skialkin:
Though I do my best not to think about its origins. I just try to enjoy it for what it is.
Myrun Skialkin:
Maybe we shouldn't tell Aurene we were here, though. While this festival is obviously not about slaying her...
Myrun Skialkin:
She might take it the wrong way.
Well, I need to stock up on
Jorbreakers
for the year. See you around.
Seitung Harbor

Aurene's Enclave
in

It's good to see you up and about, Commander. Aurene is still resting at the moment.
How are you all here?
This is where Aurene dragged herself to shore. With her in such a vulnerable state, the empress thought it fitting to let those who knew her tend to her. So we were allowed to accompany Caithe here.
That's reassuring to hear.
I hope she feels better soon.
Outside Aurene's Enclave in Crystal Cave, after completing Deepest Secrets

verification requested
]
Aurene isn't here, Commander. She and Caithe are helping reverse the aftereffects in the Jade Sea.
Thank you, Myrun.
In
Seitung Prison
cells, from
To Catch a Spider
to

Oh, thank Aurene. It's good to see another mainlander. Have you heard anything from Ambassador Meade? Or...the commander?
It's me, Myrun. I'm in disguise.

Myrun Skialkin:
Ah, I see, it's you, Commander. Any tips for the recently incarcerated?

(ends dialogue)
I'm glad to know you're okay. Though I wouldn't linger too long... I wouldn't want the guards catching on to you.
What are you doing in here?
We stayed behind to protect Aurene's sanctuary here, but the Minister of Security rounded us up so quickly after Yong. I suppose we made ourselves easy targets.
We'll clear our names. Don't worry.
Aurene is helping to contain Soo-Won in Dragon's End.
As noble as ever. We should be by her side, helping! Not here, in these cells. I can only hope some of the others made it there.
We'll get you out of here soon.
I'm sorry, Myrun. I have to go.
Hoelbrak
during
Dragon Bash
once player has completed
End of Dragons
Commander, I'm glad to see you're well. Nothing like a festival to bring us all together, eh?
It's been a while — how are you faring, Myrun?
The seasons have been kind to me. And we have you and Aurene to thank for this time of peace.
So the end of the Elder Dragons hasn't taken the shine off of the festival?
Not at all. With Canthan trade reopening, I've had the opportunity to learn more about their Dragon Festival tradition. We can commemorate the triumph over the Elder Dragons while also celebrating Aurene.
That's a good way of looking at it.
Good to hear.
It's good to see everyone celebrating — and to earn some candy, of course.

)
Trivia
Myrun is
voiced
by
Liam O'Brien
.
Myrun's name comes from
Old Norse
"Mýrún" which is a feminine name derived from the
Gaelic
name "Muirinn" (composed of
muir
= 'sea' and
fionn
= 'white, fair') or "Muirne" (which means 'festive').
They wear dyed
Priory's Historical Pants
, a
Lupine Doublet
and
Lupine Gloves
with the pauldrons and helmet hidden.
They wield a
Verdant Staff
.

Naima Ice-veined
Race

Locations

Pirates

Interactive map
Naima Ice-veined
is a
pirate
that used to be located in
the Undermarket
, which she ran alongside
Bort the Bloodless
. She survived the attack on
Lion's Arch
and was temporarily located in a small camp in
Vigilant Hills
, but on returning to Lion's Arch found herself and her comrades ousted from the Undermarket by a group of
skritt
. She then made her home just outside the cave with her fellow pirates on a raised ledge in
White Crane Terrace
.
After the construction of the
New Lion's Arch
, her current residence is
The Free Market
in the
Western Ward
.

Dialogue
For previous dialogue, see
here
.
It's going to take some getting used to, plying our trade out here in the open.
What do you mean?
Our crew had a good thing going before that
sylvari witch
drove us out of our cave. While we were gone, some
mangy skritt
moved in and seized our stash, and we had to start over.
You seem to be doing all right for yourselves now.
Yeah, well, I liked it better when our business belonged to us and we kept all our earnings.
Your business is being taxed now, is that it?
Something like that. Let's just say we're tolerated here only as long as a certain someone gets his cut.
Interesting. I'll leave you to it
I see. Well, good luck
Sorry to hear that
I see. Good luck with that!
In the Memory of Old Lion's Arch
Are you sure you're in the right part of town, cur? You look more out of place than a carbunkle
[
sic
]
on the
queen
's rump. What do you want?
I'm just seeing all that
Lion's Arch
has to offer.
Pah. Nice try, but I hardly think
the Undermarket
is part of the approved tour. I'm sure you're here to see things that aren't on offer in the
Trader's Forum
.
Like what?
On your way up here, you passed
merchants
who specialize in...acquired goods. If they don't have what you want, they can get it for you—if they can find an owner who'd rather part with it than a limb.
What do you and the
lug
do here, then?
Bort and I oversee this little bazaar. We used to run the waves together, stealing from the innocent and foolish. Then we decided to put those skills to use in retail. Better hours and higher profits.
I should just let you get back to your business.
(If member of
Order of Whispers
)
Knowledge of the unknown.
Then you've come to the right place-I'm a purveyor of of the unpurveyed. Pull up a seat, Whisperer.
Anything in Lion's Arch of interest to the Order?
The Order's had us investigating a
hidden cavern
in the southeast. It's said a
great treasure
is there - one that is as old as the city.
Found anything?
No one's been able to get past the cavern's obstacles. Some are even claiming the place is
haunted
, which only makes the Order more interested in what's there.
Maybe I'll have better luck. See you.

PAGE: Nessa
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Nessa
======================================================================

This article is about the karma merchant in Malchor's Leap.  For the NPC offering repairs with the same name, see
Nessa (repair)

======================================================================
PAGE: Order of Shadows
CATEGORIES: Order of Shadows
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Order_of_Shadows
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
The Order of Shadows agents.
“
Shadows plays all sides off each other to keep the status quo churning and exploitable. They advance Joko's cause one day, Sunspears' the next. A very dangerous game.
—
Order of Whispers Mission Dispatches
The
Order of Shadows
is a splinter faction of the
Order of Whispers
. They are stationed primarily within
the Desolation
, with agents working throughout the
Crystal Desert
and within
Vabbi
and
Istan
. The Order of Shadows divides itself into four practices: preventing the situation of
Elona
's politics from getting out of hand, monitoring
the Mouth of Torment
,
preserving the knowledge and history of Elona and
Vabbi
,
and assisting Elonians with external threats like the
Forged
and
ley line
-crazed
creatures
.
History
The
Chantry of Shadows
.
Early history
With both the rise of the
Elder Dragons
and
Palawa Joko
's return, the Order of Whispers began to fracture between the ideals of what the order should focus on. The Order of Shadows broke off from the Order of Whispers, believing Palawa Joko to be a bigger threat than the Elder Dragons. With this splinter faction based in
the Desolation
, the Order of Whispers in
Jahai Bluffs
were cut off from those in
Central Tyria
.
The Order of Shadows primarily focused on Palawa Joko's rule over
Elona
, but also watched over
the Mouth of Torment
due to their location. Their main headquarters became the
Chantry of Shadows
, located in the Desolation overlooking the Mouth of Torment. Despite their open claims of working against Palawa Joko, however, the Order of Shadows did not proactively work to dispel Palawa Joko's reign. They worked from the shadows to keep a status quo in Elona, preventing Joko from becoming too strong, but similarly sacrificing innocents and hindering the remnant
Order of the Sunspears
to prevent instability and to protect their own ability to influence events.
For centuries, the Order of Shadows was more interested in being capable of subtly influencing events than it was about freeing Elonians from the undead king's rule.
Modern developments
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Expand lore with the events from Path of Fire and Season 4.
Ranks
Due to their shared origins with the Order of Whispers, many Order of Shadows ranks are either the same, or parallel, Whispers' ranking:
Recruit: new members, often found in camps.
Agents: most prominent rank, the ones who go out undercover amongst other organizations.
Creators: members in charge of crafting gadgets for agents and forging up plans for the Order.
Keepers: members leading dangerous operations.
Redeemers: the highest rank underneath the Master of Shadows. Currently, the only known Redeemer is
Kossan
.
Master of Shadows
: The leader of the Order of Shadows. His real name is unknown.

Dialogue
Agents at Bounty Boards
All over the region, rogue elements are being driven wild by chaotic
ley energies
. Innocent lives are at stake. If you're willing to help, pick a target from our bounty board.
How do your bounty boards work?
You can only contract for one bounty at a time. Just choose a target from the board, and hurry to catch up with them in the field. Your contract has a time limit.
What if I change my mind about the bounty I've chosen?
You can swap your contract, join someone else's contract, or abandon the target you've chosen. We keep offering new bounties, so be sure to check back here and at other bounty boards.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
How formidable are these bounty targets?
Each presents a challenge worthy of a group, especially the bounties we've termed "legendary". You may need to cross large distances to find your target. Better
mount
up and take along some friends.
Why have you labeled some bounties as "legendary"?
Legendary bounties are particularly dangerous. Only renowned adventurers can contract to take on one of those targets. However, anyone can join a bounty hunt in progress. Good fortune!
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
What do you mean, "driven wild by chaotic ley energies"?
Magical feedback from nearby ley lines seems to be driving some creatures and people into a savage frenzy while also granting them unpredictable, anomalous abilities.
Do you know why this is happening?
We have no idea why or how, but something recently caused a major imbalance in the flow of magic across the region. All we know is that it's put all of us in mortal danger.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I have another question.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.

Qadim
and
Zommoros
are sorting out their differences. You know
djinn
—powerful, temperamental, with a penchant for collecting exotic creatures and then smashing their cages.
I'm not sure I understand.
Me neither. I'm just cleanup. You'd have to ask the authorities at the
Lion's Arch Aerodrome
for more information.

I'll consider taking on one of your bounties.
I have another question.

Agents deployed in the field
Shhh! What are you doing? Keep your voice down! There are
Awakened
all around us.
What are you doing out here?
Keeping an eye on the
Forged
and Awakened. Specifically, we're documenting where they go, and in what numbers. The conflict could shift at any moment, and we won't be caught unprepared.
You probably need to focus, then. Good-bye.
Right. I'll leave you be.
Keep quiet. Speak in whispers, if at all. We don't want any Forged or Awakened to hear us and come to investigate.
What are you doing out here?
Tracking Forged and Awakened movements. If anything about their activity changes, we need to know immediately. For now, they seem happy to just keep fighting each other.
Good. Hope it stays that way.
Noted. I'll try to not draw any attention.

or

======================================================================
PAGE: Order of Whispers
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Order_of_Whispers
======================================================================

“
It doesn't matter where the dragons came from, or why they awakened.
The secrets we discover will help us send them back to hell.
—
Riel Darkwater
The
Order of Whispers
is a secretive ancient
order
with roots in
Elona
. They work behind the scenes to safeguard the world against the rise of the
Elder Dragons
and free and restore the troubled Elona. Its members, often spies, thieves and agents, operate within every
Tyrian
nation, sometimes to the highest levels. Their extensive network is able to gather information from everywhere, even areas blocked to travel, such as Elona which has long since been isolated under
Palawa Joko
's rule.
The Order of Whispers is one of three multi-racial orders which players join as part of their
personal story
. They believe there is no way to physically destroy or negotiate with the Elder Dragons so the only way to counter them is to find a way to send them back to sleep.
Like the other orders, it has a base inside each major city. Prior to the destruction of
Lion's Arch
, the order also had a hidden base in the sewers of the city.
Personal story involvement
Players may join the Order during their
personal story
at the end of the level 30 story mission. Joining the Order of Whispers leads you to work with
Lightbringer Tybalt Leftpaw
.
Armor and weapons
Both armor and weapons can be purchased only by characters in the order.
Whisper's Secret armor
can be purchased from
Armor Requisitioner Betje
in the
Chantry of Secrets
.
Whisper's Secret weapons
can be purchased from
Weapon Requisitioner Illiam
in the Chantry of Secrets.
History
Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
.
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Flesh out more possibly? Add PS involvement
Formed by the elite bodyguard of
Warmarshal Turai
of Elona after the fall of
Palawa Joko
, they were tasked with keeping the undead lord sealed beneath the sands of Elona for eternity; however, it was necessary for them to let him escape so
Warmarshal Varesh
could be defeated.
They played an important part during the event of
Nightfall
in 1075
AE
through aiding
Kormir
's ascension to godhood and helping maintain the Realm of Torment alongside the
Forgotten
.
Since then, Joko has returned and conquered Elona. Despite this, the Order has spread beyond Elonian borders, quietly expanding its ranks. They were the only group other than the
dwarves
to have any knowledge of the
Elder Dragons
prior to their awakening and worked to warn others of the Elder Dragons' awakening though they were ignored.
At some point during Joko's rule, a splinter group of the Order of Whispers known as the
Order of Shadows
formed because of differences in their ultimate goals.
Ranks
The Order of Whispers has several ranks in its organization.
Initiate: new members, often found in camps
Agents
: most prominent rank, the ones who go out undercover amongst other organizations
Lightbringers
: squad leaders and spy masters often in charge of camps
Creators: members in charge of crafting gadgets for agents and forging up plans for the Order
Keepers: members leading dangerous operations
Slayer: members tasked with taking care of dangerous threats to the Order
Preceptors
: the highest rank underneath the Master of Whispers. At the moment, these are
Doern Velazquez
,
Halvora Snapdagger
and
Valenze
.
Master of Whispers
: the leader of the Order of Whispers, whose identity is often unknown to most members. Current leader is
Riel Darkwater
.

Notes
Once a player joins the Order, many NPC around the world will reveal themselves to the player as members of the Order, allowing the player to discuss the Order's operations in the area.

game update, replaced by

The
Order of Whispers Emissary
was a
norn
member of the
Order of Whispers
that appeared in the
Hall of Monuments
in the
Eye of the North
, in a weekly rotation with a
Vigil Emissary
and a
Durmand Priory Emissary
. The
Whispers Emissary Chest
could be found next to the Emissary.

Dialogue
Hello
Commander
. The
Order of Whispers
is interested in any prophet crystals or shards you recover from Strike Missions, especially the red ones.
What will you trade for shards?
What's with the chest?
It's a Priory artifact that reacts to Red Prophet Crystals and contains echoes of treasure from
Strike Missions
. Use one to three green crystals to open it. I recommend three. More is better
Where can I get Red Prophet Crystals?
You can get one per day from Priory Strikes. If you're in a hurry, you can get some shards from Strike Missions to trade up. or exchange other colors with the asura over there.
I want to ask another question.
I have to go.
What about my blue and green shards and crystals?
The
Durmand Priory
is looking for green crystals and the
Vigil
wants blue crystals. The
Emissary of the Priory
should be here next week.
I have another question.
Good-bye.
Just what are these crystals?
The Priory belives they are formed as a side effect of Aurene's travels. Or in this case, her Champion's. They're still reaserching, but we're happy to pay you for finding them.
I wanted to ask another question.
I have to go.
I have to go.

======================================================================
PAGE: Order of the Crystal Bloom
CATEGORIES: Order of the Crystal Bloom
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Order_of_the_Crystal_Bloom
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
,
Living World Season 4
,
The Icebrood Saga
, and
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
.
“
Like Aurene, we focus on healing and restoration. The world needs stability. Order.
—
Caithe
The
Order of the Crystal Bloom
is a newly made faction of people who have come to revere
Aurene
. It consists mostly of
Elonians
who had been saved by Aurene from the
Branded
, including
Sunspears
, the
Awakened
and even the
Zaishen Order
, for whom
Balthazar
's teachings and potential would live on in Aurene.
The Order also has individuals from all across
Central Tyria
who have heard of Aurene's deeds.
History
Living World Season 4
Formed in 1332
AE
in the aftermath of the
battle
of
Thunderhead Keep
, the Order of the Crystal Bloom rallied behind
Caithe
, who had received
Aurene
's
blessing
, and looked up to her as their leader, forcing her into the position.
The members consisted of
Elonians
who had been saved by
Aurene
from the
Branded
, including
Sunspears
, the
Zaishen Order
, and
Awakened
, while also attracting individuals from all across
Central Tyria
who had heard of Aurene's deeds.
The Order worked alongside the
Pact
,
Olmakhan
and
Mist Wardens
to kill
Kralkatorrik
in the
battle
of
Dragonfall
. They witnessed Kralkatorrik's fall and Aurene's
ascension
into the
Elder Dragon
of Light.
The Icebrood Saga
After Aurene had made her new lair in
Eye of the North
in 1333
AE
, members of the Order established a base there, taking it upon them to guard the place.
Crystal Bloom Trainees
of various races could be seen training in the
Hall of Monuments
while waiting for Aurene to call for their assistance. Several months after the awakening of
Jormag
, the Elder Dragon of Ice, another Elder Dragon,
Primordus
, began to stir. His forces,
destroyers
, started attacking settlements around Tyria, and the Order of the Crystal Bloom was one of the first groups to come to aid them.
End of Dragons
In 1335
AE
some of the Order, led by Myrun Skialkin, were allowed by
Empress Ihn
to enter
Cantha
with Caithe to attend to Aurene's exhaustion.
They stayed with Aurene during her time at the
Crystal Cave
and remained there while she left to confront
Soo-Won
. The
Ministry of Security
took this opportunity to imprison the Bloom in
Seitung Prison
, leaving most of the members behind bars until the
Pact Commander
was able to clear their name.
Several Crystal Bloom members appeared with Aurene in
Arborstone
after the events of
Dragon's End
.
During this time, Order member
Leivas
became capable of assisting the Commander in forging
Legendary weapons
to honor Aurene.
Members

Ouissal
(This NPC's appearance may change randomly.)

Sunsmith Ouissal
is a
human
who can be found in the secret
Sunspear
base
, located in the basement level of
The Astralarium
in the
Domain of Istan
. She forged the
legendary
axe
Astralaria
and is involved in the
Astral Purification
achievement.
Speaking to her also progresses the
Ally Assessment
achievement.
Location
Crystal Desert
Domain of Istan
The Astralarium
Istani Sunspear Base
(Talk to the heart vendor after completing the renown heart to enter the restricted library. Head down on the right until you find a book entitled "Nightfall." Interact with the book and click through the dialogue to open a portal to the secret base.)
Dialogue
Ahai. You look like you have a question to ask me.
(If
Note from Yasna
are in your inventory.)
Yasna
sent me here with this message.
(Receive
Yasna's Notes
)
(If you've previously brought her the notes.)
I'd like you to craft a mirror for me.
From these notes, you will need several aspected mirrors. I should be able to craft these for you if you bring me the materials.
What materials are required?
Each mirror will require ten orichalcum ingots, ten powdered rose quartz, five globs of ectoplasm, and three lodestones corresponding to the specific aspect.
I'd like you to craft a mirror for me.

Thanks.

Population Control Device
Location
Critical Blowback
(
Chantry of Secrets
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
The
Population Control Device
is part of the
Chantry of Secrets
's automated security. Normally it sits invisibly, waiting for circumstances to activate it. Its known capabilities include communicating warnings.
Story involvement
Personal story
Chapter 6: The Battle of Claw Island
Critical Blowback
Combat abilities
Behavior
Noncombatant. Despawns on approach.
Notes
The device has no actual model, so it needs no effects to maintain invisibility.

======================================================================
PAGE: Preceptor
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Preceptor
======================================================================

“
Doern, Valenze, and I are the Preceptors. We guide the Order of Whispers.
—
Halvora Snapdagger
The
Preceptors
are leaders of the secret organization the
Order of Whispers
, working for the
Master of Whispers
. They guide the Order on the Master's behalf by passing along his commands to the various agents, in order to keep the identity of the Master of Whispers a secret. Each Preceptor specialises in a specific area of expertise.
The current Preceptors are
Doern Velazquez
,
Halvora Snapdagger
and
Valenze
.

.

,

)
Organization
College of Synergetics
Order of Whispers
Pact
“
You may have already heard my theory about the Elder Dragons consuming magic. Today, I'm here to present incontrovertible proof of that theory. Proof, and a warning. If left unchecked, the dragons will consume every last iota of magic in the world.
—
Professor Gorr
Professor Gorr
is an
asuran
scientist from the
College of Synergetics
who is studying
Elder Dragon
energies
and their consumption of
magic
in
Tyria
. He is the inventor of the
Vacuumagic Polarizer
and author of the revolutionary
Draconic enchanto-consumption theory
.
Biography
Personal story
As Professor of the
College of Synergetics
, Gorr authored the controversial
Draconic enchanto-consumption theory
according to which
magic
was finite and that the
Elder Dragons
' goal was to consume all of it. In 1325
AE
he was arrested by the
Arcane Eye
while having a lecture in
Rata Sum
and was taken to a remote cell.
The
Snaff Savant
,
Zojja
and affiliates from the three
Orders
believed the professor and worked hard to free him, confronting Arcane Eye agents and their leader
Rakt
along the way who wanted to stop them from spreading Gorr's theories further by any means necessary. The group devised a way to let Rata Sum know of the professor's revolutionary discoveries about dragon minions absorbing magic and spread the word while disposing of Rakt as well who made a last ditch effort to stop them.
However, when Gorr confronted
High Councillor Flax
about the theory and his mistreatment by the Arcane Eye, the group learned much to their horror that Flax and the
Arcane Council
had known the validity of Gorr's theory long in advance but had chosen to use Rakt's forces to keep the information a secret from the rest of the
asuran
populace in order to exploit the knowledge for Rata Sum's gain and to promote asuran superiority over the other races. When confronted with accusations of corruption, Flax coldly dismissed the group, letting Gorr leave alive but also mentioning that the group's meddling would work in the Arcane Council's favor in the long run.
The incident with the Arcane Council did not dissuade Gorr from continuing his research, however; instead he worked twice as hard now that he was certain there was more to discover as he aligned himself with the
Order of Whispers
. Further studies on dragon minions revealed more about the Elder Dragons, particularly
Zhaitan
whose
Risen
were causing more and more trouble in
Central Tyria
. To test his theory and figure out a way to combat the dragons, Gorr invented the
Vacuumagic Polarizer
which was a rifle that essentially removed magic from a person or object, poisoned it, and then transfered it back.
The field test on dragon minions with the polarizer was a rousing success, allowing Gorr to finetune his invention for later campaigns as the orders united forces and formed the
Pact
which was led by Marshal
Trahearne
and the
Pact Commander
. Gorr did his part in reclaiming
Claw Island
from the Risen invasion force led by Zhaitan's champion,
Blightghast the Plaguebringer
, and helped locate the
Mouth of Zhaitan
which the Pact forces destroyed to weaken Zhaitan as the Pact pushed into the dragon's domain in the
Ruins of Orr
.
When Trahearne and the Pact Commander came under assault at
the Artesian Waters
while trying to purify the source of Orr which was Zhaitan's main source of magic sustenance, Gorr was one of the potential reinforcements present to assist the pair when they were close to being overwhelmed by retaliating Risen.
Gorr's research helped in part as Zojja designed the
MEGA-LIT cannon
which was used by the airship known as
The Glory of Tyria
to fell Zhaitan in the final battle above the skies of
the Ruined City of Arah
. After the Pact's victory over the Elder Dragon, Gorr attended the festivities at
Fort Trinity
.
Living World Season 2
Gorr traveled to
Camp Resolve
in 1327
AE
and helped the Pact prepare to take the fight to the newest Elder Dragon threat,
Mordremoth
. While in the camp, he briefly chatted with the Pact Commander who was en route to find
Glint
's
egg
. It is not known if the professor later accompanied the Pact Fleet on its flight to the
Heart of Maguuma
to confront Mordremoth.
Living World Season 4
Following the Pact's victory over Mordremoth, Gorr returned to Central Tyria and continued working with the College of Synergetics. He
provided the Pact Commander's contact information
to Synergetics researcher Traskk in 1331
AE
, suggesting that they use the Commander as a candidate for field testing the latest Synergetics invention: the
Infused Crystal
which was an ectoplasmic matrix infused within a vision crystal that allowed its user to view apparitions and
ghostly
entities invisible to the naked eye.
Story involvement

voiced
by

.

What is it you do here?
I oversee our incoming supplies and put them to use upgrading the fort's defenses. We're trying to take advantage of these abandoned fortifications.
How are our supplies doing?
They come via pack bull from the nearest fort in the direction of Camp Resolve. If we lose the forts along our supply route to the enemy, our supplies won't reach us.
Are we doing anything to upgrade our forts' defenses?
Our teams can make several levels of upgrades to our siege weapons and gates, but that varies by location. Once we have the supplies to make an upgrade, it's just a matter of keeping it in repair.

Dialogue
Ahai, Commander! You could really help our cause by inspiring potential recruits, and by finding us some Kralkatite that we can use to forge weaponry for those recruits.
I have some Kralkatite here. What other supplies do you have?
Looks like you're planning something big.
The spearmarshal has been planning to raid Palawadan for weeks, and said we should go on with or without him. Either way, First Spear Hakima says we strike at dusk.
Sounds risky, but I know my way around a battlefield. I'll help if I can.
How has your effort been going?
We took serious losses in the uprising at Champion's Dawn. Many of our people were killed or imprisoned during the loyalist's "liberation" of the village.
Is there any good news?
We've had some success with quick hit-and-run raids of the Mordant Crescent's Great Hall. Grabbing what we need and retreating before they can react.
Impressive. Even the smallest victories can go a long way.
It's just frustrating that we haven't been able to get beneath the hall where Warden Amala has some of our people locked up. She's sealed her prison with powerful magic from the inside.
Hmm, I'll think on that. There has to be a way in...
Speaking of retreating, I should be on my way.
Maybe I'll see what I can do there for your people.
Where is everyone? Are your people up to something elsewhere?
Our forces are attacking Palawadan right now! Our recruits have spirit but they lack training—they could use a good commander.
Thanks. Maybe I'll head over there now.
(If the player has no Kralkatite Ore in inventory)
Can't spare any Kralkatite now, but I'd like to see what supplies you have.
I'm fresh out of Kralkatite right now, but I'll see what I can do to help.

“
I see through your lies. Too often have thieves and trespassers defiled these hallowed grounds.
— Queen Nadijeh
Queen Nadijeh
is a
ghost
from the era of the ancient
Primeval Dynasty
. In her life, she rallied warriors to her cause from all over
Elona
and united them under her rule. She founded the Sunspear Guard order, which later became known as the
Order of the Sunspears
.
She is buried in the
Tomb of the Primeval Kings
, where she guards the portal to
The Mists
. She also recently allied with the ogres from the
Lommuld Kraal
against the
Branded
plaguing the land.

Dialogue

Do you remember me from before?

You are vaguely familiar, but the living look all the same to me. As lifetimes pass by, names and faces become a blur. Don't take it personally.
What makes your rule of Elona significant?

I'll try not to. Good-bye.
What makes your rule of Elona significant?
Significant? Don't you know my name? I was the one who united Elona. I am the reason it became a great nation. It exists because of me.
How did you manage to unite Elona?
Perseverance and the love of my people. Change does not come easy, but some causes are worth making great sacrifices for. In the end, it was worth it.
I don't doubt it. Good-bye.

be good—a visitor! Do you have a question, or did my warm fire draw you here?
A little of both. How old is this camp?
We haven't lived here long, but this camp dates back to the time of the
dwarves
. Back then, it used to be part of an escape route for
Ascalonians
fleeing their homeland.
(If in Order of Whispers)
I see. And where can I learn the unlearnable?
(same as "I'm seeking the unsought.")
Impressive. Thanks for the information.
(If in Order of Whispers)
I'm seeking the unsought.
I know a thing or two about the unknown. There's a rumor a
dwarven treasure
was uncovered inside the
Trionic Lattice
, but no one's gotten close enough to confirm it.
Why not?
Aside from the
snow trolls
prowling the area, the lattice is a natural maze. Most folks who go in never come back out.
I see. Say, how old is Yak's Bend?
(same as "A little of both. How old is this camp?")
I might have better luck.
I'll look into it. Thanks.
I'm just exploring the area. See you.

Recruit Hamdi
is a member of the
Order of Shadows
stationed in the
Chantry of Shadows
.
Location
The Desolation
The Darklands
Dialogue
You must be the commander everyone's talking about. Can I help you with something? Status reports? Latest intel? I'm new, so I'm not very in the know.
Status reports. How's the war going?
There's good news and bad news. We started making progress against Joko, but then the Forged showed up and added a whole new wrinkle to things.
What was happening with Joko's forces?
They started becoming disarrayed. Like their command structure was breaking down. Made it easy to isolate and destroy small detachments.
Any idea what happened to cause their loss of focus?
I'm not privy to the top-level intel, but I heart rumors that Joko himself was MIA, and that's why they were so disorganized.
What else can you tell me?
Thanks for the insight.
Tell me about something else.
Lucky break for you, then.
How's the fight against the Forged going?
Honestly, not well. We still don't know how to fight them effectively. There's only one thing that keeps them from overrunning us completely.
What's that?
They're just not all that interested in fighting us. They'll launch raids to keep us contained, but never an all-out assault.
Why do you think that is?
Their objective is somewhere else. First they were moving north. Now it's more southeast, into Vabbi. Beyond that, I can't say.
What else can you tell me?
Nice talking with you.
Strange.
Save it for later.
If it's not one thing, it's another.
I'd better be going.
