======================================================================
PAGE: Redeemer Kossan
CATEGORIES: Order of Shadows, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Redeemer_Kossan
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
Redeemer Kossan
Race
Human
Locations
Sun's Refuge
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
The Ruination
(
The Desolation
)
Organization
Order of Shadows
Image(s)
Kossan unmasked
“
Elona calls, and my family has a long history of answering that call.
—
Redeemer Kossan
Redeemer Kossan Dejarin
is a descendant of
Koss Dejarin
and
Melonni
, and the right-hand man of
Keeper Pamir
in the
Order of Shadows
. He views his ancestor Koss with reverence and does his best to be the hero
Elona
deserves in its time of need.
Biography
Path of Fire
In his youth, Kossan Dejarin heard tales of his famous ancestors
Koss Dejarin
and
Melonni
, and he grew to idolize them for their bravery and skills. At some point he joined the ranks of the
Order of Shadows
in order to fight against the
Awakened
and free
Elona
from the grip of
Palawa Joko
. He was eventually made the right-hand man of
Keeper Pamir
who, realizing Kossan's charisma and skills, began grooming him to become the hero Elona needed and whom the Order could use to rally people behind.
While stationed at
Torment's Watch
as a Redeemer, Kossan handled operations for the camp, which included scheduling watches and patrols around
the Mouth of Torment
, and kept morale up among the agents. He earned the respect of many of his fellow agents, some of whom became bashful in his presence. According to the agents' tales Kossan had single-handedly broken into
the Bone Palace
and killed a member of the dreaded
Mordant Crescent
.
When
Balthazar
's
Forged
army invaded Elona in 1330
AE
and began using the Mouth of Torment as their staging ground, the Order of Shadows observed their progress and witnessed clashes between the Forged and the Awakened. Kossan frequently investigated strange disturbances near the
Chantry of Shadows
, and in his patrols he experienced the cunning and strength of the Forged who proved to be formidable adversaries. He put down a tenacious
Forged officer
, who had been ambushing agents, as well as
"Scraps"
, which had been sabotaging the Order's surveillance devices.
Kossan ran into the
Pact Commander
during these excursions, and the two exchanged ideas while Kossan informed the Commander about his lineage with Koss and Melonni and how significant those heroes of the past had been to Elona. He eventually learned of the Commander's success in
stopping
Balthazar in the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
.
Living World Season 4
Following the
death
of Palawa Joko in the
Battle of Gandara
in 1331
AE
, Kossan accompanied
Shadows Agent Kito
and other delegates from the Order of Shadows to
attend a summit
at the
Fortress of Jahai
where they witnessed the Commander's
dragon
ally
Aurene
landing there much to some of the delegates' shock. Although Kossan was receptive to the treaty by
Spearmarshal Zaeim
who had been gathering various factions in the fortress to decide on a post-Joko ruling council for Elona, the attempted coup by the
Mordant Crescent
's
Archon Iberu
nearly ended the summit if not for the timely intervention by an Awakened Koss Dejarin who killed the archon.
The summit was interrupted again, however, when the
Death-Branded Shatterer
assaulted the fortress with its
Branded
minions on behalf of the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
and forced the Commander and the delegates to flee into the courtyard before it gave chase to Aurene. Kossan finally got to talk with Koss in the aftermath, revealing that they were related and asking to speak with him in private.
After the
Order of the Sunspears
decided to move survivors of the attacks and local refugees to a safer location to withstand Branded incursions, Kossan
accompanied
Koss, the Commander and
Braham Eirsson
to the old Sunspear base in
Jahai Bluffs
. The group divided into two pairs and began a friendly competition to clear the cave of the spiders and the
Colossal Broodmother
, which had been dwelling there, and lighting protective braziers before Koss named the reclaimed base as
Sun's Refuge
.
Shortly after clearing the cave, the group met with the Commander's other allies who arrived there, only to witness another Branded invasion from
rifts
which suddenly appeared. Kossan stayed with Aurene to protect her along with other allies while the Commander took a small group to fight the Branded head-on. After the invasion had been thwarted, Kossan witnessed the Commander return to Aurene with the
ghosts
of
Eir Stegalkin
and
Snaff
who had come with great urgency: Kralkatorrik was devouring
magic
in
the Mists
, and Aurene needed to confront the Elder Dragon before it would become too powerful to face. Aurene shared visions of multiple futures, however, in which she died no matter which allies she brought with her for the confrontation with Kralkatorrik, and fled while leaving the Commander and the allies behind to worry about the future.
To stop panic from spreading, the Commander's ally
Blish
pointed out that he and the Commander already had a plan to attach a tracker to Kralkatorrik via a magical lure so they could monitor the Elder Dragon's movements and evacuate settlements before the Branded could invade populated areas. Sometime later, Kossan witnessed the Commander and Blish
returning
to Sun's Refuge with
Balthazar's greatsword
in tow and restoring its lost flame with the assistance of
Rytlock Brimstone
's
Sohothin
. Upon learning that the Commander and Blish were to depart to the Mists to attach the tracker, Kossan wished them luck and reminded them that the fate of Elona depended on their success. He later learned of Blish's sacrifice to ensure the tracker would function despite a faulty battery.
Kossan spoke with Koss whenever he could in Sun's Refuge, only to see Koss leaving temporarily upon the latter learning that
Tahlkora
, an old friend from the past, had been Awakened. While waiting for Koss to return, Kossan conversed with Zaeim, and the two men came to respect one another although Zaeim was amused by Kossan's enthusiasm about Koss. When Koss returned to Sun's Refuge, Kossan found himself unable to ask him all the questions he had been thinking of. However, Koss sensed the younger man's uncertainty and answered the most important question the agent had had in mind: he was proud of Kossan but also reminded him that the agent's combat stances and shouts still needed some polishing.
Location
Crystal Desert
Jahai Bluffs
Sun's Refuge
(as Kossan Dejarin)
The Desolation
The Ruination
Story involvement

A Star to Guide Us

:
Ashhe, the Rune of Valor
— Embolden the descendant of two famous Sunspears on a
dangerous patrol
in the Desolation to acquire this relic.
Trivia
Kossan's line "Screw your courage to the sticking place" is an homage to the same line said by Lady Macbeth in Act 1 Scene 7 of the play
"Macbeth"
by William Shakespeare. It is also a reference to the
Guild Wars Nightfall
hero
Melonni
, from whom he is descended.

======================================================================
PAGE: Remains of the Last Spearmarshal
CATEGORIES: Order of the Sunspears
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Remains_of_the_Last_Spearmarshal
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
Remains of the Last Spearmarshal
Location
Yahnur Plateau
(
Domain of Vabbi
)
Type
Awakened
Interact
Yes
Destructible
No
Other images
Interactive map
“
I've been many things. A monk and a Sunspear. A historian and a teacher. An activist and an agitator. Even a princess. Now, I am a shepherd of sorts, with an extraordinary flock.
— Remains of the Last Spearmarshal
The
Remains of the Last Spearmarshal
is the undead corpse of
Tahlkora
, the last
Spearmarshal
of the
Order of the Sunspears
, who once fought alongside
Kormir
and other
heroes
against the fallen
god
Abaddon
.
Palawa Joko
has reanimated her, but only partially, preventing her from becoming fully
Awakened
and trapping her soul within her unmoving corpse on a
remote plateau
in the
Domain of Vabbi
. Despite her cursed existence, Tahlkora continues to watch over the Sunspears through the eyes of local
griffons
and hopes to see
Elona
freed from Joko's yoke one day. She is the author of
The Writings of the Last Spearmarshal
.
Biography
Early years
Princess of Vabbi
Tahlkora was born as the daughter of
Prince Mehtu the Wise
in the
Elonian
province of
Vabbi
in 1057 or 1058
AE
. As a child she often visited her father in the great library in the
Halls of Chokhin
where he entertained her with inspiring stories about the
Order of the Sunspears
and their role as protectors of Elona. Tahlkora loved these stories, especially the
Istani
folk tales, and she studied them diligently. She dreamed of being just like the heroes she read about, but her father wanted to keep her by his side as a princess, so when she was 18, she ran away from home to meet the Sunspears she had come to romanticise. Fearing that they would send her back to Vabbi if they learned she was Prince Mehtu's daughter, she kept her identity hidden, and pretended that she was just a
priestess
of
Lyssa
.
While staying in Istan, Tahlkora
overheard
the
Kournan
leader
Warmarshal Varesh Ossa
plotting with
one of her generals
to free the fallen
god
Abaddon
from the
Realm of Torment
. After a difficult time
convincing
the Sunspears that Varesh was responsible for this threat, Tahlkora joined them in their efforts to prevent it only to witness the Sunspears
walking straight into a trap
that Varesh had set as well as
Spearmarshal
Kormir
being assaulted by Abaddon's
demonic
minion called
the Hunger
which blinded the Spearmarshal.
Following the surviving Sunspears' retreat and being pursued by Kournan forces, Tahlkora gained an appreciation for how difficult and dangerous real adventures were, and steadily grew from a naïve girl into a true hero. She developed feelings for
Koss Dejarin
, who had taken an interest in mentoring her, but
stood aside in the end
to let him and
Melonni
pursue their relationship.
Eventually, the Sunspears' retreat to Vabbi forced Tahlkora to face her father, who hoped that she would return home with him. However, she stood up to him and told him that she would continue traveling with the Sunspears. The death of her friend
Kehanni
soon afterwards only strengthened her resolve. Tahlkora witnessed the
troublesome deal
the Sunspears made with the released
lich
Palawa Joko
to learn how to safely cross
the Desolation
in pursuit of Varesh before the Warmarshal could open a gate to Abaddon.
After regrouping, gaining allies, and helping Joko take control of
the Bone Palace
and rebellious
Awakened
, the Sunspears successfully crossed the Desolation and
cornered
Varesh at the
Mouth of Torment
. Although she failed to finish the ritual properly before perishing, the Kournan Warmarshal managed to weaken the barriers between the realms enough to bring about
Nightfall
and opened a gateway into the Realm of Torment. Realizing that the fate of the entire
world
was at stake, Tahlkora followed the Sunspears and a group of
heroes
to confront Abaddon, the God of Secrets, directly. After Abaddon's
defeat
and the
Ascension of Kormir
as the Goddess of Truth in 1075
AE
, Tahlkora decided to carry on adventuring and see more of the world.
The Last Spearmarshal
After a great many adventures, Tahlkora settled into her homeland and became a librarian as well as the Spearmarshal of the Sunspears. As decades passed, she witnessed Palawa Joko's conquest of Elona and the decline of the Sunspears while the Awakened and the
Mordant Crescent
, turncoat Sunspears, rose to prominence under Joko's rule. Joko turned Elonians against one another, burning books and rewriting the nation's history in his image, defiling the legacy of the Sunspears and crushing anyone daring to stand against him.
Tahlkora continued rebelling in her own way despite her advanced age. She spoke against Joko and eventually had her tongue taken as Joko did not wish to turn her into a martyr and instead tried to make an example of her to demoralize her followers. When Tahlkora could no longer speak, she wrote volumes instead, which would be titled
The Writings of the Last Spearmarshal
. She spread these writings to remind Elonians of their history and to prevent her countrymen from falling into apathy. Joko took Tahlkora's eyes next, but the Last Spearmarshal continued defying the lich king by gathering disciples around her and spreading her teachings through them. Despite her followers urging her to flee, Tahlkora chose to stay as an act of defiance against Joko despite knowing what the cost may be.
Joko ultimately had Tahlkora brought before him and took her life, intending to Awaken her as one of his thralls and use her to undo the damage she had caused. However, much to both the lich king and the Last Spearmarshal's surprise, Tahlkora's will remained her own even after Awakening. In response Joko took back all but the smallest spark of Awakening in Tahlkora and had her deposited on the remote
Yahnur Plateau
, condemning her to languish there as an unmoving corpse for centuries.
Tahlkora came to believe that both her free will and her ability to communicate with her mind after Awakening were gifts from Kormir herself. The Last Spearmarshal honed her ability over the following centuries and learned to communicate with local
griffons
and see through their eyes while waiting for the Sunspears to rise again.
Path of Fire
In 1330
AE
, Tahlkora met with the
Pact Commander
who had been prompted to visit the Yahnur Plateau after finding evidence of the existence of griffons in the area. Choosing to remain anonymous for a time, the Last Spearmarshal revealed that she had been watching the Commander through the eyes of her griffons. She urged the Commander to aid the scattered Sunspears in their time of need and rally them to the plateau, recover lost Sunspear artifacts and supplies, as well as find and retrieve the lost eggs of the griffon broodmothers. After successfully completing the Last Spearmarshal's tasks, the Commander brought the few remaining Sunspears together; the group included
First Spear Kitur
,
Master Sergeant Shadi
,
Second Spear Nayrim
, and
Second Spear Olishar
. The Commander also learned to bond with the griffons, setting the stage for reviving the Sunspears as griffon riders who would be guided by Tahlkora's wisdom.
The Sunspears
came under attack
soon after their reunion, however, as a
Mordant Crescent Hierarch
discovered the plateau and intended to kill them all there to end their rebellion before it could take root. Thanks to the assistance provided by the Commander and the griffons, the Sunspears were able to defeat the Hierarch and ensure that their Sunspear Sanctuary would remain hidden from Joko's enforcers. Tahlkora thanked the Commander and rewarded them with a griffon mount of their own. She revealed her name to the Commander and explained the reason for being the way she was. The Last Spearmarshal also promised to watch over both the mount and the Commander as well as revive the Order of the Sunspears so they could free Elona from Joko's tyranny one day.
Living World Season 4
While the Vabbian Sunspears built the Griffon Sanctuary under Tahlkora's guidance, a new Spearmarshal named
Zaeim
, former disciple of
First Spear Kitur
, had risen to prominence in the
Domain of Istan
and taken Tahlkora's title to carry on her work in his own way. Zaeim built a new order of Sunspears under his leadership and sparked a
rebellion
that would inspire the common folk of the
Isle of Istan
to take a stand against Joko's oppressive regime.
After she had used her griffons to watch Zaeim's deeds in the war against Joko and his attempts to recruit Sunspears throughout Elona, Tahlkora sent the Vabbian Sunspears to
Sun's Refuge
, Zaeim's latest base of operations, in
Jahai Bluffs
to help in the fight against the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
following Joko's
demise
in 1331
AE
. She sent word via the Sunspears that she had been keeping an eye on Zaeim and saw him as her worthy successor as Spearmarshal. Some time after Zaeim had received the message, the now Awakened Koss Dejarin visited Tahlkora to learn what had happened to her. What the pair talked about is not known, however, as Koss did not want to discuss the specifics of their conversation upon returning to Sun's Refuge.
Location
Crystal Desert
Domain of Vabbi
Yahnur Plateau
Sunspear Sanctuary
Story involvement

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Riel Darkwater
Race
Human
Rank
Champion
Location
Chantry of Secrets
(
Kryta
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Pact
Tyrian Alliance
Image(s)
Riel with hood equipped.
Riel as seen in a cutscene.
“
Our order will be watching you closely.
—
Riel Darkwater
Lightbringer
Riel Darkwater
is a member of the
Order of Whispers
. She is the Order's primary spy in
Lion's Arch
and their most accomplished sniper.
Riel works under
Commodore
Lawson Marriner
, and in exchange for the details of
Captain's Council
meetings she keeps the Commodore informed on the criminal underworld of the city.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
.
In reality, Riel is the current
Master of Whispers
. Her role is a closely-guarded secret, known only to the
Preceptors
, a few of the Order's
agents
, and
Trahearne
. Her cover story is that she is an attaché to the Preceptors, and the lead Whispers agent stationed in Lion's Arch.
Biography
Early years
Riel Darkwater served in the
Order of Whispers
and was eventually promoted to the rank of
Lightbringer
, becoming the Order's most accomplished sniper.
She eventually came in contact with the
sylvari
Trahearne
, the foremost expert on
Elder Dragons
. At some point she became the attaché of
Commodore
Lawson Marriner
, the leader of the
Ship's Council
, and worked directly under the three
Preceptors
of the Order. She kept her identity as a Whispers member a secret from the commodore, however, which put her in the perfect place to spy on and manipulate the leaders of
Lion's Arch
.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to the
personal story
.
When the previous
Master of Whispers
had fallen to the
corruption
of the Elder Dragons and became a threat, Trahearne helped Riel put him down. After the deed had been done, Riel succeeded her predecessor as the next Master of Whispers but acted as if she was working under the Preceptors, keeping her true role a secret from most of the lower-ranked Whispers
agents
and outsiders. The only ones who knew her true identity as the new Master of Whispers were the Preceptors, a few select agents, and Trahearne. She had also learned a secret only known by each Master of Whispers who would pass the information on to their successor: the Master who had originally commissioned the
Chantry of Secrets
to be built had planted explosives in the island's foundation as a safeguard if the base ever came under attack. Because Riel was aware of this secret, she had likely already been chosen as the Master's successor before the latter got corrupted.
Personal story
Order Neophyte
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information
The Battle of Claw Island
Section-stub. This key section of the article is incomplete. You can help the Guild Wars 2 Wiki by
expanding it
.
Reason:
Add missing information
Victory or Death
After Zhaitan had been
slain
, Riel, acting the part of an ordinary agent, attended the subsequent victory celebration at
Fort Trinity
which had been acting as the
Pact
's base of operations during the invasion of
Orr
. In a conversation with
General Almorra Soulkeeper
and
Steward Gixx
, Riel admitted that the Commander and Trahearne had made the victory possible by uniting the factions when previously the three orders had only ever been occasionally thinking about it. Almorra, annoyed by Riel and Gixx being so formal and only talking about work, told them to shut up and start having fun while the celebration lasted. Upon being reunited with a Whispers-aligned Commander, Riel pointed out that they still had four Elder Dragons to deal with after Zhaitan, seemingly being unaware of the existence of a sixth Elder Dragon at the time. She indicated that the Elder Dragons had become the Order of Whispers's primary goal and stated that the loud celebration helped conceal the preparations her agents were making for the next Elder Dragon confrontation.
Janthir Wilds
Act 1
In 1337
AE
, Riel attended the
second meeting
of the
Tyrian Alliance
at the
Guild Initiative Headquarters
as one of the
Pact
leadership's representatives alongside the Priory and the Vigil after they and various other factions' representatives had received an urgent summons to discuss a matter relating to a new threat emerging in
Janthir
. Upon reuniting with the Pact Commander, Riel accused them of having been aware of the
Astral Ward
's existence and not informing her about it although she accepted the Commander's explanation on why they had kept the ward's actions a secret until now. She used the opportunity talk to the ward's representative
Frode
who would put her in contact with the ward's specialists for further questioning. After listening to the ensuing conversation with the Tyrian Alliance's founders, Riel learned about the return of the
titans
and observed the founders' vote on whether to use the
Scepter of Orr
against the titans or not after
Livia
had revealed her identity and her history with the Scepter to everyone present.
Absolution
After the titan threat had been dealt with, Riel participated in the
victory celebration
held in
Lion's Arch
in 1338
AE
. Upon meeting with the Commander, Riel kept up the act of being just an ordinary agent only interested in gossip if the Commander was not a member of the Order of Whispers. However, a Whispers-aligned Commander learned that it was just an act to put some distance between the two of them to keep Riel's masquerade going. She also pointed out how pleased she was that the Whispers-aligned Commander had not forgotten all the tricks of spycraft and that the Commander should return to the Chantry one day for a thorough debriefing.

steam creatures

Pinnacle Enclave
in
the Godspurs
.
Location
Shiverpeak Mountains
Lornar's Pass
The Godspurs
Dialogue
If you have no business with me, please be on your way.
(If in Order of Whispers)
I mean to surpass the unsurpassed.
Shh, lower your voice, you imbecile! Oh, and restore the unrestored. Heading to the Priory for spy work? Or, no, you're an explorer of some sort, aren't you?
Yes. Any advice?
My advice? Be careful. These steam creatures are going to cause more trouble than most expect.
Have you learned much about them?
Very little, actually. Studying them is difficult. I do theorize that while they are covered in metal, their core is organic. And I believe they have a purpose here. What it is, I'm not sure yet.
I'll be careful.
Right. Okay.

PAGE: Shadows Agent Kito
CATEGORIES: Order of Shadows, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Shadows_Agent_Kito
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Guild Wars 2: Path of Fire
and
Living World Season 4
.
Shadows Agent Kito
Race
Human
Level
80
Rank
Elite
Locations
Elona Reach
(
Crystal Oasis
)
The Bonestrand
(
The Desolation
)
Reclaimed Chantry
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
The Grotto
(
Thunderhead Peaks
)
Organization
Order of Shadows
Services
Renown Heart
Part of
Start dialogs between the Elonian factions
Shadows Agent Kito
is a member of the
Order of Shadows
who volunteered to be stationed at the
Temple of Kormir
. He helps the
Pact Commander
with the pursuit of
Balthazar
and later assists in the battle against
Kralkatorrik
.
Biography
Early years
Kito's origins remain as mysterious as he is except that he eventually became an agent of the
Order of Shadows
. His discreet missions led him to many locations in the desert, making even his fellow Shadows agents unsure what he was up to at any given time.
Path of Fire
Act 1
When the rogue
god
Balthazar
's army of
Forged
invaded the
Crystal Desert
in 1330
AE
, Kito was observing events in the
Temple of Kormir
where he sought out the
Pact Commander
who was accompanied by
Dragon's Watch
members
Canach
,
Kasmeer Meade
and
Rytlock Brimstone
. Kito
revealed
his affiliation with the Order of Shadows to the party and hinted at Shadows origins as an offshoot from the
Order of Whispers
. He also claimed to know more about the Commander than just their title as well as the fact that the Commander was looking for the
dragon
Vlast
whom Balthazar had set his sights on. The meeting was interrupted by the arrival of the
Herald of Balthazar
, who had come to conscript more souls to the Forged army as well as persuade the Commander to join Balthazar in the campaign against the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. The Commander stopped the Herald and forced her to flee with Kito's help, however.
Kito pledged the Order of Shadows to the Commander's cause and told the party to meet him at
Makali Outpost
which was being assaulted by the Forged. Once the party had gotten there, Kito
evacuated
villagers from the war zone, but paused long enough to tell the party that Balthazar himself was nearby, closing in on Vlast.
Act 3
After saving whom he could from the outpost and learning about Vlast's sacrifice causing Balthazar to leave, Kito followed the Commander's trail to the
Elon Riverlands
. The agent learned that Balthazar had the Commander watched but did not intervene in time. He could only watch from the shadows as Balthazar not only
ambushed
and slew the Commander but also kidnapped the dragon
Aurene
, Vlast's sister, who had hurried to the Commander's aid.
Believing the Commander to be gone forever, Kito traveled to
the Bonestrand
where he observed the
Awakened
and locals for some time. However, he was surprised to be
reunited
with the Commander who had somehow returned from the dead. Although Kito tried to inquire how the Commander had done the impossible, the Commander promised to tell him all about the supernatural experiences later in exchange for his continued help. Intrigued, the agent agreed and listened to the Commander's plan to deceive the missing
Palawa Joko
's Awakened army to stop the advance of the Forged before Balthazar could get to Kralkatorrik and disrupt the magical
balance
of the
world
.
Although skeptical at first, Kito soon realized the merits of the Commander disguising themselves as
Archon Iberu
to fool the Awakened marshals and gave his blessing to the plan to lure out the archon so he could be dealt with before the Commander began the ruse. After the Commander's departure, Kito stayed out of sight and did not participate in the
Battle of Kodash Bazaar
during which the Commander successfully
ended
Balthazar's life at the cost of empowering both Kralkatorrik and Aurene.
The agent eventually showed up at the
victory celebration
held in the Commander's honor in Amnoon. He congratulated the Commander and promised that the paths of the Order of Shadows and the Commander would cross again in the future. The festivities were ended abruptly by a sudden
Brandstorm
hitting the city, however.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
Following the initial Brandstorm hitting Amnoon, Kito arranged a farm on the outskirts of the city to act as a low-profile meeting place for Dragon's Watch to keep them safe from Joko's agents who were none too pleased about the Pact Commander having commanded the Awakened army in the Battle of Kodash. Another Brandstorm hit the city soon after and brought with it Kralkatorrik's invading
Branded
horde, however, forcing Dragon's Watch and local defenders to face the dragon minions and save the civilians they could. Kito showed up soon after as well and assisted in the
defense of the city
.
After the battle, Kito and Dragon's Watch briefly encountered the Commander's previously missing dragon ally Aurene who gave them a troubling vision about a
Mysterious Figure
leading an Awakened army to invade
Central Tyria
from an unknown city. Although Kito claimed to not know about the rest of the vision, he identified the seen location as
Fahranur
, an ancient city of the fallen
Primeval Dynasty
located on the
Isle of Istan
. He explained that the Istani branch of the
Order of the Sunspears
, who hid in
the Astralarium
, should know a way into the city. Kito booked an undercover voyage to the
Domain of Istan
for the Commander to avoid alerting Joko's minions while he decided to travel on a different boat as a safety measure.
At an unknown point in time before the Commander reached the
Istani Sunspear Base
, Kito had met with
Spearmarshal Zaeim
, the leader of the local Sunspears, as well as several priests of
Kormir
and astronomers who were sympathetic to the Istani rebellion. While talking with these contacts, Kito spoke highly of the Commander and even let them believe that he had played an integral part in the Commander's campaign against Balthazar and the Elder Dragons
Zhaitan
and
Mordremoth
.
When the Commander sought out the
corsair
Sayida the Sly
to have her release Spearmarshal Zaeim whom
the Sly Crew
had captured, Kito observed the pair's exchange in the shadows aboard
The Dark Leviathan
. When it turned out that Sayida had already delivered Zaeim to the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
to collect a bounty for capturing him, Kito made himself visible and helped the Commander
persuade the corsair captain
to help spring Zaeim free from the prison in exchange for the Sly coin which Sayida was after.
Sayida was annoyed by Kito's sporadic appearances and disappearances on her ship, however. Once the heavily intoxicating grog, which was needed to drug the Awakened guards for the prison break, had been brewed from the ingredients the Commander had gathered, the corsair captain made it clear to kill Kito if the Shadows agent dared appear on her ship unannounced again. Before leaving, Kito promised to be stationed outside the great hall with the cavalry should the Commander need their assistance.
Although the cavalry consisting of Sunspears, Dragon's Watch members
Canach
and
Rytlock Brimstone
and the occasionally helping Aurene arrived to aid the Commander, Zaeim and the Sly crew, Kito himself was absent. The Shadows agent also did not show up later to accompany the Commander's party to Fahranur; however, he learned that the team had successfully
rescued
their ally
Taimi
from the returned Palawa Joko and shut down Awakened operations in a fallen
Inquest
facility deep within Fahranur.
A Bug in the System
As Dragon's Watch began investigating Joko's plans to invade Central Tyria and how to find the Inquest gate hub which had connected to Fahranur before the shutdown, Kito discovered another hidden Inquest facility in the
Desert Highlands
. In 1331
AE
, he
summoned
the Commander,
Braham Eirsson
and
Rox
to the
Lifeblood Ravine
where he suggested the trio ambush an upcoming Inquest prison caravan and disguise themselves to get inside. Before leaving, Kito pointed out that the Order of Shadows had spotted Joko's agents digging up remains that dated back to the Primeval Dynasty, and the Commander promised to inform the agent if the team found out anything about the lich king's most recent course of action.
Long Live the Lich
After the Commander had
discovered
that Joko had successfully acquired the Inquest's scarab samples from the gate hub of
Rata Primus
to breed a new generation of
Scarab Plague
which the
lich
king intended to unleash on his enemies in Elona and Central Tyria, an allied force joined the Commander in the
Domain of Kourna
where they besieged
Gandara, the Moon Fortress
. Due to not being a front line fighter, Kito was absent from the battle and eventually learned of Joko's
demise
and the end of the Scarab Plague threat.
A Star to Guide Us
Realizing that the Awakened had been freed from Joko's control upon the lich king's demise and that Elona needed a post-Joko governing council or the land would fall into anarchy, Spearmarshal Zaeim sent invitations to various parties throughout Elona to join him on a summit at the
Fortress of Jahai
. Kito,
Redeemer Kossan
and several Order of Shadows members traveled to
Jahai Bluffs
although Kito chose to stay at the
Reclaimed Chantry
, a ruined base of the original Elonian
Order of Whispers
, while Kossan participated in the talks. Once a
sudden Branded invasion
persuaded the bickering delegates to sign Zaeim's treaty, Kito focused on starting dialog between the various parties to make them work together against the common Branded enemy and was assisted by the Commander.
All or Nothing
In 1332
AE
, Kito accompanied the combined forces of the
Pact
and the Elonian alliance to
Thunderhead Keep
in
Deldrimor Front
to assist the Commander in the battle against Kralkatorrik and end the Elder Dragon's reign of terror once and for all. As the allied forces prepared for battle, the agent observed their training and assessed their odds before joining a briefing held by the Commander and fighting against the Branded which swarmed the keep upon Kralkatorrik's arrival. Despite the Pact's
efforts
, however, the wounded Kralkatorrik managed to decimate much of
the Auditorium
and Brand several soldiers in it, kill Aurene and flee back to
the Mists
, leaving the Commander and surviving allies to mourn the losses.
Following Aurene's
revival
via the use of the lich magic she had absorbed from consuming Joko, many of the Commander's surviving allies joined her and the Commander in the second battle against Kralkatorrik on
Dragonfall
. Kito was not seen among the allied forces, however.

Kito.
Gallery
Kito in the trailer for
A Bug in the System
.
Trivia
Kito is
voiced
by
Jason Spisak
.
He wields the
Cursed Flintlock
, a
rifle
associated with the
deadeye
thief
elite specialization
.
Kito started as a generic unnamed
NPC
before developing into one of the main characters for Path of Fire story. He was designed by
Matthew Medina
who also wrote a backstory for him.
Kito means 'gem' and 'precious stone' in
Swahili
. The name ties into Kito's so far unexplored backstory.
Matthew Medina's planned backstory for Kito involved him having been an Elonian noble from a wealthy southern family who fell in love with the woman whom his family had arranged him to marry. However, the bride-to-be plotted to assassinate Kito on the wedding night due to her family wishing to gain the wealth of Kito's family. Kito was saved by the Order of Shadows who killed the bride and abducted him into their ranks. Kito became a reluctant member of the Order of Shadows but stayed with them as he had nowhere else to go after the incident.
See also
Associated items
Kito's Secret Cache
Mini Shadows Agent Kito

======================================================================
PAGE: Spearmarshal Zaeim
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Order of the Crystal Bloom, Order of the Sunspears, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Spearmarshal_Zaeim
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
,
Guild Wars 2: End of Dragons
, and
Guild Wars 2: Janthir Wilds
.
Spearmarshal Zaeim
Race
Human
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Allied Encampment
(
Domain of Kourna
)
Sun's Refuge
(
Jahai Bluffs
)
Crystal Bloom Camp
(
Dragonfall
)
Organization
Order of the Sunspears
Order of the Crystal Bloom
Tyrian Alliance
“
The Hero of Istan has shown us that good people can't sit idly by while evil ones threaten the innocent. He's young, but he's everything a Sunspear should be: a champion of the oppressed, who knows peace without justice is surrender.
—
Priest Sazeer
Spearmarshal Zaeim
, known as the Hero of
Istan
, is the current
Spearmarshal
of the
Order of the Sunspears
.
Biography
Early years
Zaeim was born in the
Domain of Istan
and was trained as a
Sunspear
by
First Spear Kitur
on the
Elonian
mainland. However, when the rogue
god
Balthazar
's
Forged
army assaulted King
Palawa Joko
's lands in 1330
AE
, Istan suffered from the
Awakened
regime which became more tyrannical and demanding in response to supply the mainland's troops who were defending Elonian settlements from the Forged. Zaeim learned of Istan's plight and, against his mentor's protests, returned home to aid his fellow Istani. He vowed to bring the Sunspears out of the shadows as a symbol of hope to those oppressed by Joko.
The first person Zaeim recruited was
First Spear Hakima
, and many others followed as they conducted their operations from their
hidden headquarters
below the archives of the
Astralarium
. The Istani Sunspears eventually convinced Zaeim to take the title of
Spearmarshal
, leader of the Sunspears. He was the only person among the Istani Sunspears to know a way into
Fahranur, the First City
, and guarded that secret with his life.
When the nobles of
Palawadan, Jewel of Istan
decided to keep food shipped from the mainland to themselves, people on the countryside objected. The
Temple of Joko
responded with claims that the people outside Palawadan's walls were disloyal to the regime and thus did not deserve food.
Issnur Isles
had enough and declared their independence from Istan, and the
loyalists
to Joko's regime traveled there to make an example out of the dissidents. Zaeim had taught his Sunspear recruits guerrilla tactics, however, and they crushed the loyalist incursion.
In response to the Sunspears' actions, the
Mordant Crescent
sent fifty Awakened to find the Spearmarshal and end his rebellion. Although the exact details of how that encounter went are unknown, two facts remain undisputed: Zaeim lived, and the Awakened sent after him lay dead. People of Istan began calling him the Hero of Istan, galvanizing the whole island behind him.
As a result of Zaeim's victory, the Mordant Crescent imposed martial law everywhere on the island, conscripting the living and Awakening the dead. With supply lines broken and families being recruited, it became harder for the Spearmarshal and his Sunspears to find more recruits for their cause.
Living World Season 4
Daybreak
Albeit weakened at first because of the Forged attacks causing many Awakened casualties in Joko's absence, the Awakened suddenly reorganized and retaliated in Istan with boosted numbers, taking back settlements such as
Champion's Dawn
which the Sunspears had previously liberated. Zaeim had been planning for weeks to raid Palawadan, but decided to ride south to reclaim Champion's Dawn from the Awakened after learning the news. Before leaving, he left the raid on Palawadan in the capable hands of Hakima. He also came to learn of the
Pact Commander
's deeds against Balthazar and the
Elder Dragons
Zhaitan
and
Mordremoth
thanks to being informed by
Shadows Agent Kito
sometime earlier. However, Kito had also made him falsely believe that Kito had had an important part to play in the deaths of Balthazar and the two Elder Dragons.
Zaeim was ambushed by
Sayida the Sly
and her
corsairs
, however, and was carted off to be sold to the Mordant Crescent who then took the captured Spearmarshal to the jail in the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
.
While waiting for his punishment there, Zaeim witnessed a new prisoner being taken in. After chatting with this prisoner, the Spearmarshal introduced himself and learned the prisoner's identity as the Pact Commander who had come looking for him to find a way into Fahranur. Although the Commander claimed to have come to rescue him, Zaeim remained skeptical that the Commander could find a way out, only to be surprised by the Commander's resourcefulness.
After they were out of their cells, the pair found their confiscated armor and weapons and headed out, only to be confronted by
Warden Amala
. They
fought the warden
with the help of Sayida's corsairs whom the Commander had contracted for the mission. After defeating Amala, the group were greeted with reinforcements consisting of Sunspears,
Dragon's Watch
as well as the
dragon
Aurene
who helped clear out the remaining guards. In gratitude and after learning that Joko had seemingly returned and kidnapped the Commander's friend
Taimi
in Fahranur, Zaeim instructed the Commander to meet him at the old docks of
Issnur Bay
. He also told the Commander not to take Sayida with them as he did not trust the corsairs. He then left to prepare the Sunspears to reclaim Champion's Dawn as he had originally planned.
Having finished with Champion's Dawn, Zaeim traveled to the old docks and urged the Commander to go ahead while he would look for Dragon's Watch members
Canach
and
Rytlock Brimstone
. The trio eventually caught up with the Commander and
braved the traps
of the First City and the Awakened lurking within it. As they hurried on, Zaeim explained the city's history, including its fall to the dreaded
Scarab Plague
and
Kormir
's
involvement
in waking up the fallen god
Abaddon
centuries ago.
Much to the party's surprise, they discovered not only
Branded
battling Awakened in the fallen halls but
asuran
technology as well as corpses of
Inquest
agents within the city despite Zaeim's belief that Joko had expelled the asura from Elona decades ago. Even more surprisingly the asuran corpses rose as Awakened and attacked the party with an enraged Awakened abomination joining the fray as well. The bombs Canach had set up to clear an obstacle nearly buried the party if not for the timely intervention of
Rox
and
Braham
whose protective bubble kept them all from harm.
After brief introductions from everyone present, Braham and Rox joined the party in their search for Taimi, only to find the young asura trapped deep within the complex while an army of Awakened led by a
mysterious figure
exited through the many
asura gates
found within. While the Commander and Braham hurried to help Taimi, the rest of the party stayed back to fend off attacking Awakened who were trying to reach the pair.
Once the party had saved Taimi from a tampered
Scruffy 2.0
, they learned that the Awakened had used the asura gates to begin an
assault on Tyria
in retaliation for the Commander's earlier interference in Joko's kingdom. Zaeim pledged his Sunspears to the Commander's cause, not wishing to see Tyria fall to Joko like Elona had. The Commander promised Zaeim that Dragon's Watch would help end Joko's reign in Elona once and for all now that the lich king was posing too great a threat to the world to ignore.
Long Live the Lich
After Joko's attempt to
unleash the first generation of Scarab Plague
had been thwarted in the
Free City of Amnoon
in 1331
AE
, Taimi sent word to the allies whom the Commander had gained during their stay in Elona and asked them to travel to the
Tomb of the Primeval Kings
where she and her friend
Blish
were setting up a portal to Joko's last known location, the
Domain of Kourna
. Zaeim rallied his Istani Sunspears and went through the portal with other allies to assist the Commander in establishing a foothold at
Zelbahn Garrison
from which the allied army would stage an assault on
Gandara, the Moon Fortress
where Joko was maturing the deadly third generation of Scarab Plague.
After securing the area around the
Allied Encampment
, Zaeim was ordered to command his Sunspears from the main camp and send them scouting the north to protect the army's flank. Zaeim understood the Commander's concern to not subject any of the human Sunspears to the Scarab Plague. After hearing his scouts' reports, Zaeim let the Commander know what troubles were brewing throughout Kourna and directed the Commander to many sites where the allied army hoped to stop Joko's plans to spread the Scarab Plague and secure new allies from the locals.
Following the Commander's successful recruitment of allies, Zaeim reported that the army's assault on Gandara had stalled because they needed high-grade explosives to break the defensive structures guarding the fortress. He also let the Commander know of
Lord Faren
's dangerous mission to acquire the explosives and expressed concern over Faren's safety, prompting the Commander to
go after Faren
.
Once news spread that the third generation of Scarab Plague had been destroyed and Joko had been
killed for good
in the
Battle of Gandara
, Zaeim and the Sunspears were asked to stay in Kourna for a time to watch for the now dazed and masterless Awakened and make sure the Awakened would not hurt themselves or anyone else once they came to their senses.
A Star to Guide Us
Some time later, Zaeim sent invitations to various factions to attend a
summit
at the
Fortress of Jahai
where they would discuss a post-Joko ruling council for Elona. Despite his initial attempts, the Spearmarshal had difficulty convincing the
Free Awakened
, the
Joko Loyalists
, the
Mordant Crescent
and the
Order of Shadows
to agree to the Sunspears' proposal to end old animosities and work together. Tensions grew among the delegates when the Commander's exhausted dragon ally Aurene, who had devoured Joko in Gandara, landed in the fortress, and Zaeim was relieved to see the Commander arrive shortly after and convince Aurene to leave the fortress so the negotiations could continue.
Angered by what he perceived as the Commander using Aurene to intimidate him,
Archon Iberu
and his Mordant Crescent staged a coup in which they attempted to kill everyone present so Iberu could lead a united Elona. The Archon's ambitions were cut short by the timely arrival of
Awakened Koss Dejarin
who killed Iberu with a single blow and intimidated the Mordant Crescent to agree to the Spearmarshal's treaty. Former
Grand Vizier Utumishi
was less receptive to signing the treaty despite Zaeim's pleas, however, but their arguing was cut short by the sudden attack from the
Death-Branded Shatterer
which was hunting for Aurene on behalf of the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
. The invading
Branded
forced the Commander and the delegates to flee into the courtyard before it gave chase to Aurene.
Following the fall of the fortress, the
Order of the Sunspears
decided to move survivors of the Branded attacks in
Jahai Bluffs
and local refugees to a safer location to withstand future Branded incursions. Zaeim learned that a group led by the Commander had secured an old cave which had been dubbed
Sun's Refuge
, and escorted Aurene and the refugees to the cave alongside the Commander's allies. He witnessed
another Branded invasion
from
rifts
which suddenly appeared there and ensured the refugees' safety while the Commander took a small group to fight the Branded head-on.
Once the invasion had been thwarted, Zaeim witnessed the Commander return to Aurene with the
ghosts
of
Eir Stegalkin
and
Snaff
who had come with great urgency: Kralkatorrik was devouring
magic
in
the Mists
, and Aurene needed to confront the Elder Dragon before it would become too powerful to face. Aurene shared her visions of multiple futures, however, in which she died no matter which allies she brought with her for the confrontation, and fled while leaving the Commander and the allies behind to worry about the future.
To stop panic from spreading, the Commander's ally
Blish
pointed out that he and the Commander already had a plan to attach a tracker to Kralkatorrik via a magical lure,
Balthazar's greatsword
, so they could monitor the Elder Dragon's movements and evacuate settlements before the Branded could invade populated areas. In response, Zaeim sent word to the Sunspears throughout Elona, hoping they would answer his call for action to come protect Elonians from the Branded. He also asked the Commander to place
recruitment posters
around Elona and
Central Tyria
to add more able warriors to the Sunspears' ranks.
While organizing the forces stationed at Sun's Refuge, Zaeim got to know
General Almorra Soulkeeper
's stranded
Pact
forces and came to respect their duty to protect Tyria from the Elder Dragons. Upon receiving recruits and witnessing
Vabbi
Sunspears answer his call, Zaeim learned from them that
Spearmarshal Tahlkora
, who had been guiding the Vabbi branch of the Sunspears, had given him her blessing to officially succeed her as Spearmarshal. The news lifted Zaeim's spirits as deep down he had always wondered if he had had any right to be called Spearmarshal. He also granted permission for Koss to seek out Tahlkora, and talked with
Redeemer Kossan
and
Lord Faren
about their personal accomplishments.
All or Nothing
As the Pact mobilized under
Marshal
Logan Thackeray
and traveled to
Thunderhead Keep
to help the Commander and Aurene prepare for the confrontation against Kralkatorrik, Zaeim was one of the Commander's many Elonian allies who decided to join the Pact in the keep to offer their assistance in gratitude for what the Commander had done on their behalf. While he made preparations in Sun's Refuge for the journey ahead, he delegated
Second Spear Koja
as his representative in the keep until his arrival in early 1332
AE
.
Zaeim participated in the
battle
against Kralkatorrik and helped the Commander get past the Branded. Despite the Pact's efforts, however, the wounded Kralkatorrik managed to decimate much of
the Auditorium
and Brand several soldiers in it, kill Aurene and flee back to the Mists, leaving the Commander and surviving allies to mourn their losses.
War Eternal
After learning of
Aurene
's
revival
, Zaeim followed
Dragon's Watch
and the
Pact
to
Dragonfall
where the Commander and the rejuvenated Aurene had managed to lure the wounded Kralkatorrik to. He brought only Sunspears who had decided to leave the order and rededicate themselves to Aurene as members of the
Order of the Crystal Bloom
. Alongside the Commander's ally
Caithe
, Zaeim helped the Crystal Bloom
establish
and build up a
forward camp
in preparation to fight the heavily wounded Kralkatorrik before the Elder Dragon could heal himself. While facing the Branded and the
Shadow Army
, Zaeim and Caithe discussed their experiences on Dragonfall, and Zaeim encouraged Caithe to embrace her role as the leader of the Crystal Bloom just like he had embraced becoming the Spearmarshal of the Sunspears. After Kralkatorrik's
demise
in
the Battle of Dragonfall
, Zaeim expressed his plans to return to the Order of Sunspears and continue to rebuild its strength as well as find reliable people to lead Elona.
End of Dragons
While the Pact Commander's party, which now included
Canthan
allies, battled
the Dragonvoid
at the
Harvest Temple
in 1335
AE
, the
Void
horde began a
massive global assault
as the Dragonvoid intended to end all existence everywhere on
Tyria
. Zaeim and the
Zaishen
leader
Zafirah
witnessed
the Void outbreak on the
Sandswept Isles
and rallied their troops to face this new threat and protect Elona. They, like the Commander's other allies across the world, ultimately witnessed the Void horde dissipating once the Commander and Aurene had defeated the Dragonvoid.
Janthir Wilds

expanding it

Squad Leader Bennett

Pact
Image(s)
Appearance as a prisoner in the Forsaken Thicket.
“
I'm either exceptionally unlucky or exceptionally lucky. Can't decide which.
—
Squad Leader Bennett
Squad Leader Bennett
is the leader of the
Pact
squad which went missing in the
Forsaken Thicket
. After he was rescued from his captors and recovered his strength, Bennett returned to aid the Pact against the
White Mantle
in
Bloodstone Fen
in 1329
AE
. He traveled to
Thunderhead Peaks
to help train Pact forces for the assault on the
Elder Dragon
Kralkatorrik
in 1332
AE
.

A Shadow's Deeds
Dialogue
Salvation Pass
It's hard to believe the
White Mantle
have been operating unchecked for so long. We have to do something about it.
How do you know these people are White Mantle?
Squad Leader Bennett:
Look around. Those banners, their weapons, the outfits—they all scream "religious fanatics".
Squad Leader Bennett:
There's propaganda everywhere. Explore the temple and the living area. You'll see what I mean.
Squad Leader Bennett:
The other prisoners might know more.
We have to stop them.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Agreed. If the White Mantle has been operating out here unchecked for this long, who knows how strong they are.
Squad Leader Bennett:
They're sacrificing people and harnessing their
magic
, but for what? The
Mursaat
are all dead.
Squad Leader Bennett:

Krytan

queen
and the

What do you know about the
Bloodstone

Squad Leader Bennett:
Their weapons are imbued with powerful magic. I'd bet my right eye it has something to do with the Bloodstone.

It's a relic that can store vast amounts of magical energy, including the souls of the sacrificed.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Centuries ago, the Mursaat used bloodstone magic to keep their enemies at bay.

But the Mursaat were all killed off, except for one who was gravely wounded.

Lazarus
was the last surviving Mursaat, though most theorize he wandered off to die.

Mordremoth

Recap the White Mantle's origins. [Replay Cinematic]
Bloodstone Fen

Glad you made it,
Commander

Recap the White Mantle's origins. [Replay Cinematic]
(starts cinematic)
The Mursaat preyed upon the weak and impressionable, who in turn worshiped them as gods.
Calling themselves the White Mantle, these converts sacrificed innocents and toppled the Krytan monarchy.
The chaos they inflicted on
Tyria
was short-lived. They were eventually crushed, and their scattered remnants went into hiding.
But pockets of their fanatical followers have been operating in the shadows ever since.
What happened in Forsaken Thicket?
<Character name>:
Tell me what happened to your squad.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Our platoon was on recon. We found something at the north edge of
Verdant Brink
, so we pitched camp.
Squad Leader Bennett:
The plan was to scout the area at dawn, so we turned in for the night. Then everything went to
Torment
in a town cart.
Squad Leader Bennett:
There was an explosion, then screams. I grabbed my gear and ran out of the tent.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Saw one of my men lit up like a
Wintersday
bonfire.
Sabetha
and her lackeys torched everything, laughing as they went.
Squad Leader Bennett:
And then...blackness. I woke up with a splitting headache, caged like a dog. Kept my mouth shut and just watched them.
Squad Leader Bennett:
After a few days, I told them I was ready to talk. Said I'd cooperate in exchange for the safety of my troops.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Told them they could use me for hard labor or experimentation. Didn't matter. I'd made my peace with
Grenth
.
Squad Leader Bennett:
I was taken deeper inside and thrown in a pen with some others. They deprived us of food and water.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Let their dogs snarl at our cages. At night we heard the other prisoners begging for their lives before being cut open.
Squad Leader Bennett:
The sky would light up, and I would hear chanting. Over and over.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Sorry, Commander. It...stuck with me.
What are you doing here?

What are you doing here?

After I was rescued, I notified the queen about Salvation Pass. Then I regrouped with my squad and chased some White Mantle here.

I never did that.

How have you been since Salvation Pass?
<Character name>:
How've you been since Salvation Pass?
If rescued Bennett at Salvation Pass:

I've been thanking
Dwayna
you busted me out of that infernal place. If not for you, I'd be rotting in a ditch.
If didn't rescue Bennett at Salvation Pass:
Squad Leader Bennett:
I've been thanking Dwayna that the rescue team found me in time. If not for them, I'd be rotting in a ditch.
Squad Leader Bennett:
When I close my eyes at night, I can still hear the screams. Men, women. They spared no one. I lost count.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Anyway, I made my way back to
Falgeir
's camp and rejoined the squad. Saw the field medic and got checked out.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Then we sent word to Queen Jennah about everything. Gave her and the Shining Blade as much information as we could.
Squad Leader Bennett:
The
Pact
offered to put me on leave, but I turned it down. No way I'm giving these murderers a break.
Squad Leader Bennett:
So we pursued a lead on White Mantle movement and tracked the pigs up here.
Squad Leader Bennett:
Look what they've done with the place! Shameful.
Good-bye.
Thunderhead Peaks
Good to see you again, Commander.
I'm glad to have a veteran of the White Mantle campaign fighting with us.
I've seen terrible things in my time, but what Kralkatorrik has done... It's unthinkable. You'll need all the help and experience you can get.
I accept yours gratefully.
The same. At ease.
Notes
In Salvation Pass, Bennett is located at the end and can only be spoken to after
Matthias Gabrel
has been defeated.
Trivia
His name is a reference to
Trevor Bennett
, a member of the
Guild Wars 2 Raids Team
.
The expression "everything went to Torment in a town cart" is probably a Guild Wars 2 equivalent reference of the expression "everything went to hell in a handcart".

======================================================================
PAGE: Stumm
CATEGORIES: Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Stumm
======================================================================

Stumm
Race
Asura
Locations
Lornar's Pass
(
Shiverpeak Mountains
)
Malchor's Leap
(
Ruins of Orr
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Pact
“
A genius and his time are soon parted.
— Stumm
Stumm
is an
asuran
tradesman and
Order of Whispers
Creator, an inventor of gadgets to assist agents in field work. In typical asura fashion, he is arrogant and narcissistic. It is also claimed that he's a bit of an eccentric, being involved in some unusual schemes.
Stumm is never actually seen in-game, but he is interacted with indirectly during the events of which he is a part. In one instance an order is placed by
messenger crow
and delivered via teleporter. In another, he sends a
messenger golem
that projects a
hologram in his likeness
.
Operations
In
Lornar's Pass
, Stumm makes shipments via caravan, however the shipments are frequently attacked by
grawl
, who steal the shipments and worship the contents.
Shipping Logs
left behind in the attacks reveal that one of Stumm's frequent patrons is
Ogden Stonehealer
of the
Durmand Priory
, to whom Stumm attempted to send a shipment of flowers as a gift of thanks.
He is a financial backer of
Researcher Bramm
and
Venpa
in their research into ettins as a potential source of cheap labor. He provides Venpa with one of his
Stumm Brand Power Suits
to facilitate her
construction of an ettin-proof suit
. He also arranges for a shipment of artifical
Wind Riders
to be sent to
Researcher Suki
as part of a plan to teach fear to norn youth.
In
Malchor's Leap
, in his duty as Whispers Creator, he assists the Whispers Agents stationed on
Murmur Isle
, in
Whisper Bay
, with their assault on the
Omicron Test Facility
. He decrypts codes acquired by one of the agents and sends his messenger golem, programmed with the necessary codes to shut down the facility's energy tower.
Event involvement
Help Venpa finish the Ettin Proof Suit Mark II
(39)
Help Iduna destroy the Omicron golem factory
(78)

Sunspear Paragon
Race
Human
Location
Tyria
(
Tyria
)
Organization
Order of the Sunspears
Sunspear Paragons
are
humans
summoned with the
Sunspear Paragon Support
consumable. When summoned, they will assist the
player
in attacking enemies. The ally disappears after 2 minutes of being summoned or when changing zones.
Combat abilities
The
Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Sunspear Paragon
.
Skills
Song of Power
- Applies Quickness, Might and Vigor. Recharges all skills when removed.
Anomaly
:
Although the Sunspear Paragon's attacks play an effect when hitting an opponent in the
Guild Arena
, it seems no status effect is actually applied.

PAGE: Tamela Okar

Tamela Okar
Race
Human
Profession
Spellbreaker
Level
80
Rank
Elite
Location
Salt Flats
(
Desert Highlands
)
Organization
Order of the Sunspears
Image(s)
Interactive map
Tamela Okar
is a
human
Spellbreaker
found in the
Salt Flats
near the
Kanuz Cache
. As an Elite Specialization Mentor, she helps players learn more about spellbreakers.
Location
Crystal Desert
Desert Highlands
Salt Flats
Kanuz Cache
Combat abilities
Abilities
Removes Boons
Dialogue
I wouldn't stand too near me if were you. I'm a spellbreaker, so I'm on
Joko
's most-wanted list, and
Awakened
come through here regularly.
A spellbreaker? I took you for a
Sunspear
.
I am a Sunspear, it's just that I don't use a spear. Our special talents are common knowledge around here, but I guess it makes sense that we'd be unfamiliar to an outlander like yourself.
Where do spellbreakers come from?
When Joko finally defeated the Sunspears over a hundred years ago, he decreed that anyone owning a spear would be put to death.
That seems extreme.
Destroying us wasn't enough for Joko. He wanted to destroy our symbol too. Our survivors were forced underground. Some of us continued to train without our spears.
So you can still fight the Awakened?
Yes. We've had to get creative. We've learned to counter their
magic
, detect their hidden agents, and prevent their escape. It's quite effective.
I have another question.
I Need to get moving.
I have another question.
Very brave of you. Good luck.
I have another question.
On second thought, I have somewhere to be.
Do spellbreaker always wield daggers?
A spellbreaker can strike at the weave of magic using any weapon, but the precision of daggers makes it easier. You could say daggers have sentimental value as well.
Sentimental value? Like a family heirloom?
When our spears were broken, many of us reforged the spearheads into daggers. It helps keep our symbol alive, even if we can't display it openly anymore.
I have another question.
I understand. Hope it all works out for you.
I have another question.
We should chat more on this later.
Why do they call you spellbreakers?
Spellbreakers specialize in counterattacks and destroying enchantments. We can absorb a powerful blow from an opponent and deflect it back with many times the force.
So, you can dispel enchantments?
Destroy, not dispel. It takes a lot of training, but we've learned to sense the weave of magic and strike where it is weakest. Most mages never see it coming.
I have another question.
I'll be sure not to cast any spells at you.
I have another question.
You're a formidable opponent, then.
Why are you out here?
I'm trying to lay low. Every time I think I've found a good hiding place, I'm flushed out by the Awakened. I'm lucky to have survived this long.
Are you always on the run?
It's the spellbreaker life. We make occasional guerilla strikes on Awakened outposts and convoys, but we're too small a force to maintain a dedicated push against them.
So you're kind of like Sunspears?
(Same as "A spellbreaker? I took you for a Sunspear.")
I'd better get moving.
I won't draw attention to you.
That's all I needed to know.
Why are you out here?
(Same as "Why are you out here?")
I'll keep moving, then.
See also

Dialogue
Anyone who believes that crash was my fault should have their license to draw conclusions revoked. Which is to say, I made the only logical decision.
Which was?
Set a collision course with the dragon. Given that our airship had no weapons, and it seemed the dragon was headed for the main fleet, it only made sense to hurl our tiny craft at the giant beast.
What did that accomplish?
Less than I'd hoped. Apparently, dragons are constructed in such a way that they're immune to the normal laws of mid-air collision. Which is to say, ramming them is ineffective.
What happened?
We bounced off the creature at a most unfortunate angle- straight down. We plummeted. Luckily, our uncontrolled descent was truncated by the ground.
What do you do after this...setback.
A setback is merely a disastrous conclusion to one's plan. We continue on toward the Inquest base, of course, only at a lower altitude. Which is to say, we walk. We must put a stop to whatever they're planning.
Okay.
Ouch.
At least you learned something.
I think I understand why you ended up here on the ground.
The important thing is that you're convinced.
Before Destroy the Mark V Golem defending the Inquest gate
That Mark V defense golem is a formidable obstacle. I'm afraid there's little that my vast technological prowess can do against it. Which is to say, this calls for brawn over brain.
Okay.
During Destroy the Mark V Golem defending the Inquest gate
There's no special trick to defeating the golem. So don't waste both our time with finesse, just bash away at it until it stops working.
Good plan.

Clever.

What was it like?
A while ago, this water used to be deeper and the lakes were more connected. Iron ore was starting to see more use, and there were good deposits up here. Ore and supplies would be sent upriver on boats.
This lake here?

I'm impressed. You've somehow managed to penetrate the impenetrable.
There's always risk in learning the unlearnable. Why is the Order of Whispers here?
One of our agents watching the
Flame Legion
expansion to the north noticed this congregation of grawl. They tend to favor smaller social units, so it's unusual for this many to gather in one place.

======================================================================
PAGE: Turai Ossa
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Order of Whispers
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Turai_Ossa
======================================================================

This article is about the former King of Elona and his
watchknight
fascimile.  For the echo, see
Echo of Turai Ossa
. For the Mist Champion, see
Turai Ossa (Mist Champion)
. For the actor, see
Turai Ossa (actor)
.
Turai Ossa
Race
Ghost
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Shallows of Despair
(
Elon Riverlands
)
The Crown Pavilion
(
Kryta
)
Organization
Order of Whispers
Image(s)
Turai Ossa's
watchknight
fascimile during the
Queen's Gauntlet
.
Warmarshal
Turai Ossa
was the
Kournan
hero who fought and defeated
Palawa Joko
, and later founded the
Order of Whispers
to ensure the
lich
would remain imprisoned. Following his victory, he was anointed as the king of all
Elona
, and ruled over Elona for years before taking a large group of his people on the
Great Pilgrimage
to the
Crystal Desert
in order to
Ascend
at
Augury Rock
. A war against the
Forgotten
, infighting among his people, and the brutally inhospitable land resulted in their deaths, and their
ghosts
remained in the Crystal Desert until the
Chosen
of the
Flameseeker Prophecies
arrived to Ascend. Turai assisted them in their journey and was rewarded with access to
the Hall of Heroes
although he later returned to the
Throne of Pellentia
to offer counsel to anyone wishing to follow in his footsteps.
A
watchknight
appearing as Turai Ossa has been integrated into
the Crown Pavilion
as a challenging fight in the
Queen's Gauntlet
from 1331
AE
onward.
Biography
Warmarshal of Kourna
Turai Ossa and
Palawa Joko
duel above the
Battle of Jahai
.
In life, Turai Ossa served as the
Warmarshal
of
Kourna
and fathered a son,
Kunai Ossa
.
During
Palawa Joko
's conquest of
Elona
beginning in 860
AE
, Turai led the dwindling resistance, consisting of Kournans,
Istani
,
Vabbi
, and the
Order of the Sunspears
against the
lich
's combined force of
corsair
mercenaries and
Awakened
. Using the desperate tactics of hit-and-run warfare, the renegade warmarshal hunted Joko's army wherever possible, but his forces—including his lieutenants
Commander Joziah
,
General Mendoza
, and
Lord Valodor
—were surrounded near the
Grand Cataract of Jahai
, where they prepared to make their final stand against the lich's army, in 862
AE
. Turai's scouts learned that Joko had insufficiently garrisoned his flank in his hurry to crush the resistance, however, and the warmarshal believed a small but powerful group might be able to fight its way through and slay the lich while the rest of the army held the line against the Awakened horde.
The resulting
Battle of Jahai
lasted for days; each time night fell, the battleground remained unchanged, as neither army gave ground to the other. Turai's squad was ultimately able to reach Joko's camp just in time to witness the lich gloating about his impending victory. Playing to Joko's vanity, Turai challenged him to single combat. The lich could not resist the urge to fell the warmarshal personally, and the two leaders faced off high above the warring armies. There, in full view of their respective followers, Turai bested Joko, and the undead lord fell from his lofty height. On the plains below, the resolve of the unliving hordes wavered and broke. Turai's forces cheered at the defeat of the enemy leader as they cut down the fleeing undead legions.
Though beaten, Joko could not be slain. Turai's bodyguards used powerful
magic
to bind the lich within a great tomb, and eventually built a
huge stone plinth
commemorating the victory at Jahai Bluffs while forming the
Order of Whispers
to watch over the tomb. There, Joko would lie forgotten, trapped with his hatred and weakness.
King of Elona
Following his victory over Joko, Turai wrote the
Proclamation of Palawa Joko's Defeat
. Riding on his success and the need for Elona to have a strong leader, he was anointed the king of all Elona, uniting the once separate
human
provinces under his banner in a feat that had not been matched since the days of the
Great Dynasty
.
During his reign, Turai proved to be a benevolent king although he secretly yearned for loftier goals than wealth, power, and influence. Openly, he ruled a grateful populace, but secretly, he spent his nights studying ancient wisdom. The more he read, the more he contemplated the role of the
gods
in his life. If he had been chosen by the gods as the savior of his nation like his adoring subjects claimed, he thought he might be destined for greater accomplishments. Musing on mysteries, Turai sought his answers by seeking
Ascension
, a state of communion with the gods. He knew the path to that ideal ran through the wasteland north of Elona—lands that had been ruled by Palawa Joko years before.
Having learned of
Glint
's
Flameseeker Prophecies
, Turai likened himself to the eponymous Flameseeker in the texts. Resolved to face his newest challenge and embrace a destiny he believed to be his, the king stepped down from his position of authority in 868
AE
, passing the title of warmarshal to his son Kunai.
Turai and his followers began the
Great Pilgrimage
north into the
Crystal Desert
where the gods had once walked among men, and Turai recorded the path of the pilgrimage on a
map
. The king entrusted shards of the
Vision Crystal
needed to perform a ritual to access
Augury Rock
to three high ritual priests named
Hehmnut
,
Kahdat
, and
Nahtem
.
Turai's loyal entourage erected temples, raising pillars toward the heavens, and even founded towns such as
Lannur
and a sizable
colony
during their journey. Despite these efforts, however, Ascension eluded the king who eventually came into conflict with the
Forgotten
and faced distrust and greed from his fellow pilgrims, including the three priestly brothers who became divisive and kept the shards of the Vision Crystal for themselves. The entourage lost their lives in the ensuing battles with the Forgotten, in-fighting among the pilgrims, and to other hazards in the desert.
After Turai fell in battle, his body was reportedly recovered by Lord Valodor.
Legacy
Ghostly Hero
Instead of everlasting life, Turai and his followers found the torment of eternal living death. In
ghostly
form, they walked the earth, remaining in the desert that had claimed their lives. In the following decades, Turai became known as the
Ghostly Hero
who would guide others in search of Ascension.
Following Turai's disappearance, the three Elonian provinces became autonomous again by 874
AE
. Turai's son Kunai Ossa, having refused the Dynastic Crown, ruled Kourna as a warmarshal and passed on the title to his descendants in the Ossa clan in a tradition that would last for centuries. In 899
AE
, the
Fortress of Jahai
was built near the site of Turai's triumph over Joko.
In 1072
AE
, the spirit of Turai helped the
Chosen
, including the
Hero of Ascalon
, to Ascend in the trials of
Augury Rock
by reclaiming the
Throne of Pellentia
from a
rival ghostly hero
who had usurped his place and by using a ritual to unite the three shards of the Vision Crystal which the Chosen had brought to him after a series of challenges by the Forgotten.
As a reward for restoring his honor by helping the Chosen who had gone on to fulfill the Flameseeker Prophecies and save
Tyria
, Turai was granted access to
the Hall of Heroes
in
the Mists
, a goal he had yearned for a long time.
Despite receiving his reward, however, Turai remained in the corporeal world for a time, appearing at the tournaments held at the
Tomb of the Primeval Kings
. After the
Wintersday
celebrations in the same year, the forces of
Dhuum
and
Abaddon
interrupted the tournament. These forces defeated the spirits of Turai,
Lord Victo
, and the
Primeval Kings
, and began to slip through
a portal
into Tyria until they were driven back by the combined efforts of the
Zaishen Order
and the Order of the Sunspears.
[
verification requested
]
The legacy of Turai Ossa remained controversial. While some Elonians remembered the king for his heroism in the war against Palawa Joko, others remembered him as a madman whose false pride led him on a doomed pilgrimage, yet others viewed him only as the Ghostly Hero meant to aid pilgrims seeking Ascension. His reputation would be further soiled by the actions of his distant descendant, Warmarshal
Varesh Ossa
, who betrayed Elona and nearly secured the fallen god Abaddon's release by ushering in
Nightfall
in 1075
AE
.
Thanks to Turai's actions, however, Elona remained free from Joko for centuries, and his guidance ensured the success of the Hero of
Ascalon
who would end up fulfilling the Flameseeker Prophecies and saving Tyria. Hymns from the annals of Turai would be recited by
firebrands
inspired by the warmarshal to cast the
Mantra of Potence
centuries later, and books such as
Of Greatness, Of Madness: The Life of Turai Ossa
and
The Romance of Turai
were written in remembrance of his deeds.
Although he had been rewarded with access to the Hall of Heroes, the ghost of Turai Ossa remained in the Crystal Desert and provided guidance to pilgrims and lost spirits at the
Throne of Pellentia
. Since Palawa Joko's return and conquest of Elona since 1175
AE
,
Turai remained hopeful that a new generation of heroes would rise up to defeat the lich once more.
Path of Fire
In 1330
AE
, Turai
met with
adventurers and the
Pact Commander
who accompanied the ghosts of Commander Joziah, General Mendoza, and Lord Valodor to the Throne of Pellentia. In the ensuing conversation Turai sensed a familiarity in the Commander's spirit if the Commander had gained access to the
Hall of Monuments
previously, suggesting offhand that the Commander's spirit might have ties to the Hero of Ascalon who had saved all of
Tyria
centuries prior. He also provided the Commander with advice on how to fight Joko, suggesting that the resistance should unite all of Elona under one banner and out-strategize the lich with cunning and strength.
Following the Commander's journey to the desert,
mesmer
magic
was used to make the last of the reworked
watchknight
opponents appear as Turai Ossa in
the Crown Pavilion
's duel ring during the
Festival of the Four Winds
from 1331
AE
onward. Challengers faced this mechanical fascimile of the warmarshal as the final battle in the
Queen's Gauntlet
of
Divinity's Reach
to prove their mettle. The watchknight's abilities were based on historical records of the real Turai Ossa's skills as well as engineers' guesswork.
Location
Crystal Desert
Elon Riverlands
Shallows of Despair
Kryta
The Crown Pavilion
(as King Turai Ossa)
(only during
Festival of the Four Winds
)
