Walkthrough
Professions with lots of damage avoidance and
crowd control
are good in this fight, such as
Spellbreakers
,
Mesmers
, and
Thieves
.
Avoid combat and try to survive for 35 seconds.
After 35 seconds, Turai Ossa will leap to the center of the platform and prepare to throw his shield. If you avoid getting hit by the shield throw, he will get 5 seconds of
protection
and his
defiance bar
will unlock. Depleting his defiance bar will stun him and he'll lose his shield and the
Shield of Elona
effect, allowing players to damage him for a few seconds. If you get hit by the shield, you get stunned for a really long time, receive heavy damage and Turai will gain 25 stacks of
might
, 15 seconds of
quickness
, 15 seconds of
swiftness
, and 15 seconds of
resistance
.
Continue to avoid his attacks and if possible try do damage him at the same time. Again, after 20 seconds, he will leap to the center of the platform to throw his sword. If you avoid getting hit by the sword, he will get 5 seconds of
protection
and a defiance bar will appear. Depleting this defiance bar will stun him and he'll lose his sword and the
Eternal Blade
effect, greatly reducing his attack damage. If you get hit by the sword, he will get back his shield, the Eternal Blade effect and gain 25 stacks of
might
, 15 seconds of
quickness
, 15 seconds of
swiftness
, and 15 seconds of
resistance
.
After both effects are removed, he'll attack you without weapons and you can damage him safely. Always deplete his breakbar after he leaps to the center, or he'll get back his sword and shield, and the Eternal Blade and Shield of Elona effects.
Dialogue
You honor the
heroes
of the past, who attempted the trials put forth by the
Forgotten
. The victors are deserving, but so are those who lost their lives trying. The desert remembers.
(If account is linked to the
Hall of Monuments
)
The desert remembers?
There's a familiarity about your spirit, something ancient from centuries ago. I believe you will play a role in defeating
Joko
again. I can feel it.
That's my hope. What advice can you give me?
(continues as below)
I will help bring him down if I can.
(Otherwise)
What advice do you have for those who fight Joko?
It was no easy task to fell the Scourge of
Vabbi
. The forces of
Kourna
, Vabbi, and
Istan
, and the surviving
Sunspears
, united to make our last grand stand at the
Grand Cataract
at
Jahai
.
So all of
Elona
must unite and rise.
Our forces were outnumbered by Joko's. As the battle waged below, I led a small detachment of elite troops across the mesa-tops to flank Palawa's army.
So we must out-strategize Joko as well.
Only then could we fight our way to Joko, where I defeated him. Even then, Joko could not be eliminated, only entombed. Be ready. Be clever, and be strong.
I will. Thanks for your help.
I can outsmart Joko, too. I think.
I'll keep that in mind.
It's an honor to have met you.
Related achievements
Elon Riverlands
:
Ghostly Trio
—
Follow the three ghostly heroes through each step of their return path, and meet their leader.
(3
)
Queen's Gauntlet
:
Kingslayer
—
Defeat
King Turai Ossa
in the special tier of Queen's Gauntlet.
(20
)
Amnytas
:
Echoes of the Obscure
—
Learn more about the Bastion of the Obscure and a legendary hero to acquire a powerful and ancient weapon.
(3
)
Gallery
Turai Ossa's shield
Trivia
Turai is
voiced
by
Fred Tatasciore
.
After the
Augury Rock
mission in
Guild Wars Prophecies
, Turai Ossa had the opportunity to move on to his proper afterlife in
the Hall of Heroes
. It appears he instead chose to remain in
Elona
and continue guiding adventurers, for he can still be found at the
Throne of Pellentia
.
His name is similar to the Japanese phrase "Tsurai Ossan" which means "middle aged man in hardship."
Turai was initially considered to appear in the
Pact Commander
's final confrontation with
Palawa Joko
in
Be My Guest
before his role was removed in the rewrites of the
Long Live the Lich
episode.
See also

Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Turai Ossa
.

Guild Wars
Wiki
has an article on
Ghostly Hero

======================================================================
PAGE: Tyrian Explorers Society
CATEGORIES: Tyrian Explorers Society
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Tyrian_Explorers_Society
======================================================================

Tyrian Explorers Society emblem. Letters spell out "TES".
The
Tyrian Explorers Society
is an organization which sends congratulatory mail messages to players when they reach 100%
map completion
for a zone. They also coordinate the
Guild Treks
, sending players on missions to find specific locations around
Tyria
.
Dougal Keane
in the
Guild Initiative Headquarters
mentions that the Society "is a group of cartographers, adventurers, and wanderers. They've made it their purpose to create a complete map of Tyria." According to him, the
Guild Initiative
is "an arm of the Tyrian Explorer's Society", operating with the endorsement of the five major nations of Tyria and the
Captain's Council
in Lion's Arch.
Known historical members
Princess Emilane
Tyrian Explorer Olread
Gallery
Render of the Tyrian Explorers Society's emblem.

Oh?

I look forward to it.
Thank you!

Dialogue
Hey, <Character name>. You may not know me, but I know of you. I'm here on a mission to scout around Jormabakke Stead. I could use someone with your talents.
I'm listening.
The
Svanir
in Jormabakke Stead have been growing in strength recently. I've seen them do some terrible things, but I can't intervene without blowing my cover. That's where you come in.
What should I do?
These guys are so far gone, there's nothing left but to put them down and destroy their banners. You should also kill any corrupted ice or creatures you see.

Are you open for business?
You're welcome.

Dialogue
Well, what have we here? An unfamiliar face? Either I've never met you, or you weren't worth remembering. Which is it?
(If in Order of Whispers)
Ah, to remember the unremembered.
Is to meet the unmet. You have anything for me?
No. You for me?
I might. I've been working the fringe of Hoelbrak, talking to some of the shadier hunters this place attracts. I'm hearing rumors that the
Sons of Svanir
are mobilizing.
Let me guess. Heading southward?
You got it. It's starting to feel like the

[
sic

walk away before I have Olaf over there turn you around for me.

======================================================================
PAGE: Warden Amala
CATEGORIES: Legendary NPCs, Order of the Sunspears, Story characters
URL: https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Warden_Amala
======================================================================

Spoiler alert:
The following text contains spoilers relating to
Living World Season 4
.
Warden Amala
Race
Awakened
,
Human
Level
80
Rank
Legendary
Locations
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
(
Domain of Istan
)
Twilight Oasis Fractal
(
Fractals of the Mists
)
Organization
Mordant Crescent
Order of the Sunspears
Image(s)
Appearance as a human—High Priestess Amala
Interactive map
“
We all serve Joko—in life or in death.
—
Warden Amala
Warden Amala
, previously known as
High Priestess Amala
and
Executioner Amala
, is a high-ranking officer in the
Mordant Crescent
. In the past, after the events of
Nightfall
, she was the high priestess of the
Sunspears
in an unknown
Elonian
city, but was defeated by traitors and subsequently killed and
Awakened
by
Palawa Joko
. She is a powerful
dervish
, allowing her to invoke the power of the
gods
.
Biography
Early years
High Priestess Amala was the high priestess of the
Sunspears
in an unknown
Elonian
city during
Palawa Joko
's conquest of
Elona
, which the lich king's
Awakened
and
Mordant Crescent
assaulted. Despite putting up a fight, rallying loyal Sunspears and invoking the
gods
as a dervish, she was ultimately defeated when Joko's army stormed the city.
Joko gave Amala a choice: either pledge allegiance to him and serve him alive, or die and be Awakened to serve him as an undead. The high priestess remained loyal to the Sunspears and refused to submit to the lich king. Disappointed but nevertheless expecting such a defiant response, Joko had Amala killed and Awakened, dubbing her Executioner Amala. Joko then ordered her to execute her Sunspear companions, and she carried out the lich king's orders without mercy despite the pleas of her former allies.
Amala was eventually made the warden of the
Mordant Crescent Great Hall
in the
Domain of Istan
. She would imprison enemies of state in the dungeon beneath the hall and have her Awakened minions keep watch. Centuries later Amala worked with local
corsairs
, offering them bounties on the heads of dissidents whom the corsairs tracked down and brought to her.
Living World Season 4
In 1330
AE
, Amala received the latest quarry from the corsair
Sayida the Sly
:
Spearmarshal Zaeim
who had been leading the Sunspear revolt in Istan. Sayida later returned to the great hall, bringing with her the shackled
Pact Commander
who had interfered in the internal politics of Joko's kingdom during the campaign against the rogue god
Balthazar
and become an enemy of state. Amala was surprised by Sayida's success but was unable to learn how the corsair had bested the infamous Commander.
Before leaving with the gold, Sayida insisted on offering the warden and her minions a drink of the famous corsair grog in Joko's honor, arguing that refusing the offer would displease the lich king who wanted his name to be praised. Amala reluctantly agreed but quickly focused on the Commander when she heard a strange voice calling for the Commander. Angry and suspicious, the warden knocked the Commander unconscious and had them dragged to the dungeon. She recovered a remote communicator from the Commander's belongings for further study.
Later that day, the spiced grog began affecting the Awakened who became intoxicated and passed out, allowing the Commander and Zaeim to escape the dungeon. Amala confronted Sayida and her crew about the ruse, having expected such a trick from the Commander not but not from the corsairs. By the time the Commander and Zaeim arrived on the scene, Amala announced that Joko had ordered her to kill the Commander. The warden entered the fray, focusing on slaughtering everyone present and holding her ground while battling the Commander, Zaeim and the corsairs. However, she was ultimately defeated, and the Commander recovered the communicator from her body, hearing
Taimi
being assaulted by what sounded like Joko himself.
Following the Commander's escape, Amala Awakened and resumed her duties as the warden. She defended the Mordant Crescent Great Hall from raiders who consisted of Sunspears, adventurers and the
dragon
Aurene
. After a fierce fight, Amala was struck down once again although she promised that she would see the mortals again. Keeping her promise, the warden would indeed rise once more as a dark ritual was cast over the great hall which not only resurrected her but also forced the raiders to flee to safety and try their luck again later.

Trivia
Amala means 'hope' in
Arabic
.
"
Amala
", a combat
track
composed by
Brendon Williams
, is named after her.
Amala uses the
Sunscythe
staff.
As High Priestess Amala, she uses the
Scythe Staff
instead.
She wears Mordant Crescent headgear,
Bounty Hunter's Mantle
,
Spearmarshal's Vestments
,
Funerary Gloves
,
Spearmarshal's Pants
, and
Funerary Shoes
.
As High Priestess Amala, she instead wears the
Glorious Hero's Crown
.
Four of Amala's avatars use the respective god's outfits based off of
Dervish
god avatars:
Lyssa's Regalia
,
Dwayna's Regalia Outfit
,
Grenth's Regalia Outfit
, and
Balthazar's Regalia Outfit
.
Her
Avatar of Melandru
form uses a
sylvari
instead.
Spoiler alert:
The hidden text contains spoilers relating to
Path of Fire
.
Amala is able to channel Balthazar, despite the god's death at the hands of the
Pact Commander
, by channeling Palawa Joko's power in a way that resembles how she would channel Balthazar in life.

is a
Sylvari
living in
Trader's Green
and is a member of the
Order of Whispers
.
Location
Maguuma Jungle
Caledon Forest
Trader's Green
Dialogue
Cathal
is a peaceful community, sheltered by the haven nearby. You're welcome to our hospitality.
You're under the wing of the
Lionguard
at the haven?
We have
wardens
here too. The two groups keep
Wychmire Swamp
from swallowing us whole. One of these days, they won't be enough.
Perhaps. Let's hope not.
(if member of the
Order of Whispers
)
What can you tell me about the area?
While keeping an eye on the
Durmand Priory
explorers stationed at
Caledon haven
, I overheard mention of a darkness pervading the Wychmire Swamp.
What flavor of darkness are we talking about?
The wardens are keeping it hush-hush. They don't want to panic the locals or the
Lionguard
. Apparently, there's serious
nightmare
magic
at work in the swamp.
Nightmare magic? What do you mean?
Dig into your memories of your nightmares, and you'll have an inkling. There are those of my kind who prefer nightmare to Dream. They deserve no mercy.
I see.
I understand the seriousness.
I'll keep that in mind.
Notes
Anomaly
:
Her mention of the Durmand Priory at the haven is incorrect, as
asura
researchers are there instead.
Captain Ragna
herself mentions in her dialogue that she would prefer hosting the Priory over the asura.

In
Chokevine Gorge

At
Jinotl Deadgrounds
I hope you've come to support us in our fight. One can never have enough allies.
What brought you here?
Many
valiants
, including myself, were called on a
Wyld Hunt
to push back the
undead
here. We all
dream
of the
Stone of Hazaan
in the nearby ruined
hylek
village. As the undead are called to it, so are we, but to a different purpose.
(If not sylvari)
What's a Wyld Hunt?
A Wyld Hunt is an undeniable calling that drives a sylvari to fulfill a certain purpose. Each Wyld Hunt is different for each person, but many sylvari can find themselves called to the same cause.
Interesting.
I see.
(If in
Order of Whispers
)
We must do our best to shield the unshielded.
And defend the undefended. I thought you looked familiar, but I wasn't sure. I had just joined the Order of Whispers when the Wyld Hunt called me. My superiors bid me best of luck and asked me to send reports from the front lines.
Anything interesting you can tell me?
Not many secrets here. Anything you want to learn about Dryground you can get by asking. Oh, but I do know that there've been teams sent in to explore the depths of Shimmerstone Cave, but no one has come out.
That certainly is interesting. Thanks.
I'll see.

Dialogue
Quiet. My disguise may be convincing, but the
Nightmare Court
is ever vigilant. These are dark times, they're taking villagers from
Astorea
and turning them to their cause.
What can I do to help?
If your courage is the equal of your conviction, I can disguise you as a member of the Nightmare Court. If the guise succeeds, you can enter
Briarthorn
and disrupt their operation.
I'll do whatever needs to be done.
When you are ready, you can don the disguise and infiltrate Briarthorn.
I'm ready.
On second thought, I'd rather not.
I have other things to do.
Why are you disguised?
We know the Nightmare Court is taking people, but we don't know how they're turning them. But with so many undead around, we don't have the numbers for an assault. Hiding and observing is all I can do.
What can I do to help?
(Same as "I'll do whatever needs to be done.")
Hopefully the tide will turn.
Good-bye.
Thank you for infiltrating the Nightmare Court. We've gained useful information and slowed their advances towards
the Grove
.
It was my pleasure. Do you have anything for sale?
I'd like to re-enter Briarthorn.
I'm glad I could help.
Luckily, this disguise fooled the Nightmare Court before. Let's hope we're not pushing our luck.
You really, REALLY need to work on that pep talk.

Location

The Desolation
The Ruination
Event involvement
[Group Event]

(80)
Dialogue
The Ruptured Heart is teeming with Forged. Check back later when we attempt to shut down the portals they've been coming through.
What are you using to shut down the portals?
Portal Spikes! Creator Jadi's invention. They're a type of bomb that will destabilize the realm portals at the Maws. Once in place, demolitionists tune them to sap the portal's stability.
Handy. Thanks for the chat.
I'll do that.
During
Weaken North Maw defenses for the demolitionist assault
We've spotted activity at the Maws, which means more Forged reinforcements from the Real(sic) of Torment. Can you weaken their defenses, so we can make a bombing run on their portals?
What needs to be done?
The Forged gathered around the Maws need to be dealt with. Destroy the cannons along the path, and take out the Forged officers. Trash some barricades if you get a chance.
What's going on, again?
Sounds like a plan.
What are you using to shut down the portals?
Portal Spikes! Creator Jadi's invention. They're a type of bomb that will destabilize the realm portals at the Maws. Once in place, demolitionists tune them to sap the portal's stability.
What needs to be done?
Same as above option.
Handy. Thanks for the chat.
I'll see what I can do.
During
Help destroy the North Realm Portal
The demolitionists are bringing portal spikes to destabilize the realm portals at the Maws. Hurry and you can catch up to them.
How can I help close the portals?
The demolitionists need to make it all the way to the Maws through a bunch of Forged, and then set up the portal spikes. Go with them. Keep them safe.
What's going on, again?
Got it. Will do.
What are you using to shut down the portals?
Portal Spikes! Creator Jadi's invention. They're a type of bomb that will destabilize the realm portals at the Maws. Once in place, demolitionists tune them to sap the portal's stability.
How can I help close the portals?
Same as above option.
Handy. Thanks for the chat.
I'd better get going, then!

I'll do that.

Forged officers, their cannons, and their barricades bar our path. Clear them out, so our demolitionists can get through. The barricades are the lowest priority.

Same as above option.
Thanks for the information.

During

Good to see you! We've got demolitionists running portal spikes to try to close the Forged's Realm Portals at the Maws. They'll need help. Hurry! Run, and catch up to them!
How can I help close the portals?
The demolitionists will need protection on their way to the Maws, and while they're using the portal spikes on the realm portals. It'll take them several minutes to bring the portals down.
What's going on, again?
I should go help!

Creator Jadi invented portal spikes—bombs that will destabilize the realm portals that appear at the Maws. Once in place, demolitionists tune them to sap the portal's stability.

Same as above option.

at
The Ruination
, seeking if any new portal has appeared inside
the Ruptured Heart
.
Location
Crystal Desert
The Desolation
The Ruination
Event involvement
[Group Event]
Help destroy the East Realm Portal
(80)
Dialogue
Forged
are crawling all over the Ruptured Heart. We think they're coming through portals at the Maws. Check back later—we may need your help shutting the portals down.
What are you using to shut down the portals?
Bombs!
Creator Jadi
insists on calling them portal spikes, but they're just specialized bombs, designed to destabilize the Realm Portals. Our demolitionist have to tune them to the portals.
Are you new around here? You look new.
(same as
Are you new here?
)
I'll come back, thanks.
Are you new here?
What gave it away? Is it my tan lines? My
brother
and I just got here not too long ago. We heard rumors the
Order of Shadows
needed a hand, so we brought four.
How did you hear they needed help?
At the
Order of Whispers
, we do sometimes hear from our Shadows friends. Some of the messages suggested they were in trouble, but we had no way to send anyone over until now.
Just the two of you?
Well, our trip wasn't officially sanctioned. We heard there was passage to
Elona
and took off. We left a note, of course. Maybe they'll send more after us. Maybe not. Who knows?
You mentioned portals. What will you use to shut them down?
(same as
What are you using to shut down the portals?
)
Well, any help is welcomed.
Interesting. You should probably get back to work on those spikes.
Funny. See you around.
Funny. See you around.
During
Weaken East Maw defenses for the demolitionist assault
Hey, come help! Forged are sending reinforcements through realm portals at the Maws. We can shut down the portals if we get a clean shot, but Forged defenses are too strong right now.
What needs to be done?
Clear out the Forged, especially the officers. Destroy their cannons...and be careful of their shells! If you want to destroy a few barricades too, we won't stop you.
What's going on, again?
I'll take care of it.
What are you using to shut down the portals?
Bombs! Creator Jadi insists on calling them portal spikes, but they're just specialized bombs, designed to destabilize the Realm Portals. Our demolitionist have to tune them to the portals.
What needs to be done?
(same as above option)
Interesting. Thanks.
I'll go cull some Forged for you.
During
Help destroy the East Realm Portal
We've got demolitionists carrying portal spikes to the Maws to try to shut down the realm portals letting Forged through. Can you go help them?
How can I help close the Realm Portals?
Help the demolitionists reach the Maws. It takes awhile for the portals to connect to the Realm of Torment, so you'll have to guard the demolitionists while they destabilize them.
What's going on, again?
I'm on it.
What are you using to shut down the portals?
Bombs! Creator Jadi insists on calling them portal spikes, but they're just specialized bombs, designed to destabilize the Realm Portals. Our demolitionist have to tune them to the portals.
How can I help close the Realm Portals?
(same as above option)
Interesting. Thanks.
I'll try to catch up to them!

Interactive map
Watcher Mage
is a
Order of Shadows
agent who appears in several
areas
throughout
The Desolation
during the
Help the Shadows handler deliver field orders to the watchers
event.
Locations
Crystal Desert
The Desolation
(only during related events)
Joko's Domain
The Bonestrand
The Scourgeway
Vitriolic Rim
Event involvement
Help the Shadows handler deliver field orders to the watchers
(80)
Dialogue
(If carrying Mage field orders)
Hello. Have you got the field orders I've been waiting for?
I have field orders for you.
(If carrying field orders other than the Mage field orders)
Sorry, those field orders aren't meant for me. You'll have to bring them to the agent they're assigned to.
(Otherwise)
Thanks for delivering field orders, but we're still looking for more. Pick up field orders from the
Shadows handler
. If they're assigned to me, bring them back here.
What's so important about these field orders?
Our agents gather intelligence on the position, movement, and readiness of our enemies. New field orders direct them to new intelligence targets.
And who is your Shadows handler?
Our Shadows handler is our link to the
Chantry of Shadows
. The handler sends me new orders for reconnaissance, which I disseminate to the other agents.
What else can you tell me?
I'll see what I can do.
What else can you tell me?
I'll see what I can do.
Who are the Order of Shadows?
Our organization is charged with keeping the
Realm of Torment
in check. The emergence of the
Forged
has us on our heels. The Shadows hope and plan to free
Elona
someday, but our numbers are few.
So, how do the Shadows intend to free Elona?
I'd think the name would give it away. Subterfuge. Having superior intelligence is key, as is remaining nimble so we can strike when the opportunity arises.
What else can you tell me?
I'll see what I can do.
What else can you tell me?
I'll see what I can do.
I'll see what I can do.

Shadows Handler
Watcher Shaman

Dialogue
We're nearly out of supplies again. If I can't round more up soon, there'll be some rumbling stomachs in my future.
Supply troubles? Maybe I can lend a hand.
You have no idea how much I'd appreciate your help. The supply caravans from Oxbow Isle rarely make it here unscathed, and our provisions have been dwindling for a while now.
What happens to the caravans?
It's different from day to day. The road from oxbow Isle is long. It's usually either aggressive wildlife defending their territory, or Inquest agents harassing us. Either way, we lose supplies.
I'll try to help out if I see them. Anything else?

Could you tell me about what you do here?
Of course! I'm always happy to educate others. Here at Aethervolt, we observe the sources nature draws upon for energy and power, and try to reproduce them through technological means.
